job.title: Speech Language Pathologist
job.description:
Speech and Language Pathologist

$72,000 – 85,000 base on experience

We are seeking a skilled and compassionate Speech and Language Pathologist to support students’ communication, language, and speech development. You will provide assessment, intervention, and guidance to help students achieve their educational goals while collaborating with families, educators, and community professionals.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assess students’ speech, language, voice, and fluency to identify communication disorders and determine eligibility for services.

  • Develop and implement evidence-based interventions, IEPs, and educational plans to support student communication skills.

  • Collaborate with teachers, administrators, support staff, and families to optimize learning outcomes.

  • Administer and interpret formal and informal assessments.

  • Provide guidance on assistive technology and communication tools to support student learning.

  • Maintain accurate documentation, progress reports, and other required records.

  • Train and support staff, students, and families on communication strategies and assistive devices.

  • Participate in professional development to stay current in the field.

Qualifications

  • Master’s degree or higher in Speech-Language Pathology.

  • Eligible for or holds New Hampshire Speech/Language Pathologist licensure.

  • Experience in school or educational settings preferred.

  • Knowledge of speech and language assessment tools and intervention strategies.

  • Strong communication, collaboration, and organizational skills.

  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and maintain confidentiality.

Why Join Us

  • Make a meaningful impact on students’ educational and social success

  • Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team of professionals

 

#toponehire

job.title: Psychologist
job.description:
Psychologist

$62 – 90k Based on experience 

We are seeking a dedicated and skilled School Psychologist to support the academic, social, and emotional development of students. The ideal candidate will assess student needs, provide interventions, and collaborate with families, educators, and community resources to ensure every student has the support they need to succeed.

Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct assessments of students’ cognitive, academic, social, emotional, and behavioral functioning.

  • Develop recommendations and interventions to support student learning and development.

  • Collaborate with teachers, parents, and outside professionals to create individualized plans and behavior strategies.

  • Administer and interpret standardized and supplemental assessments.

  • Provide counseling and guidance to students and families to promote success in school.

  • Prepare clear and comprehensive reports, evaluations, and documentation.

  • Coordinate with community agencies and service providers as needed.

  • Support school-wide programs and interventions, including crisis response and behavioral support.

  • Supervise interns or practicum students as appropriate.

Qualifications

  • Master’s degree in School Psychology or related field (advanced degree preferred).

  • Eligibility for or possession of state School Psychologist certification and Psychologist license.

  • Experience in school or educational psychology preferred.

  • Knowledge of assessment tools, psychological and educational principles, and best practices.

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.

  • Ability to maintain confidentiality, manage multiple priorities, and collaborate effectively.

Work Environment

  • Primarily office/school-based setting

  • Requires a mix of sitting, walking, and interaction with students and staff

Why Join Us

  • Make a meaningful impact on students’ academic and social success

  • Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team of professionals

  • Opportunities for professional growth and development

#toponehire

job.title: Board Certified Behavioral Analyst
job.description:
Board Certified Behavioral Analyst (BCBA)

$62,000 – 85,000 based on experience

We are seeking a skilled and dedicated Behavior Analyst to provide direct services and consultation to students, staff, and families. This role focuses on promoting positive behavior, developing and implementing behavioral assessments and interventions, and collaborating with school staff and outside agencies to support student success.

Key Responsibilities

  • Apply principles of Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) to develop interventions for students PK–12.

  • Conduct and interpret functional behavioral assessments to inform intervention strategies.

  • Develop and implement positive behavioral intervention plans to address student behavior concerns.

  • Collect, analyze, and use data to monitor progress and adjust interventions.

  • Train and support school staff on ABA principles and behavior management techniques.

  • Collaborate with teachers, administrators, and families to support student outcomes.

  • Attend IEP meetings as needed to provide expertise in behavioral interventions.

  • Support students with communication or autism spectrum disorders.

Qualifications

  • Master’s degree in a related field.

  • Board Certified Behavioral Analyst (BCBA).

  • 3–5 years of experience as a Behavior Analyst.

  • Knowledge of ABA, single-subject experimental designs, and behavior intervention strategies.

  • Strong communication, collaboration, and organizational skills.

  • Ability to maintain confidentiality, manage multiple priorities, and work as part of a team.

Why Join Us

  • Make a meaningful impact on student behavior and success.

  • Work collaboratively with a multidisciplinary team.

  • Opportunities for professional growth and development.

#toponehire

job.title: Special Education Administrator
job.description:
Elementary Special Education Administrator

$85-100k based on experience 

Seeking an experienced Special Education Administrator to lead and oversee special education and student support services. This role is responsible for program leadership, staff supervision, compliance, and continuous improvement to ensure high-quality services and positive student outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

  • Direct and coordinate special education and student support programs

  • Supervise and support special education staff

  • Ensure compliance with state and federal special education regulations

  • Collaborate with families, educators, and service providers

  • Oversee IEP development, evaluations, and case management

  • Manage budgets and departmental resources

  • Use data to monitor program effectiveness and drive improvement

Qualifications

  • Master’s degree in Special Education or related field

  • Principal or Special Education Administrator certification (or eligibility)

  • Special education certification or related field

  • Administrative and supervisory experience

  • Classroom teaching experience preferred (special education)

  • Experience with budgeting, data analysis, and change management

#toponehire

job.title: Student Services Director
job.description:
Director of Student Services

$90-135k based on experience
Full time M-F 
Benefits: 24 vacation days, 12 sick days, 12 holidays, 2 personal days. Health insurance + pension 

Overview

The Director of Student Services is a senior leadership role responsible for overseeing special education and related student support services. This position provides strategic, operational, and compliance leadership to ensure students receive high-quality academic, behavioral, and health-related support.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead and oversee student services, including special education, related services, Section 504 accommodations, nursing/health services, ELL, homeless services, tutoring, and transition support

  • Ensure compliance with all applicable federal and state regulations

  • Develop, implement, and evaluate student services programs

  • Manage departmental budgets and oversee grant writing and administration

  • Recruit, supervise, and evaluate student services staff and service providers

  • Coordinate services with external providers and community agencies

  • Support crisis response planning and complex student situations

  • Partner with leadership to align student services with organizational goals

Qualifications

  • Master’s degree in Special Education, Psychology, Special Education Administration, or related field

  • Certification or eligibility for certification as a Special Education Administrator (or equivalent)

  • 5+ years of experience in special education or student services

  • 2+ years of leadership or supervisory experience

  • Strong knowledge of special education law, ADA, and Section 504

  • Excellent leadership, communication, and organizational skills

#toponehire

job.title: Detox Nurse
job.description:
Substance abuse clinic hiring for detox nurse (LPN or RN)
3x12s 7am-7pm w/ rotating 11pm-7am shift as needed. Weekend rotation every other Sat & Sun 7am-7pm
Contract to hire

Responsibilities:

  • Conducts intake assessments through interviews with patients and relatives to gather case history.
  • Plans and provides nursing care for patients.
  • Makes nursing diagnosis of the medical status of patients and recommends treatment options.
  • Consults with medical and other behavioral healthcare staff in designing appropriate treatment plans for clients.
  • Administers and notes reactions to psychotropic drugs and other medications.
  • Arranges further medical attention when necessary.
  • Assists the client in understanding the nature of the disease of addiction and the need for treatment.
  • Assists in maintaining a safe and secure environment for staff and patients
  • Tests for vital signs of pain and ensures treatment of pain.

Requirements:

  • MA RN or LPN Licensure
  • At least 1 year of recent detox experience

job.title: Leasing Assistant (Affordable Housing)
job.description:
Temp to Hire
Schedule: Monday – Friday, 8:30-5pm 
Pay: $23/hr 

MUST Have Affordable housing experience and recertification knowledge 

Responsibilities :

  • Demonstrate community and apartment/model and apply product knowledge to clients’ needs by communicating the features and benefits; close the sale.
  • Have prospect complete application and secure deposit in accordance with company procedures and Fair Housing requirements.
  • Update availability report and process applications for approvals.  (i.e., credit check, rental history, income information, etc)  Submit processed applications to the Property Manager for review.  Follow up with the applicant regarding status. 
  • Maintain at least four (4) pre-approved applications for all apartment sizes on an ongoing basis. 
  • Ensure the apartment is ready for the resident to move-in on the agreed date.
  • Secure new resident’s signature(s) on appropriate paperwork prior to move-in.  Orient new residents to the community.
  • Assist in monitoring renewals.  Distribute and follow-up on renewal notices.
  • Maintain high occupancy levels to reduce vacancy loss.  The company’s goal is to maintain a minimum of 98% occupancy annually.
  • Monitor advertising effectiveness.  Gather information about market competition in the area
  • Represent the company in a professional manner at all times.
  • Maintain and record daily inspections for the community.
  • Distribute company and community-issued notices.
  • Assist the management team with other various tasks as required.
  • Consistently implement policies of the community.
  • Inventory office supplies on a periodic basis.  Report any needed items to the Property Manager.
  • Comply with federal, state, and company policies, procedures, and regulations.
  • Communicate with the property manager regarding the accuracy of invoices received; that purchase orders are attached and invoices are coded correctly, according to company procedures.
  • Provide general clerical assistance to the community office.
  • Comply with federal, state, and company policies, procedures, and regulations.
  • Receive telephone calls and in-person visits.  Listen to resident requests, concerns, and comments.
  • Quickly complete maintenance service requests and inform the maintenance team.  Answer questions for residents about community, repairs, rent, rules, etc.  Follow up on a timely basis if unable to respond to residents on all matters.
  • Ensure all maintenance repairs are handled satisfactorily by contacting residents with completed service requests on a weekly basis.
  • Maintain open communication with Property Manager and Maintenance Supervisor.
  • Contribute to cleanliness and curb appeal of the community on a continuing basis.
  • Assist in planning resident functions.  Attend functions and participate as host for any functions as directed by the Property Manager.
  • Participate in outreach marketing activities on a regular basis to obtain prospective residents.
  • Advise residents of referral concessions (if permitted).
  • Assist in placing, removing/updating banners, balloons, bandit signs, flags, etc.
  • Distribute newsletters, pamphlets, flyers, etc.
  • Conduct market surveys and shop competitive communities.
  • Keep advertising methods current and up-to-date. (i.e. Craigslist, Apartment Guide, Apartments.com, Newspaper Ads, etc)

Qualifications: 

  • 1-2 years of affordable housing experience 
  • Demonstrate an ability to support and contribute to the community team.
  • Demonstrate strong oral and written communication skills.
  • Operate telephone, copy machine, facsimile, personal computer, Google Docs, and community software.
  • Must possess a positive attitude and the ability to smile under all circumstances.
  • Participate in training in order to comply with new or existing laws.
  • Ability to work a flexible schedule, including evenings & weekends, if requested.

job.title: Assistant Property Manager
job.description:
We are looking for a highly organized and proactive Property Manager or Assistant Property Manager to oversee daily operations at a luxury 100+ unit community.  This position is to cover a Maternity Leave and is starting early/mid-May.

This role focuses on administrative coordination and leasing support, making it ideal for someone who excels in a fast-paced environment, delivers excellent customer service, and maintains strong attention to detail.

Pay is $25/hour with a schedule of 8:30am – 5pm Mon-Fri
Leasing bonuses available!

Open to a seasoned Manager with a Market Rate/Class A background or a strong Assistant Manager who is looking for an opportunity to take the next step in their career.

Key Responsibilities:
Administrative Functions:
Maintain accurate and up-to-date tenant records, lease files, and property documentation
Process rental applications, conduct background screenings, and prepare lease agreements
Manage incoming calls, emails, and inquiries from residents and prospective tenants
Coordinate maintenance requests and ensure timely follow-up and resolution
Assist with rent collection, tracking, and financial reporting
Prepare and maintain reports on occupancy, leasing activity, and overall property performance
Ensure compliance with company policies and local housing regulations

Leasing & Resident Relations:
Promote available units through online listings, social media, and other marketing channels
Conduct property tours and respond promptly to leasing inquiries
Screen prospective tenants and manage the full application process
Negotiate lease terms and execute lease agreements
Monitor market conditions to maintain competitive pricing and occupancy levels
Foster a positive experience for both prospective and current residents

Qualifications:
Prior experience in property management, leasing, or administrative support preferred
Strong organizational skills with the ability to multitask effectively
Excellent communication and customer service abilities
Proficiency with AppFolio property management software
Ability to work independently and meet deadlines
Working knowledge of fair housing laws and leasing practices

Additional Requirements:
Availability for the full duration of the temporary assignment
Flexibility to work occasional evenings or weekends as needed

job.title: Loan Servicing Supervisor
job.description:
Loan Servicing Supervisor
Boston, MA

OVERVIEW
Oversee and optimize all facets of loan servicing operations. Provide strong leadership for the loan servicing team, and ensure compliance with regulations, all while fostering member engagement. Responsible for developing and maintaining a deep understanding of the credit union’s loan products, services, and staying current with industry trends, regulations, and best practices in loan servicing.

RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Provide comprehensive oversight of loan servicing operations, including consumer and real estate Loans.
  • Ensure efficient management of all loans within the credit union, both current and delinquent.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to streamline loan servicing, enhancing the overall member experience.
  • Provide training and development opportunities for all employees that service members with loans.
  • Ensure compliance with all loan servicing related laws and regulations, including Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) guidelines.
  • Provide strong leadership to the loan servicing team, setting clear goals and objectives to drive departmental success. Conduct performance reviews and allocate resources to meet operational needs within the department.
  • Communicate with members, understanding their financial situations. Ensure a high level of service and support for members across all loan servicing activities.
  • Manage relationships with repossession companies, external collection agencies, legal counsel, and other service providers to support collections and loan servicing efforts.
  • Ensure Credit Union policies and procedures are being followed in compliance with applicable laws and regulations.
  • Strategic Leadership: Demonstrate strong leadership skills to effectively guide the loan servicing team, setting clear goals and fostering a culture of continuous improvement.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Ensure strict compliance with all relevant laws, regulations, and credit union policies, particularly those related to loan servicing.
  • Member-Centric Approach: Maintain a member-focused mindset, engaging with members to understand their unique financial situations and working collaboratively to find solutions for debt resolution.
  • Industry Knowledge: Stay abreast of industry trends, regulations, and best practices in loan servicing, incorporating relevant insights into credit union operations to drive continuous improvement.
  • Effective Communication: Exhibit excellent communication skills, both within the team and when engaging with members, external partners, and legal professionals.
  • Team Development: Invest in the training and development of the loan servicing team, enhancing their skills and knowledge to drive departmental success.
  • Adaptability: Be adaptable in a dynamic financial services environment, proactively responding to changes and challenges in the industry to maintain operational excellence.
  • Supervising direct reports

Requirements:

  • Five years of similar or related experience in managing servicing employees.
  • Must be knowledgeable of laws and regulations applicable to lending, and loan service. COCC, Velara, and/or Encompass experience a plus.
  • A four-year college degree is desired but may be substituted with relative experience in lending.
  • Must have strong managerial skills and possess excellent communication skills, both verbal and written.
  • Position involves ability to organize and delegate work assignments, motivate others to achieve desired outcomes and promote a team environment.
  • A significant level of trust, diplomacy and professionalism is required, and possess a cooperative attitude in relations with members, vendors and other employees
  • Must be self-motivated, able to prioritize workflow, handle multiple assignments concurrently, work a flexible schedule, and perform under time constraints.
  • Must have working knowledge of PC and general office equipment, with experience using Microsoft Outlook, Word and Excel.
  • Attention to detail and accuracy is essential.
  • Must be bondable

job.title: Production Manager
job.description:
.Position Summary:

Essential Job Functions:
Production & Operations
• Lead and manage all production activities ensuring consistent performance and handover between crews.
• Achieve production targets related to output, efficiency, scrap, and on-time delivery.
• Optimize press utilization, cycle times, and labor allocation for high-volume tote production.
• Coordinate production scheduling and sequencing to meet customer demand while minimizing WIP and downtime.
• Support mold changes and setups, ensuring adherence to standardized processes and SMED principles.
Safety & Compliance
• Champion a strong safety culture across all shifts, ensuring compliance with PPE, LOTO, and safety procedures.
• Ensure product quality meets internal standards and customer expectations for storage tote products.
• Partner with Quality to maintain process control, reduce defects, and investigate non-conformances.
Lean Manufacturing & Continuous Improvement
• Lead and facilitate Kaizen events focused on safety, productivity, quality, and cost reduction.
• Support Lean Manufacturing tools implementation including SMED, 5S, Standard Work, Visual Management, and 3M analysis (Muda, Mura, Muri).
• Analyze Uptime, Efficiency, OEE, downtime, scrap, and changeover data to identify improvement opportunities.
• Coach supervisors and team leads on problem-solving methods such as root cause analysis, A3, and 8D.
Leadership & Workforce Development
• Directly lead production supervisors and indirectly lead operators, setup technicians, and material handlers.
• Build strong engagement and accountability across a diverse workforce operating 24/7.
• Conduct performance reviews, coaching, and development planning.
• Ensure effective communication and shift-to-shift handovers.
Cross-Functional Collaboration
• Work closely with Maintenance, Process, Tooling to support preventive maintenance and mold readiness.
• Partner with Engineering on process improvements, new mold launches, and equipment upgrades.
• Collaborate with Supply Chain on material availability, inventory control, and cost reduction initiatives.
Qualifications:
Experience & Technical Skills

  • 5+ years of experience in plastic injection molding manufacturing.
    Minimum 2 years in a production leadership role 
  • Proven experience in a Plant Manager, Production Manager, or similar senior manufacturing leadership role.
  • Proficiency with computer skills (Excel, Word, Powerpoint, Outlook, TEAMS) and manufacturing systems such as ERP/MRP/MES systems such as Netsuite, SAP, BEAS/BEAR, or comparable production management tools.
  • Fluent in English and proficient in Spanish (spoken and written).
  • Strong knowledge of high-volume injection molding operations and automation.
  • Demonstrated experience leading Kaizen events and implementing Lean Manufacturing tools.

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Operations Management, or related field preferred, or equivalent experience.

Language

  • Bilingual English/Spanish required

job.title: Medical Billing Specialist
job.description:
Hiring for a Medical Billing Specialist in San Antonio, TX. This will be fully on-site!

Pay rate: $20-25/hr (based on experience)
Schedule (full time option): Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30
Schedule (part-time options): 16-24 Hours a week 3-5 days a week or Monday-Friday 4-5 hours a day (i.e 12:00pm-5:00pm)

Responsibilities: 

  •  Review clinical documentation to ensure the appropriate coding (CPT codes, diagnosis codes, modifiers, etc)
  • Ensure all codes meet federal, state, and insurance-specific guidelines
  •  Communicate with payers via portals or phone to resolve outstanding payment issues
  •  Post insurance and patient payments accurately to the practice management system
  •  Maintain strict adherence to HIPAA regulations regarding patient data privacy
  • Audit medical records regularly to ensure proper coding & documentation supports the service billed

Must Haves:

  • Experience: Minimum 1 year of experience in outpatient billing, Vascular is a huge plus
  • Certification: CPC (Certified Professional Coder) or CCS (Certified Coding Specialist) preferred
  • Knowledge: Deep understanding of EOBs, RCM systems, and medical terminology specific to billing/coding
  • Software: Proficiency preferred with EHR systems, Availity, major insurance portals
  • Analytical Thinker with the ability to spot patterns in denials to suggest process improvements with payer specific
    coding
  • Persistence with a “detective” mindset to track down why a claim was rejected, find a resolution, and suggest an
    action plan to avoid future errors
  • Attention to Detail for high accuracy with little to no error in data entry
  • LCD Review/Monitoring to proactively check the Medicare and Commercial Payer Coverages for new and/or
    revised policies to ensure compliance

#toponehire

job.title: OBGYN RN $55-60/hr
job.description:
Hiring for an OBGYN RN in Rye Brook, NY. 

Must be okay with floating to New Rochelle, NY as needed. 

Pay: $55-60/hr (based on experience!)
Schedule: Mon – Thurs (8a – 5p) and Fri (8a – 3p)

Open positions: Nurse *Certified Electronic Fetal Monitoring (C-EFM) preferred to perform NST* However, we will consider candidates with strong maternal/fetal/labor delivery experience.

Job Description

The RN provides care that: * Establishes and maintains a therapeutic relationship with the patient and family encompasses an understanding and integration of cultural and diversity into practice * Includes communicating and working collaboratively with the patient, family and health care team members * Includes respecting the patient’s values, preferences, expressed needs and knowledge of the healthcare situation in holistic data collection, in formulating health care outcomes and in the evaluation process.

Qualifications

Active RN licensure in NY 
Active BLS/CPR/ACLS
MUST have at least 1 year of recent experience in either L&D, Mother Baby, Postpartum or OBGYN

job.title: Pediatric Nurse Program Coordinator
job.description:
Hiring for an RN – Pediatric Program Coordinator – in Methuen, MA

Schedule: 40HPW; Monday through Friday day shift
Pay: $40-43/hr based on experience 

Position Summary:
The Pediatric RN Program Coordinator (Coordinator) works under the supervision of the Director of Population Health and in collaboration with the AVP of Quality and Clinical Integration to provide day-to-day program management oversight of the DPH and TMPEC pediatric program. The Coordinator provides administrative and operational support to implement and sustain early childhood integrated care transformation activities aligned with the TMPEC (Transforming Massachusetts Pediatrics for Early Childhood) grant. In this role, the Coordinator serves as the primary liaison between the primary care clinicians, pediatricians, and developmental specialists to ensure that children in need of early intervention programs and other supportive social services can access these services timely. The coordinator works with the Pediatric Community Health Worker and Family Partner to ensure families and caregivers are provided with resources, guidance, and support, helping them navigate their child’s journey. The Coordinator is responsible for ensuring program metrics are managed, and performance meets established targets. In addition, they are responsible for a combination of administrative, educational, and clinical support functions.

Job Responsibilities and Performance Standards:

  • Assists with documenting progress reports, including maintaining documentation for TMPEC grant reporting, including core metrics, qualitative updates, and budget-related forms.
  • Oversees the coordination of patient care, including but not limited to facilitating communication between different healthcare professionals and ensuring compliance with regulations and policies.
  • Overseeing program operations, including ensuring standard and efficient workflows and identifying areas for process improvement.
  • Collecting, analyzing, and reporting data related to program performance and outcomes.
  • Collaborates with the clinical educator to design population-based clinical competencies for nursing and MA staff.
  • Collaborates with clinical leadership to ensure the program meets accreditation standards and other regulatory requirements.
  • Serves as a primary point of contact and liaison between the TMPEC project team and stakeholders to coordinate educational sessions with TMPEC Learning Collaborative events.
  • Provides administrative support, including scheduling and managing project team meetings, prepares agendas, materials, and minutes for meetings related to early childhood transformation activities.
  • Tracks project deliverables and assists in maintaining a shared calendar and reporting deadlines for grant-related activities.
  • Independently composes and edits correspondence, reports, summaries, and presentations for review by the project lead.
  • Compiles, analyzes, and summarizes early childhood program and QI data from internal and external sources for reporting and planning.
  • Supervisory responsibilities for Family Partner and Pediatric CHW. Supports recruitment, onboarding, and scheduling for the team member with lived experience (e.g., Family Partner) and helps manage workflows integrating this role into the care team.
  • Supports the interdisciplinary care team’s efforts to engage families in QI activities, family advisory committee recruitment, and feedback processes.
  • Collaborates with the Director of Population Health and the AVP of Quality and Clinical Integration to supports ongoing quality improvement and capacity building efforts aligned with the TMPEC learning collaborative.
  • Performs all additional duties as assigned.

Qualifications and Experience:

  • Registered nurse with pediatrics experience, including 3-5 years of administrative program management experience in healthcare.
  • Strong ability to manage stakeholder relationships and collaborate effectively with team members and families.
  • Experience working with patients with chronic medical and behavioral health needs.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office and project management tools (e.g., Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and shared drives).
  • Strong interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills.
  • Experience coordinating multi-stakeholder projects and supporting QI or grant activities preferred.
  • Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple timelines and priorities.
  • Must demonstrate the basics of cultural competence when dealing with a diverse population.

Physical Environment

  • Potential for possible exposure to hazardous material as defined by OSHA, CDC, other regulators requiring strict adherence to universal precaution protocols.

Physical Effort

  • Frequently sits, stands, walks, bends, reaches, and stoops throughout the workday.
  • Frequently lifts, pull, push, and carry up to 20 lbs.
  • Periodic eye strain

Hiring for a Bilingual Spanish Medical Assistant in Haverhill, MA

*Bilingual in Spanish/English is required*

Pay: $24-26/hour based on experience
Schedule: Monday through Friday 9am-5:30pm & Tuesdays until 6:30pm

*Candidates must have a diploma/ Certificate from an MA program and a valid CPR/BLS to be considered*

Currently seeking a bilingual (English/Spanish) Medical Assistant. Medical Assistants are responsible for interviewing patients, recording medical history, confirming purpose of visit and assisting the clinician with patient care.
Prepares patients for visit by taking blood pressure, weight and temperature; and reports pertinent information in medical records.

  • Must be able to don appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE).
  • Performs electrocardiography (EKG)
  • Performs ear irrigations
  • Performs throat cultures
  • Collects urinalysis
  • Conducts flu testing
  • Conducts glucose testing
  • Conducts international normalized ratio (INR) testing
  • Ensures that results of all tests and procedures performed are available in medical records.
  • Determines equipment needs visit and sets up exam rooms accordingly.
  • Assists clinicians with treatments and dressings.
  • Translates for clinicians.

Requirements
Qualifications:

  • Bilingual, English and Spanish required
  • At least 1 year of recent MA experience
  • Certified in CPR
  • Graduate of a medical assistant program

job.title: Collections Specialist ($30/hr)
job.description:
Location: Boston, MA (Fully Onsite)
Pay: $26–$30/hr
Schedule: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM

About the Role
We are seeking a Collections Specialist for a reputable financial institution. This role will focus on managing delinquent loan accounts, contacting customers, and helping establish repayment solutions.
This is a fully onsite role in Boston, easily accessible via public transportation.

Key Responsibilities

  • Contact customers regarding delinquent loan and credit card accounts (outbound collections)
  • Monitor and manage delinquent mortgage, equity, and consumer loans
  • Establish repayment plans, settlements, and loan workout solutions
  • Handle customer inquiries professionally and resolve issues efficiently
  • Identify financial hardship cases and gather required documentation
  • Assist with loan modifications, loss mitigation, and foreclosure preparation
  • Ensure compliance with all credit union policies and state/federal regulations
  • Maintain accurate records and support reporting and administrative tasks

Qualifications

  • 3+ years of collections experience
  • Banking/financial services collections experience highly preferred
  • Open to candidates with strong outbound consumer collections experience from other industries
  • Strong communication and negotiation skills
  • Detail-oriented with the ability to manage multiple accounts
  • Knowledge of loan servicing, repayment plans, or loss mitigation a plus

8am – 5pm Mon-Fri

$22-23/hour

We are in need of a shipping clerk. Qualifications are:

We are interviewing and hiring ASAP!

job.title: Inside Sales Representative
job.description:
Keys

  • 2+ years of Clinical Implementation experience in Med Device or Healthcare
  • Proficient in the Microsoft Office Suite for reporting purposes
  • Excellent communication skills

The Clinical Practice Consultant is a strategic and hands-on role responsible for driving customer satisfaction and product utility through the development, delivery, and continuous improvement of clinical training programs. This position contributed to the Center of Excellence and delivery of customer-centric training content.  Ensures its effectiveness by incorporating feedback and aligning with evolving clinical protocols as set forth by the Global Clinical Practice Manager.

Manage a portfolio of customer accounts and work closely with the Clinical Practice Consultants on the team to ensure the delivery of exceptional onboarding, training, and ongoing clinical support.
Plays a key role in enhancing customer lifetime value and satisfaction through impactful clinical engagement, training initiatives, and data-driven decision making.

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide responsive and knowledgeable clinical focused support to physicians and healthcare staff through Hubspot Service including managing incoming tickets, emails, and calls.
  • Manage a defined group of customer accounts, acting as a trusted clinical advisor to ensure successful onboarding, adoption, and long-term satisfaction.
  • Build strong customer relationships through regular touchpoints, clinical support, and training engagement.
  • Conduct Onboarding/Training for customers, ensuring a successful initial implementation utilizing pre-determined usage success metrics
  • Enter and maintain accurate customer feedback and documentation in the company’s CRM, HubSpot to help drive improvements 
  • Contribute to process improvements in customer support operations to enhance efficiency and satisfaction
  • Identify and act on opportunities to optimize product usage and solve adoption challenges in partnership with commercial teams.
  • Assist with troubleshooting customer utilization issues when appropriate and liaise with other team members to deliver exception support.
  • Collaborate interdepartmentally with other teams to align priorities and share insights.
  • Provide comprehensive reporting to VP Operations and VP Sales while offering solutions to emerging issues.
  • Support other areas of the business as required.
  • Serve as the voice of the customer internally, helping shape future enhancements and innovations.

job.title: Condo Portfolio Manager
job.description:
We have partnered with an Condo Management company is looking for a seasoned Manager to oversee a portfolio spanning Middlesex  and New Haven CT counties, the Manager will work directly with the HOA/Condo boards and handle all tenant relations and financials.

CMCA & CAM certifications are HIGHLY PREFERRED 
PREVIOUS CONDO/HOA EXPERIENCE IS NECESSARY IN THIS ROLE

Salary range of $65K-75K

The ideal individual has excellent customer service skills, is a self-starter and highly motivated, and enjoys working in a fast-paced environment. Prior experience in the property management field or experience on a property Board of Directors is a plus.

Under limited supervision, the property manager is the primary contact to oversee and coordinate all property management, maintenance, and limited construction activities associated with small to midsized, multiple-building/unit condominium, co-op and town house buildings.

The property manager is responsible for supervising the properties to ensure that they are promptly and efficiently managed with respect to maintenance, accounting and owner satisfaction. 

DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 

  • Oversee the management of buildings, including maintenance services and outside contractors/vendors
  • Direct contact for any/all resident concerns/requests pertaining to any/all building related issues
  • Contact and place service orders with maintenance staff or specialty vendors/contractors 
  • Assist with creating and maintaining appropriate budgets for each of the condo associations and oversee accounting and financial statements.
  • Monitor contracts for compliance and control costs
  • Coordinate with account manager to ensure administrative document conformity to include insurance compliance and expiration(s) for all buildings, assessment collections and follow-up with assessment delinquencies ensuring that proper notices have been distributed
  • Solicit bids for maintenance contractors and construction projects, create scope spreadsheets, and participate in the selection of contractors 
  • Perform quality assurance and control inspections for each of the buildings to ensure proper maintenance, adherence to contract specifications, and compliance with industry standards 
  • Maintain a thorough knowledge of all property management documents (Bylaws and Declarations, Rules and Regulations, certificates of insurance, vendor contracts) and operate the properties in accordance with these documents. 
  • Perform miscellaneous job-related duties as assigned that include assistance with general property management issues and policies including small administrative projects and tasks as assigned.
  • Answer and appropriately direct concerned owner phone calls and inform residents of policies/procedures.
  • Handle emergency issues afterhours associated with properties or as needed.   

 

REQUIREMENTS:

  • Must have a minimum of 2 year experience with condominium property management and have worked directly with Board of Directors.  Must have CAM license
  • Excellent customer service, open and helpful communication, and exceptional organizational skills
  • Excellent verbal and written communication and customer service skills.  
  • Be able to create budgets, track financials, and communicate updates.
  • Effective leadership, mentoring and coaching skills.
  • Valid driver’s license and reliable transportation
  • Ability to work independently, self-manage, and motivate.
  • This is a full time position.  Salary is based on experienced  

 

QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Education:  Bachelor Degree and Community Association Management (CAM) certification or higher in property management. 
  • 2 year minimum in condominium property management with management, customer service, supervisory experience, administrative, and accounting experience. 
  • Engineering, architectural, construction or similar experience a plus. 
  • Proficient in all MS Office applications.  

#toponehire

job.description:

Imaging Retrieval & Records Support Duties:

  • Ensure patient comfort and safety during imaging procedures.

  • Current ARRT certification required.
  • Active MD state radiologic technologist license (if applicable).
  • Experience in orthopedic imaging preferred

job.title: Senior Financial Analsyt
job.description:
Responsibilities

  • As a member of the Commercial Financial Planning, and Analysis team, act as key contributor to provide analytical support for ongoing financial planning and consolidated reporting for the Commercial product portfolio, with a strong focus on the systems & processes that enable consistent reporting across brands
  • Work closely with Accounting on the month end and quarter end close processes, including expense accruals and reconciliations..
  • Support preparation of the monthly and quarterly financial reporting package – by Product P&L – including the appropriate level of detail on variance analysis and any implications to the latest forecast
  • Complete ad hoc analysis and decision support related to the Commercial organization as required

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree required, preferably in Finance, Accounting, Economics or Mathematics with 3-5 years of progressively responsible experience in a fast paced/high growth environment; MBA or Professional accounting qualification(CPA or equivalent) a plus
  • Bio-tech/Pharmaceutical experience with prior experience supporting a P&L is preferred
  • Strong planning and analytical skills, including superior financial modelling, Excel, and financial database systems skills / knowledge (Hyperion Planning, or similar). Experience with Anaplan is a plus
  • Advanced analytical skills to manipulate large data sets, automate repetitive tasks, and present data clearly and concisely
  • Excellent written and oral communication skills including preparation of clear and impactful PowerPoint presentations.
  • Robust knowledge of relevant accounting practices
  • Strong interpersonal and team building skills; ability to work with a diverse team and influence/drive change across functional and business boundaries

Professional Characteristics

  • Proven presentation, communication, verbal and written skills, with a clear ability to communicate amongst various levels of the organization; fluency in English both written and oral required
  • Must have strong analytical and organizational skills with a great deal of attention to detail
  • Responsible and extremely resourceful in finding answers and solutions when information and data is not readily available
  • Ability to adapt quickly to changing priorities in a fast-paced environment
  • Ability to work well independently as well as be a strong Team player, partner to the Business and to other support functions
  • A “people person” capable of establishing good personal relationships with the ability to work well at all levels

job.title: Registered Nurse
job.description:
Registered Nurse 

Type: direct hire

Schedule:
Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday 8am-5pm – 32 HPW
Monday-Friday and every other Saturday 8am-5pm (possible evening clinic hours on Thursday) – 40 HPW

Responsibilities:

  • Assess patient’s need for treatment or advice via telephone call or personal encounter (walk-in).
  • Consults with provider whenever necessary to advise patient of most appropriate action; reinforce provider plan of care.
  • Determine, based on patient need, if patient needs to be seen by a provider and how quickly. Promote continuity of care within limits of staffing availability.   
  • Schedule urgent appointments appropriately.
  • Respond to patient calls within a timely manner. Document all calls/visits; maintain accurate notes on telephone message and attach to progress note in patient’s chart.
  • All documentation in a patient’s medical record must be complete and easily understood by anyone who may need to access the record.
  • Prepare prescription refills (via pharmacy line; patient calls/visits) and necessary vouchers, when indicated. Chart all refills and updates as needed.
  • Continually update personal, clinical and professional knowledge and skills through formal and informal educational experience. Comply with state continuing education laws.
  • Maintain Nurse Visit schedule: responsible for attending to scheduled nurse visits and documenting care in Logician. Visit reasons may include Depo-Provera, B-12 or other injections, Medication Review, PT/INR dose changes, on-site assessment of acutely ill patients, weights for babies with failure to thrive, repeat BP checks, immunizations, or other reasons.
  • Assist with non-scheduled nursing needs during/throughout the shift. These requests vary from assistance with ECG’s, meeting with patients who have walked in for a free pregnancy test, starting a patient on an NST machine, stabilizing an acutely ill patient until the ambulance arrives, administering a nebulizer treatment, starting an IV (if trained to do so)
  • Assist with the processing of patient paperwork. The nurse should prepare as many components as possible before handing off to the provider. Samples of patient paperwork are disability forms, handicap sticker requests, authorizations for wheelchairs and other assistive devices, letters to return to work, PE and Immune report requests. This list is not all inclusive.

Requirements:

  • Education/Training: RN or LPN with 3 years experience.  
  • Current NH RN or LPN license.
  • Experience: Community health experience preferred
  • American Heart Association (AHA) BLS Certification, required

job.title: Registered Nurse
job.description:
Registered Nurse 

Type: direct hire 

Specialty: inpatient/residential – residents are ages 12-17 (males and females) –  Residents live and go to school on campus. 

Schedule: Monday–Friday, 3:00 PM– 11:00PM, with alternating weekends (two weekdays off when working a weekend)

Requirements: 

  • Current active license in Virginia as a Registered Nurse.
  • CPR, First Aid, and BLS.
  • Experience in adolescent, psychiatric, and/or case management nursing required.
  • Bi-lingual (Spanish & English) strongly preferred

Responsibilities:

  • Performs clinical assessments for the identification, evaluation, coordination and management of member’s needs, including physical and behavioral health, social services and long-term services
  • Performs assigned waived testing in the laboratory to provide data for the diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of diseases. Receives specimens and stocks supplies when received in the department. Troubleshoots/resolves instrument problems and document corrective actions in appropriate log and when necessary. Assures that quality control is properly performed and logged and follow proper department procedure for corrective action. Demonstrates the knowledge and skills to train new employees.
  • Draws blood and administers injections as needed
  • Conducts new admission assessments and completes new admission tasks within time frame specified by licensing regulations.
  • Assesses and provides first aid/appropriate follow up for acute medical issues of residents
  • Maintains and manages quality care of residents. This includes working across the care team (nursing, case management, therapists, residential staff, etc) to ensure residents’ health and wellness needs are met in accordance with licensing requirements.
  • Maintains working knowledge of preventative care and acute care issues that affect residents in order to develop treatment care plans and perform education to assist residents in health goals
  • Makes appointments, maintains and edits shared calendar, processes paperwork and ensures necessary follow ups are complete, excellent and thorough documentation, performs and responds to audits to ensure health needs are being met in accordance with licensing regulations, effectively communicates with all relevant members of the care team (including resident, guardians, placement agencies, YFT treatment team members, and outside medical offices and entities, and other tasks as assigned
  • Completes and obtains necessary paperwork on each resident, schedules appointments that are needed within the set licensing parameters, reviews and enters any new or returning paperwork of all residents
  • Implements treatment care plans by facilitating authorizations/referrals within benefits structure or extra-contractual arrangements, as permissible
  • Manages illnesses and/or disabilities ensuring cost effective and efficient utilization of health benefits; conducts gap in care management for quality programs
  • Manages and keeps all active prescriptions, ensures resident’s medications are available as appropriate, ensures medication orders are active and accurate in eMAR system, administers medication according to MATY protocol and YFT policy, completes medication administration documentation, monitors for side effects, performs medication audits, educates and supervises med techs and other medication management duties as assigned

Benefits: medical, dental, vision, group life insurance, and up to a 5% retirement match. We also offer supplemental insurances such as life (no charge), short-term disability, critical illness, accident insurance and legal resources. We have 9 paid holidays and personal time off accruals. Paid vacation days: Begins accruing on day one of first full paid pay period up to @3.69 hours per pay period/up to 12 days per year) and 2 personal days/year (after one year of employment).

job.title: Ophthalmic Technician
job.description:
Ophthalmic Technician 

Type: temp for approximately 6 months, potential to go permanent 

Schedule: Monday – Friday,  9am-5:30pm

Requirements: 

  • COA certification is required to go perm
  • Must have experience in an Ophthalmology office
  • Must be familiar with imaging

Responsibilities: 

  • Independently performs specialized testing procedures appropriate to specialty. Perform diagnostic clinical photography and digital imaging to support physician to arrive at a diagnosis and to prescribe treatment.
  • Performs preliminary screening examination for physician review. Reviews patient’s chart, patient’s condition, and physician’s notes to plan and determine method of testing. Positions patients.
  • Sets up, operates, and maintains equipment to conduct tests and procedures appropriate to specialty. Assists patients in transfers to/from wheel chair, stretcher, exam tables, etc.
  • Provides information to patients concerning specific tests and procedures. Reads and interprets obtained images. Records test results. Identifies and reports abnormalities.
  • Instructs and acts as a source of information on testing techniques, procedures, and equipment operation.
  • Schedules patient appointments and originates billing paperwork. Reviews and prepares financial paperwork related to clinical activities.
  • Analyzes and compiles patient information.
  • Creates visual aids for lecture presentations and publications.

job.title: Registered Nurse
job.description:
Registered Nurse 

Type: Temporary assignment until August 2026 

Schedule: Monday-Friday, 8:30am/9am-5pm 

Notes:

  • Patient population: adults, children – also provide OBGYN services for patients 
  • Must be empathetic – serve low income/underserved individuals (must be familiar/interested in working with this patient population)  
  • Usually no more than 6-7 providers in the office at a time 
  • Max of 20 patients/provider – average of 15 or so

Requirements: 

  • Certified as a registered nurse and graduate of an accredited college required.
  • Active DC RN License 
  • BLS certification 
  • At least 1 year of experience in a medical office practice or hospital preferred
  • Ability and flexibility to travel and rotate to multiple clinical site
  • Knowledge with previous EMR programs

Responsibilities: 

  • Prepares patients for examination and treatment including, but not limited to, taking the patient’s history, obtaining accurate vital signs, and verifying the chief complaint as needed.
  • Instructs patients in basic testing requirements (i.e. Pulmonary Function Tests, clean catch urines, and finger sticks) as needed.
  • Supplies the provider with necessary forms, information, and educational tools to accomplish visit when needed.
  • Prepares exam and treatment room with necessary instruments according to policy and/or procedure manual as instructed.
  • Performs all in-house labs and procedures as indicated by the clinical protocol.
  • Cleans up exam and treatment rooms after patient care.
  • Sets up for sterile procedures when needed.
  • Maintains and restocks exam rooms when needed.
  • Observes, records, and reports patient’s condition and reaction to drugs and treatments ordered by the provider in the Electronic Medical Record upon administration.
  • Performs vaccine and medication administration on patients of all ages when ordered by the provider as needed.
  • Assists with clinical procedures done by the providers in the office as needed.
  • Ensures patient phone calls are reviewed and addressed daily  forwarding to the appropriate team member when necessary.
  • Ensures lab results are reported to the patient daily after being reviewed and requested by the provider
  • Performs patient education and participates in care planning process for chronic conditions both newly and previously diagnosed.
  • Performs other duties as requested by supervisor.*

job.title: IT Solutions Delivery Senior Analyst
job.description:
Summary

The IT Solutions Delivery Senior Analyst is responsible for supporting the planning, coordination, and implementation of enterprise IT solutions that enhance firmwide technology capabilities. This role focuses on executing high-quality solutions across key IT service areas such as Microsoft platforms, Unified Communications, Mobility, Infrastructure & Hosting, Financial, HR, Marketing and other core technologies.

Working closely with IT Project Managers, IT Solutions Delivery Leads, Engineers, Portfolio Managers, and business stakeholders, the IT Solutions Delivery Senior Analyst helps deliver initiatives by performing a wide range of tasks from translating business needs into technical requirements, executing, coordinating implementation tasks, and supporting successful user adoption. This position is ideal for a detail-oriented, collaborative professional with a strong grasp of enterprise IT systems and a passion for reliable execution.

Duties and Responsibilities

  • Assist with the delivery of IT projects by performing business/technical analysis, gathering and documenting requirements and processes, supporting solution design, developing reports, maintaining documentation, and participating in implementation activities.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure solutions meet business goals and align with IT standards.
  • Create and maintain project documentation such as process diagrams, test scripts, issue logs, training guides, and reports.
  • Participate in all aspects of testing, troubleshooting, and validation of new solutions before deployment.
  • Help coordinate end-user training, communications, and post-launch support.
  • Monitor project progress, risks, and issues, escalating concerns when appropriate.
  • Support vendor interactions for product evaluations, execution, troubleshooting, and feedback.
  • Maintain awareness of emerging technologies relevant to the firm’s IT strategy.
  • Prioritize user experience and quality in every phase of solution delivery.

Qualifications

Education and/or Experience:

Required:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology or related field.
  • Minimum of 4 years of experience implementing IT solutions in an enterprise setting.
  • Hands-on experience in project delivery, systems support, infrastructure and application rollout.
  • Familiarity with project management tools and methodologies (e.g., Smartsheet, Asana, Microsoft Project).
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills, with an ability to work across technical and non-technical teams.
  • Solid problem-solving and analytical thinking abilities.
  • Experience in testing and facilitation of testing with end users.
  • Experience preparing presentations and project related documentation.

Preferred:

  • Consulting experience
  • Legal industry experience
  • Professional services experience
  • Experience or exposure to Microsoft 365, unified communications, cloud services, IT security or enterprise infrastructure.
  • Background in business analysis or project management.

Other Skills and Abilities:

The following will also be required of the successful candidate:

  • Strong organizational skills
  • Strong attention to detail
  • Good judgment
  • Strong interpersonal communication skills
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Able to work harmoniously and effectively with others
  • Able to preserve confidentiality and exercise discretion
  • Able to work under pressure
  • Able to manage multiple projects with competing deadlines and priorities.

#toponehire

job.title: Production Planner (Manufacturing)
job.description:
Production Planner  – Manufacturing / Supply Chain
Greater Hartford, CT
Full-Time | On-Site

We are working with a well-established, growing manufacturing organization in the Greater Hartford area seeking a Production Planner to support demand planning, production scheduling, and S&OP alignment. This is a highly visible role working cross-functionally with operations, sales, and supply chain leadership.

Position Overview

The Production Planner will play a key role in aligning demand forecasts with production capabilities, ensuring efficient operations and high service levels. This individual will be heavily involved in forecasting, capacity planning, and continuous improvement initiatives within a structured manufacturing environment.

Key Responsibilities

Demand Planning & Forecasting

  • Support development of short-, mid-, and long-term demand forecasts

  • Analyze forecast vs. actuals and recommend corrective actions

  • Partner with sales to gather inputs and improve forecast accuracy

  • Participate in S&OP cycles, business reviews, and planning meetings

Production Planning & S&OP Execution

  • Translate demand forecasts into achievable production plans

  • Monitor capacity, identify bottlenecks, and recommend solutions

  • Issue and manage master production schedules across business units

  • Track production progress and resolve planning-related issues

Process Optimization & Data Management

  • Maintain and update ERP/MRP planning parameters, BOMs, and technical data

  • Support integration between demand planning and operational capabilities

  • Develop dashboards and reporting tools to support decision-making

KPI Monitoring & Reporting

  • Track key metrics including forecast accuracy, service levels, and capacity utilization

  • Prepare reports and support leadership with performance analysis and improvement initiatives

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering, Economics, or related field

  • 2–3+ years of experience in production planning, demand planning, or supply chain roles within manufacturing

  • Strong understanding of MRP/ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, JDA, Business Central, etc.)

  • Advanced Excel skills and experience with planning tools/reporting systems

  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills

  • Ability to work cross-functionally in a fast-paced environment

Work Environment

  • Combination of office and manufacturing floor exposure

  • PPE required in designated areas (safety glasses, safety shoes)

job.title: SENIOR BUYER (Manufacturing)
job.description:
Senior Buyer – Manufacturing
Farmington, CT
Full-Time | On-Site
* BASE COMPENSATION: 95,000-100,000

We are partnering with a well-established and growing manufacturing organization in the Greater Hartford area seeking a Senior Buyer to join their operations team. This is a high-impact role supporting supply chain strategy, supplier development, and new product initiatives within a regulated, precision-driven environment.

Position Overview

The Senior Buyer will be responsible for managing procurement activities, supporting new product introductions (NPI), and driving supplier performance in a highly regulated aerospace setting. This individual will play a key role in strengthening supplier relationships, improving processes, and ensuring compliance with industry standards.

Key Responsibilities

  • Issue and manage purchase orders for raw materials, outside services, and critical components

  • Support new product introductions (NPI), including supplier sourcing and qualification

  • Partner with internal teams to develop processes and obtain customer approvals

  • Evaluate supplier capabilities, including process capability and Manufacturing Readiness Levels (MRL)

  • Drive rate readiness and continuous process improvements

  • Assist in developing and executing insourcing strategies

  • Support procurement of equipment for insourced operations

  • Maintain and manage an approved supplier list in compliance with customer and regulatory requirements

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree (BS/BA) required

  • 5–7+ years of supply chain / purchasing experience, preferably within aerospace or regulated manufacturing

  • Strong understanding of EAR, ITAR, and DFAR compliance requirements

  • Experience working within aerospace quality and regulatory standards

  • Proven ability to build and manage supplier relationships

  • Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills

  • Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration abilities

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office or similar tools

Work Environment

  • Combination of office and manufacturing floor exposure

  • Standard PPE required in designated areas

This is an excellent opportunity to join a stable, growing organization with strong leadership and long-term career potential in the aerospace sector.

job.title: Financial/Budget Analyst
job.description:
37.5 work week!
Hybrid
 
RESPONSIBILITIES

•  Prepare journal entries in preparation for Budget Variance reporting and Regional Executive Summaries.
•  Annually, develop and prepare exhibits and reports for the Budget Work Package. These exhibits include the calculation of central processor budgets and electronic binding rate calculations.
•  Calculate payroll tax percentages for the Home Office and Regional Offices, input the percentages into the budget system.
•  Annually, review Home Office and Regional Office budgets and reprojections. Analyze for reasonableness and significant variances. Prepare budget review questions for these variances.
•  Test the annual LAN Budget System and reconcile the budget system data to the MAS 500 system after the data has been uploaded.
•  Key budget adjustments for the Home and Regional Offices into the budget system and the MAS 500 System. Print department, state and summary budget reports.
•  Run the monthly Services Rendered invoices interface, review invoices for reasonableness and make adjustments as necessary. Distribute the services rendered invoices.
•  Research variances, provide explanations, and prepare exhibits and narratives for the Operations Committee and Board Books.

  • Insurance industry experience preferred
  • Proficient in the use of Microsoft Office productivity tools (e.g. Outlook, Word, Excel) to manage work. Advanced skills using MS Excel.

  • Demonstrate strong verbal and written communication skills, including ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from groups or individuals.

  • Knowledge of complex accounting software applications.
  • Exposure to insurance industry and working knowledge of residual market mechanisms.

job.title: Project Accountant-Construction
job.description:
Position Summary
We are seeking a detail-oriented Project Accountant with strong experience in construction accounting to support financial management of multiple projects. This role is responsible for job costing, AIA billing, percentage-of-completion accounting, and Work-in-Progress (WIP) reporting, ensuring accurate financial tracking and compliance with industry standards. Experience with JD Edwards or similar construction ERP systems is highly preferred.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage full-cycle project accounting for assigned construction projects
  • Prepare and process AIA billings (progress billing), including lien waivers and supporting documentation
  • Monitor and maintain accurate job costing, ensuring costs are properly allocated and tracked
  • Calculate and analyze percentage-of-completion (POC) revenue recognition
  • Prepare and maintain Work-in-Progress (WIP) schedules, including cost-to-complete estimates and variance analysis
  • Collaborate with project managers to review budgets, forecasts, and cost projections
  • Reconcile project accounts, investigate discrepancies, and resolve issues in a timely manner
  • Track subcontractor payments and ensure compliance with contract terms
  • Assist with monthly, quarterly, and year-end close processes
  • Support audits by providing required financial documentation and analysis
  • Ensure compliance with GAAP and company accounting policies

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field
  • 3–7+ years of construction accounting experience
  • Strong knowledge of:
    • AIA billing
    • Percentage-of-completion accounting
    • Job costing
    • WIP reporting
  • Experience with JD Edwards preferred; experience with other construction ERP systems (e.g., Viewpoint, Sage 300 CRE, CMiC, Procore integrations) acceptable
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUPs, data analysis)
  • Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills
  • Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Excellent communication skills and ability to work cross-functionally with operations teams

Preferred Qualifications

  • CPA or progress toward certification is a plus
  • Experience working for a general contractor or specialty subcontractor
  • Familiarity with contract management and construction documentation

job.title: Marketing Project Manager – Medical Devices
job.description:
Marketing Project Manager – Medical Devices
Salary Target: $85K +/-

Position Overview 
The Marketing Project Manager is a vital member of our growth team, driving strategic initiatives that bridge the gap between marketing operations, sales, and client engagement. This role functions as both a project lead and a strategic partner, translating high-level marketing visions into actionable programs that deliver measurable impact.
In this role, you will collaborate closely with internal departments and external stakeholders to develop playbooks, standardized processes, and best practices that ensure seamless execution and long-term project health. The ideal candidate excels at the intersection of creative strategy and operational structure, balancing big-picture thinking with hands-on management. This position reports to the Director of Marketing Operations (or equivalent) and requires approximately 25% travel.

Key Responsibilities

  • End-to-End Project Leadership: Lead marketing initiatives from conceptualization through delivery, ensuring cross-functional alignment between marketing, sales, and success teams.
  • Operational Management: Develop and maintain detailed project plans, timelines, and budgets; track progress and provide transparent updates to all key stakeholders.
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Facilitate synergy between internal creative teams (design, content, web) and external vendors or agencies.
  • Strategic Problem Solving: Identify potential risks, resolve workflow bottlenecks, and ensure the timely delivery of goals.
  • Stakeholder Partnership: Work directly with external partners/clients to co-create engagement frameworks and playbooks that drive mutual success.
  • Onboarding & Best Practices: Lead planning sessions focused on aligning goals, establishing KPIs, and implementing standardized workflows.
  • Optimization: Gather performance feedback and analyze data to iterate on processes, improving both the user experience and campaign ROI.
  • Campaign Support: Assist in the execution of multi-channel marketing efforts, including email, social media, and paid digital media.
  • Content & Systems: Manage production workflows to ensure brand consistency; build scalable templates and documentation to improve team efficiency.
  • Reporting: Monitor campaign performance and utilize project management tools/dashboards to provide insights that inform future strategy.

Qualifications & Experience

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field.
  • Experience: 5+ years of professional experience in marketing project management, campaign operations, or client-facing marketing roles.
  • Industry Knowledge: Strong background in digital marketing, content strategy, and lifecycle management.
  • Enablement: Proven track record of working with cross-functional teams to develop process playbooks or sales enablement materials.
  • Technical Proficiency: Skilled in project management software (e.g., Asana, Monday.com) and marketing automation/CRM platforms (e.g., HubSpot, Salesforce).

job.title: Machinist
job.description:
Temp to Hire
Schedule:  1st or 2nd shift  6AM-2:30PM or 2:30PM-11PM
Pay: $40/hr 

We are seeking a skilled Machinist to set up, operate, and maintain lathes and milling machines. The ideal candidate will interpret technical drawings or samples to ensure precise production, using proper machine settings in line with engineering and quality specifications.
Key Responsibilities

  • Set up and operate lathes and milling machines.
  • Review technical drawings, blueprints, instructions, or samples to ensure production accuracy.
  • Perform all aspects of CNC machining, including setup, milling, turning, and close-tolerance manufacturing.
  • Take precise measurements and determine appropriate machine settings.
  • Program and operate manual, semi-automated, or automated machines (e.g., lathes, mills, grinders).
  • Use precision tools (e.g., calipers, micrometers, compasses) for accurate measurement.
  • Maintain physical stamina (standing for up to 8 hours) and the ability to lift up to 30 lbs.
  • Plan the proper sequence of tasks for efficient workflow.
  • Monitor operations and adjust feeds or settings as needed.
  • Maintain inventory of completed components.
  • Inspect output for quality assurance and address any defects.
  • Perform routine machine maintenance (e.g., refilling coolant, lubricants, hydraulic oils).
  • Keep a clean and safe workstation.
  • Support team operations by completing related tasks as needed.

Qualifications

  • Strong coordination and mechanical aptitude.
  • Exceptional attention to detail, especially in measurements.
  • Ability to read and interpret blueprints, route sheets, and specifications.
  • Vertical Mori & 5-Axis Mori exposure preferred
  • Knowledge of metal properties, including brass, stainless steel, bronze, and aluminum.
  • Solid written and verbal communication skills.
  • Proficiency in CAD software is highly desirable.
  • Strong mathematical and basic computer skills.
  • Experience with machine tools or mechanical assembly.
  • Understanding of safety protocols in a manufacturing environment.
  • High school diploma or equivalent required.
  • Completion of a vocational/technical program or apprenticeship preferred.

job.title: CFO
job.description:
Position Summary
We are seeking a strategic and hands-on Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to lead all financial functions of our growing construction company. The CFO will play a critical role in shaping the company’s financial strategy, ensuring strong fiscal management, and supporting operational excellence across multiple projects. The ideal candidate brings deep experience in the construction industry, including job costing, contract management, and financial oversight of large-scale projects.
Key Responsibilities
Strategic Leadership

  • Partner with the CEO and executive team to develop and execute long-term financial strategies
  • Provide financial insights to support business growth, expansion, and investment decisions
  • Lead financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and performance analysis

Financial Management & Reporting

  • Oversee all accounting operations, including general ledger, AP/AR, payroll, and financial reporting
  • Ensure timely and accurate preparation of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements
  • Maintain compliance with GAAP and regulatory requirements
  • Present financial results and key metrics to executive leadership and stakeholders

Construction-Specific Oversight

  • Manage and optimize job costing systems to ensure accurate project profitability tracking
  • Oversee work-in-progress (WIP) reporting, revenue recognition, and percentage-of-completion accounting
  • Monitor project budgets, change orders, and cost controls across multiple jobs
  • Partner with operations and project managers to improve margin performance and forecasting accuracy

Cash Flow & Risk Management

  • Oversee cash flow planning, forecasting, and working capital management
  • Manage banking relationships, credit facilities, and bonding capacity
  • Ensure compliance with lender and surety requirements
  • Identify and mitigate financial and operational risks

Systems & Process Improvement

  • Evaluate and enhance financial systems, including ERP and construction accounting software
  • Implement process improvements to increase efficiency, accuracy, and scalability
  • Leverage data analytics to drive decision-making

Leadership & Team Development

  • Build, mentor, and lead a high-performing finance and accounting team
  • Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (MBA or CPA strongly preferred)
  • 10+ years of progressive financial leadership experience, with significant time in the construction industry
  • Strong knowledge of construction accounting principles, including WIP reporting and job costing
  • Experience with construction ERP systems (e.g., Procore, Viewpoint, Sage 300/100 Contractor, or similar)
  • Proven track record of strategic financial leadership and operational partnership
  • Strong understanding of cash flow management, financing, and bonding requirements
  • Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills

Preferred Skills

  • Experience supporting multi-entity or multi-project environments
  • Familiarity with contract types (lump sum, cost-plus, GMP, etc.)
  • Experience with mergers & acquisitions or company scaling initiatives
  • Advanced financial modeling and data analysis capabilities
