job.title: Director of Nursing (DON)
job.description:
Hiring for a Director of Nursing/Clinical Services for a healthcare facility in Boston, MA

MUST have at least 2 years of recent DON experience 
Active RN license in Massachusetts required

Permanent position.
Service area: Boston – Roxbury/Dorchester/Brookline (12 total areas)
Travel: may have to help with assessments, but mostly just oversight sitting onsite at head quarters in Boston
Schedule: M-F-full-time day shift (no nights, not weekends, no holidays)
Notes: Hybrid opportunity, come in as needed probably 4 days a week to start to get a handle on the work (2-3 days on site after comfortable and remote other days). COVID vaccine required.
Direct reports: 17 RNs, 2 managers
EMR: SAMS
Rate: $130-140k (based on experience)

Responsibilities:

  • Assess potential needs of each patient, nursing assessment/intake
  • Minimal clinical work such as initial assessments, BP/vitals, and documentation
  • Lead nursing operations for home care programs, care transitions, and community-based programs.
  • Responsible for motivating, providing support, consultation and regularly scheduled supervision of RN Managers and staff RNs.
  • Partner in the development of internal quality assurance activities in collaboration with the Director of Projects and Quality including random case file review, and policy and procedure training.
  • Ensure program compliance with all EOEA, state and federal policies and regulations, timeliness of assessments/community screens.
  • Develop and implement clinical and operational policies and procedures to promote quality, efficient, cost-effective service delivery.
  • Collaborate with Director of Home Care and the Director of Client Services and Community Engagement to coordinate services that promote independence and well-being
  • Assist in the design, operation, implementation, and evaluation of new and existing programs.
  • Responsible for interviewing, hiring, and training of all RNs and participating in the orientation process for program staff.
  • Identify RN training needs and collaborate with Human Resources to enhance staff skills and improve performance

Requirements:

  • Master’s degree in either nursing, social work, human services, public administration, gerontology, and/or other appropriate fields, plus six years’ experience in human services, and at least three years of which has been spent in a supervisory/administrative position.
  • Or bachelor’s degree in either nursing, social work, human services, public administration, gerontology, and/or other appropriate fields, plus seven years’ experience in human services, at least five years of which have been spent in a supervisory/administrative position.
  • Or associate’s degree in science and extensive experience in nursing in Home Care or Community Health. What is considered extensive experience, may consider, at least eight years of experience.
  • MA RN licensure
  • Bilingual (Spanish, Russian, Portuguese, Creole) preferred but not required
  • Must have community health experience in some capacity
  • Strong leadership and managerial skills
  • Comfortability with a diverse environment

Benefits: BCBS health, vision & dental- 85% paid)
2 weeks of vacation

12 paid holidays

3 personal days

Sick time

403 B- full match after 6 months (variances depending on contribution and time worked) highest match is 5-7%

job.title: Customer Service Representative
job.description:
Position Summary
A growing building materials manufacturer in the Bristol area is seeking a detail-oriented Customer Service Representative to support sales and operations teams. This role serves as a key point of contact for customers and internal departments, ensuring orders are processed accurately and delivered on schedule. The position requires strong organizational skills and experience working with SAP and Salesforce to manage orders, customer data, and project information.

Key Responsibilities

  • Serve as a primary point of contact for customers regarding orders, product information, and project updates

  • Process and track customer orders using SAP and maintain accurate customer and order data in Salesforce

  • Review purchase orders, quotes, and project documentation to ensure accuracy and alignment with product specifications

  • Coordinate with sales, production, shipping, and procurement teams to ensure timely order fulfillment

  • Monitor order status, shipping schedules, and backlogs to keep customers informed on delivery timelines

  • Assist the sales team with quote updates and order documentation when needed

  • Maintain organized records of orders, customer communications, and project documentation

  • Resolve customer inquiries, complaints, or order issues while maintaining a high level of professionalism

  • Support internal reporting and data updates within CRM and ERP systems

  • Ensure all information is entered accurately and in a timely manner to support production scheduling

Qualifications

  • 2–5 years of customer service, order processing, or sales support experience

  • Experience working in manufacturing, construction, or building materials environments strongly preferred

  • Proficiency with SAP and Salesforce

  • Strong Microsoft Office skills (Excel, Outlook, Word)

  • Excellent communication and customer service skills

  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail

  • Ability to manage multiple orders and deadlines in a fast-paced environment

Work Environment

  • Primarily office-based within a manufacturing facility

  • Regular coordination with sales, warehouse, and production teams

  • Standard full-time schedule (Monday–Friday)

Compensation

  • $25–$28 per hour, depending on experience

  • Full-time opportunity with potential for long-term growth within the organization

#toponehire

TECHNICAL DIRECTOR

Responsibilities

  • Ensures the health and safety of employees under their direction;
  • Ensures that their staff uses all operational management systems to monitor and maintain high quality standards;
  • Maintains good work practices in their departments;
  • Plans, organizes, develops, monitors, and evaluates operations related to the maintenance of injection molding machines and mechanical/electrical equipment;
  • Supervises and approves department-related purchases;
  • Coordinates team communication between different shifts;
  • Ensures proper equipment maintenance (preventive and regular maintenance);
  • Establishes and directs preventive and corrective maintenance of production equipment to optimize results and ensure optimal and seamless efficiency;
  • Coordinates maintenance work on the factory site buildings and offices;
  • Maintains and controls departmental expenses, including subcontracting costs;
  • Is responsible for requesting and monitoring special projects;
  • Continuously seeks out new opportunities to improve speed, quality, cost and delivery accuracy;
  • Participates in budget development, its management and controls maintenance expenses – spare parts, replacement parts OT, and outside contractors;
  • Is responsible for maximizing employee efficiency using systems like predictive and preventative maintenance;
  • Overall Plant uptime metrics and changeover speed metrics to provide the Plant operations a reliable nimble platform up which to manufacture quality products reliably.
  • Supervises employees and ensures good team spirit and harmony; and
  • Participates in the Plant Manufacturing Leadership Team..

Requirements
 

  • College diploma in Industrial Engineering or equivalent experience in manufacturing management;
  • 5 to 10 years of experience as a manager in a manufacturing environment;
  • Knowledge and experience with continuous improvement principles

Knowledge and Skills
 

  • Lean 6Sigma tools
  • Preventative and Predictive Maintenance systems and optimized use
  • Strong technical background – teaching and trouble shooting complex issues
  • Comfortable around higher management and strong presentation skills
  • Knowledge of the Office Suite;
  • Excellent communication skills, both oral and written;
  • Good ability to manage change and resolve problems;
  • Good analytical skills;
  • Must represent significant added value in all aspects of teamwork.

job.title: Controller
job.description:
Position Overview
We are seeking an experienced Controller with a strong background in construction accounting to lead and manage all accounting operations. This role will oversee the full-cycle accounting function, ensure accurate project financial reporting, and drive process improvements through ERP and systems optimization.
The ideal candidate brings deep experience in construction financial management, job costing, percentage-of-completion accounting, and ERP systems implementation, and thrives in a fast-paced, project-based environment.

Key Responsibilities
Financial Management & Reporting

  • Lead and manage the full-cycle accounting process, including general ledger, accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll, and month-end close.
  • Prepare timely and accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements.
  • Maintain strong internal controls and ensure compliance with accounting standards and company policies.
  • Manage cash flow forecasting and working capital planning.

Construction Accounting

  • Oversee job costing and project accounting, ensuring accurate cost tracking and reporting.
  • Monitor Work-in-Progress (WIP) schedules and percentage-of-completion (POC) revenue recognition.
  • Collaborate with project managers to review budgets, cost projections, and job profitability.
  • Manage change orders, retainage tracking, and subcontractor payment processes.
  • Ensure compliance with industry requirements including lien waivers, contract accounting, and project reporting.

Systems & Process Improvement

  • Lead or support ERP system implementations, upgrades, or integrations.
  • Optimize accounting workflows through automation and process improvements.
  • Develop and maintain financial dashboards and reporting tools.
  • Partner with leadership to improve data visibility and operational reporting.

Leadership & Team Management

  • Manage and mentor accounting staff.
  • Establish best practices and scalable accounting processes.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with operations, project management, and executive leadership.

Qualifications
Required

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field.
  • 8+ years of accounting experience, with significant experience in the construction industry.
  • Strong knowledge of:
    • Construction job costing
    • WIP reporting
    • Percentage-of-completion accounting
    • Project financial management
  • Experience managing the full accounting cycle and month-end close.
  • Experience with construction accounting software or ERP systems.

Preferred

  • CPA or CMA designation.
  • Experience with construction ERP platforms such as Viewpoint, Sage 300 CRE, Foundation, CMiC, or NetSuite.
  • Experience leading or supporting ERP implementations or major system upgrades.
  • Advanced Excel and financial modeling skills.

#toponehire

job.title: Senior Infrastructure Engineer – Messaging
job.description:
POSITION OVERVIEW

This senior role leads the day-to-day administration and engineering of our Exchange Servers and Exchange Online environment. The ideal candidate pairs strong attention to detail with deep email infrastructure knowledge and advanced PowerShell and Microsoft Graph automation skills. The engineer plays a critical role in designing, implementing, and supporting a reliable, secure, and scalable Microsoft 365 messaging environment with a focus on Exchange Online, ensuring availability, security, compliance, and performance in a hybrid, multi-office law firm environment.

The role also serves as a technical lead for Microsoft 365 messaging services, including Microsoft Purview, Mimecast (or similar email security platforms), Microsoft Teams, centralized email signature tools such as Exclaimer, and Microsoft 365 tenant settings that affect mail flow, hygiene, retention, and compliance, in close partnership with Endpoint Management and Identity teams for Intune, Conditional Access, and related identity controls.

KEY REQUIREMENTS

  • Serve as the technical lead and key owner for the firm’s Exchange Online, Exchange Server, and related Microsoft 365 messaging services, ensuring availability, security, compliance, and performance in a hybrid law firm environment.
  • Help drive the maintenance, design, development, support, and security of the firm’s messaging services, treating email and calendaring as critical business applications.
  • Provide 24×7 support for critical production messaging systems as part of an on-call rotation.
  • Lead or play a key role in migration efforts from Exchange Server Subscription Edition to Exchange Online, and contribute to planning for future tenant-to-tenant Exchange Online/Microsoft 365 migrations to support acquisitions and integrations.
  • Manage and monitor Microsoft 365 tenant components that affect messaging, including mail flow, message hygiene, retention, compliance, DKIM, DMARC, SPF, and Mimecast (or similar products).
  • Collaborate with Endpoint Management and Identity teams on Intune, Conditional Access, MDM/MAM, and identity controls (Entra ID, Active Directory, Okta) to ensure secure, low-friction access to messaging services.
  • Provide advanced administration, technical support, and troubleshooting for complex issues involving Microsoft Exchange Online and Exchange Server in a hybrid environment, acting as a Tier 3 escalation point.
  • Configure and support Microsoft Purview features related to messaging and collaboration, including data loss prevention, retention, and eDiscovery, in partnership with Security and Compliance teams.
  • Design, implement, and maintain automation and reporting for messaging operations using PowerShell and Microsoft Graph to reduce manual work and improve consistency at scale.
  • Develop and maintain documentation, runbooks, knowledge articles, and monitoring/alerting for messaging and collaboration services.
  • Implement capacity planning and proactive remediation to address issues before they impact users, and provide input to IT leadership on technology improvements, standards, and roadmap for messaging and Microsoft 365 collaboration.
  • Partner with Help Desk, Deskside, and other support teams to resolve escalated messaging tickets, mentor staff on common patterns, drive root cause analysis, and reduce recurring issues.
  • Experience with centralized email signature management platforms (such as Exclaimer) or similar products for firmwide email signatures and branding is a strong plus.
  • Other duties as assigned.

Employees approved for flexible work arrangements are expected to be available and maintain a practice of reliable, consistent attendance during their scheduled work shift, including, but not limited to, via Teams/instant message, Zoom, email, voicemail, and phone.

 

TECHNICAL AND SKILL REQUIREMENTS

  • Excellent communication, collaboration, and customer service skills, with the ability to explain technical issues in clear, business-friendly language.
  • Strong time management and organization; able to lead multiple concurrent projects and operational priorities.
  • Motivated by curiosity, ingenuity, and a desire to learn and grow in a constantly evolving Microsoft 365 environment.
  • 7+ years of IT infrastructure experience, with at least 5 years focused on Exchange administration and Microsoft 365 messaging in a mid- to large-sized enterprise.
  • Deep experience administering Exchange Online and Exchange Server in hybrid configurations, including cross-version upgrades and complex mail flow.
  • Proven experience leading Exchange migrations from on-premises Exchange (including Exchange Server Subscription Edition) to Exchange Online; exposure to tenant-to-tenant or multi-organization Microsoft 365 migrations is strongly preferred.
  • Advanced PowerShell scripting skills and practical experience using Microsoft Graph to automate and manage Exchange Online and related Microsoft 365 messaging tasks and workflows.
  • Experience administering and supporting Mimecast (or a similar email security platform) and managing email hygiene controls such as DKIM, DMARC, and SPF.
  • Knowledge of Microsoft 365 integration and migration tools and methods, such as Azure AD Connect and hybrid Exchange configurations.
  • Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot complex messaging and client issues end to end, including mail flow, connectivity, and client performance, and to drive durable fixes.

Stand-out candidates will also possess the following skill sets:

    • Experience partnering with Endpoint Management and Identity teams on Intune, Conditional Access, and MDM/MAM controls for secure, low-friction access to messaging.
    • Experience configuring and supporting Microsoft Purview (or a similar platform) for cloud-based security, compliance, retention, and DLP.
    • Comprehensive knowledge of Active Directory and identity management principles (Okta, AD, Entra ID) in the context of messaging and Microsoft 365 access.
    • Experience creating and maintaining documentation, standards, and runbooks, and mentoring junior engineers or support staff.
    • Experience with centralized email signature management platforms (such as Exclaimer) or similar products for firmwide signatures and branding.
    • Experience working in a law firm or other professional services environment where email and calendaring are mission critical.

 

IMMEDIATE POSITION GOALS

  • Contribute significantly and immediately to the Exchange Online migration project, including planning, coexistence, and post-migration stabilization for the firm’s transition from Exchange Server Subscription Edition.
  • Develop and execute a plan to migrate Public Folders to Exchange Online with minimal disruption to users.
  • Collaborate with Identity teams on Entra ID, Active Directory, and Okta workflows that support messaging services, helping to streamline provisioning and offboarding access.
  • Review and enhance documentation, runbooks, and monitoring for messaging services to support reliable 24×7 operations and effective escalation handling.
  • Assess the current messaging architecture and document considerations and patterns to enable future tenant-to-tenant Microsoft 365/Exchange Online migrations in support of potential acquisitions.

#toponehire

job.title: Quality Control Analyst
job.description:
Environmental Monitoring Quality Control Analyst
Location:
Burlington, MA -Occasionally traveling to Cambridge, MA
Hours:

  • Monday – Friday 8:30am – 5:00pm

Position Duties:

  • Perform routine Environmental Monitoring and utilities testing.
  • Perform routine QC testing for raw materials, in-process, DP, and stability samples using equipment such as balances, pipettes, pH meter, UV/Visible spectrophotometer, plate reader, etc. in accordance with SOPs and cGMP guidelines.
  • Perform cell culture microbial and/or molecular assays.
  • Perform microbial limits, Bioburden testing, and identifying and trending microorganisms.
  • Lab support including but not limited to glass washing, autoclaving, etc.
  • Assist in the review of QC data and provide summaries to management as needed.
  • Report out-of-specification or out-of-trend results in a timely manner to ensure appropriate actions are taken.
  • Other duties as assigned.

Qualifications:

  • The top requirement is having environmental monitoring experience
  • HS Diploma, 4+ years’ industry experience in cGMP lab environment, or equivalent.
  • AS/BS degree in microbiology, molecular biology, biochemistry or a related discipline. AS, 2-4 years’ industry experience. BS, 0-2 years’ industry experience in cGMP lab environment, or equivalent.

job.title: Primary Care Provider (Outpatient)
job.description:
Primary Care Provider (outpatient)

A well-established, independent outpatient medical practice is seeking a Primary Care Physician or Advanced Practice Provider to join its growing team due to increasing patient demand. This practice has served the community for more than 30 years and continues to expand as patients seek alternatives to large health systems. Providers benefit from a collaborative environment, strong clinical autonomy, and the opportunity to build long-term patient relationships.
The organization emphasizes thorough patient care rather than high-volume metrics and offers a supportive staff structure that allows providers to focus on clinical work.
Position Overview

  • Full-time outpatient primary care role
  • Open to MD, DO, Nurse Practitioner (NP), or Physician Assistant (PA)
  • Monday–Friday schedule with rotating Saturday call (9:00 AM – 12:00 PM)

Clinical Environment
This growing practice includes multiple providers offering primary care and specialty services. The organization strives to provide a comprehensive care model by offering several on-site diagnostic and ancillary services to support coordinated patient care.
Services available on-site include:

  • Cardiac and general ultrasound (including echocardiography)
  • Cardiac stress testing (standard and nuclear)
  • Heart monitoring services
  • Sleep studies
  • Blood pressure monitoring
  • Walk-in care and seasonal clinics

Providers are encouraged to practice with a high degree of clinical independence while maintaining a team-oriented approach. The culture values flexibility and recognizes the importance of work-life balance.
Qualifications

  • MD or DO, or licensed Nurse Practitioner or Physician Assistant
  • Board-certified or board-eligible in Family Medicine or Internal Medicine (physicians)
  • New graduates may be considered, though prior clinical experience is preferred
  • Interest in building a long-term practice and patient panel

Compensation

  • Physician base salary up to $220,000 (flexibility possible based on experience)
  • NP/PA base salary up to $125,000
  • Annual performance bonus based on revenue collections
  • Partnership opportunities available for physicians

Benefits

  • Health insurance (employer covers a portion of premium)
  • Short-term and long-term disability
  • Life insurance
  • 401(k) with employer match and profit sharing
  • Malpractice coverage and licensure renewals
  • CME: 5 days and $1,500 annually
  • 3 weeks PTO (combined bank for vacation/sick time)
  • 7 paid holidays

job.title: Staff Accountant (Multi-Entity Operations) – North Shore
job.description:
Staff Accountant (Multi-Entity Operations)

Salary $80-90K

Position Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented Staff Accountant to manage the general ledger and financial reporting for a diverse portfolio of business entities. This role is responsible for full-cycle accounting, including year-end tax preparation, budgeting, and oversight of accounts payable and operational inventory tracking.

Core Responsibilities

  • General Ledger & Financial Control: Perform monthly G/L reviews, prepare recurring journal entries, and collaborate with department heads to resolve accounting discrepancies.

  • Operational Reporting: Manage Cost of Sales (COS) reporting and conduct periodic inventory audits. Reconcile point-of-sale transactions and operational revenue streams.

  • Financial Analysis: Prepare comprehensive internal financial packages, including P&L statements, variance analysis, and reforecasting for management review.

  • Real Estate & Project Accounting: Handle specialized reporting for lease-based entities, including CAM reconciliations and capital job cost tracking. Process change orders and maintain capital budgets.

  • Year-End & Tax Prep: Finalize year-end adjustments, depreciation schedules, and amortization workpapers to support external tax filings.

  • Budgeting: Assist in the creation of annual operating budgets on both an accrual and cash basis.

Qualifications & Requirements

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related business field.

  • Experience: 3–5 years of professional accounting experience, preferably within property management, hospitality, or multi-unit operations.

  • Technical Skills: High proficiency in Excel and Adobe. Experience with industry-standard ERP/Property Management software and specialized POS integration tools is highly preferred.

  • Key Competencies: Must possess strong knowledge of lease interpretation, commercial financial language, and job cost reporting.

  • Soft Skills: A meticulous eye for detail, the ability to pivot between shifting priorities, and a collaborative mindset for a small-office environment.

job.title: Operations Manager
job.description:
Operations Manager
A microelectronic inductor manufacturer, is seeking a highly motivated individual to join our team as Operations Manager. This individual will be responsible for overseeing the Customer Service, Manufacturing and Quality Departments with the goal of smoothly guiding purchase orders from acceptance to shipment.
This role reports to the Vice President of the Company.
Responsibilities:

  • Responsible for overseeing the Customer Service, Manufacturing and Quality Department Managers by fostering growth and collaboration amongst the team.
  • Ensure each department is running efficiently by working with Managers to resolve bottlenecks and implement process improvements.
  • Formulating and implementing policies to achieve company goals by working with stakeholders within the departments.
  • Work with department Managers to identify staffing needs and oversee the hiring process with the various temp agencies.
  • Assist managers with employee issues.
  • Interface with customers to resolve order, quality and delivery issues.
  • Provide feedback to Senior Management on issues and ways to improve processes.
  • Manage vendor relationships and supply chain logistics to ensure on time delivery of materials.
  • Occasional Saturdays from 6am to noon are required.
  • Perform other operations related duties as directed by Senior Management.

Required Skills:

  • Minimum of 5 years’ experience in a Manufacturing Operations Management, Production Management, Quality Management or similar leadership role.
  • Minimum of 8 years experience in the Manufacturing Industry.
  • Experience working in a small company (less than 100 employees)
  • Strong team building and communications skills.
  • Exceptional problem-solving skills.
  • Be well organized, possess the ability to multitask, and be able to work with minimal supervision and from verbal instructions.
  • Proficient with ISO 9001 and Military Specification standards.
  • Proficient with Microsoft Office (Word, Excel & Outlook)
  • Experience with Microsoft Dynamics is a plus.
  • B.S degree in Business Administration, Management or a related field.

Benefits:

  • 401K Plan with Match

job.title: Principal Software Engineer
job.description:
We are seeking a Senior Principal Software Engineer to join our highly dynamic team developing machine control solutions for our products. 
 

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

Work as part of or lead multidisciplinary teams of engineers to gather requirements, architect, design, implement and deploy real-time software to control high speed mail processing equipment

Coordination of deliverables that affect multiple projects or products.

Define, perform or guide all aspects of system level testing and utilize results to improve solution performance

Capable of leading cross functional project teams on high-risk projects to success against critical deadlines

Plan and maintain project schedules, ensure solutions meet all requirements and are delivered within specified timeframes and budget allocations

Collaborate closely with project team members, product management and internal and external partners

KEY RELATIONSHIPS

The Senior Principal Software Engineer will collaborate closely with cross-functional engineering teams, product and project managers, and senior leadership, while also interfacing with manufacturing, customer support, and external partners to deliver high-performance machine control solutions.

QUALIFICATIONS (SKILLS, EXPERIENCE, EDUCATION AND CERTIFICATIONS)

MINIMUM:

– 10 years of experience with large scale, object-oriented software design & development over the full life cycle process

– 8 years software development with strong C++ programming skills

– Experience designing software controls for mechatronic systems

– Experience with real-time operating systems, such as RTX

– Proficiency using a systems level approach to developing solutions

– Superior analytical and creative problem-solving skills

– Proven troubleshooting skills and the ability to resolve complex system level problems, including mechanical and electrical issues under tight timeframes

– Experience working with Agile development methodologies

– Experience in software quality assurance and testing

– Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, including the ability to converse with engineers and non-engineers of diverse backgrounds and present to senior management

– BS or MS Degree in Computer Science or related discipline

PREFERRED:

 – Experience with Java, C#, .NET, ASP.net

– Knowledge of web based service architecture and application development

– Experience with database technologies including SQLServer and Oracle

– Knowledge of network protocols, hardware and standards

– Experience with motion control and servo motor control

  #toponehire

job.title: Principal SAP Analyst
job.description:
Seeking an experienced Principal Analyst specializing in SAP Order to Cash (OTC) and Finance to Manage (FTM) processes. The ideal candidate will bring 10+ years of hands-on SAP experience, with deep expertise in OTC/FTM modules, strong knowledge of interlinked modules, and proven ability to design, configure, and optimize complex business processes. This role will serve as a strategic partner to IT and Finance leadership, ensuring seamless integration across systems and third-party platforms.

Required Skills:

  • Lead end-to-end solution design, configuration, and implementation of SAP OTC and FTM processes
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless integration with MM, SD, FI, CO, and other interlinked modules
  • Manage and optimize integration with third-party tax engines (e.g. OneSource, Avalara, Vertex) and third-party invoicing platforms (e.g. Edicom, Ariba, Coupa)
  • Provide expert-level configuration for pricing, billing, credit management, accounts receivable, and general ledger processes
  • Partner with business stakeholders to translate requirements into scalable SAP solutions
  • Drive continuous improvement by identifying process gaps and recommending enhancements
  • Lead troubleshooting and root cause analysis for complex system issues
  • Mentor junior analysts and serve as a subject matter expert across OTC/FTM domains
  • Ensure compliance with financial regulations, tax requirements, and internal controls
  • Collaborate with technical teams on interfaces, data migration, and system upgrades
  • Support testing cycles (unit, integration, UAT) and ensure high-quality deliverables
  • Document functional specifications, configuration guides, and process flows
  • Experience in integrations with CRM and CPQ tools (e.g. Salesforce)

Qualifications

  • 10+ years of SAP OTC/FTM experience, with at least 5 years in a senior or principal analyst role
  • Strong expertise in SAP SD, FI, CO, MM and their interdependencies
  • Proven experience with third-party integrations (tax engines, invoicing platforms, payment gateways)
  • Advanced configuration skills in pricing, billing, AR/AP, GL, and credit management
  • Experience with SAP S/4HANA migrations or implementations preferred
  • Solid understanding of finance processes, compliance, and audit requirements
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills
  • Ability to lead cross-functional workshops and present solutions to senior stakeholders
  • Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Finance, or related field (Master’s preferred)
  • SAP certifications in OTC/FTM or related modules are a plus
  • Salesforce experience is a plus

#toponehire

job.title: Director of Accounting
job.description:
Director of Accounting
New Bedford, MA

Job Details
Full-time
From $104,000 a year

Director of Accounting
Program & Location: Administration, New Bedford, MA
Status: Full Time Salaried Employee
Shift: 8:30am-5pm

Successful candidate should have a Bachelor’s degree in accounting or related field and 5+ years of progressively responsible experience in the not-for-profit healthcare industry. Candidates with experience in the preparation of the Uniform Financial Report (UFR).
Must have strong knowledge of accounting principles, analytical and computer skills, be well organized with the ability to research data sources. An ideal candidate will have excellent interpersonal, communication and problem-solving skills, while being a self-starter and a team player with the ability to multi-task and work independently with minimal supervision.

Responsibilities

  • Utilize accounting skills to support a nonprofit organization provide diverse mental health, substance use disorder, and other support services for social challenges.
  • Oversees the operations of the accounting department, including the design of an organizational structure adequate for achieving the department’s goals and objectives.
  • Coordinates and reviews the preparation of annual budget.
  • Maintains and enforces a documented system of accounting policies.
  • Supervision of accounting staff including maintaining staff by recruiting, selecting, and training employees.
  • Creating a well-supported accounting department, through coaching and disciplining employees, planning, monitoring and appraising job results.
  • Issue timely and complete financial statements, including actual to budget variances analysis.
  • Coordinates and reviews annual audits, including the preparation of the UFR.
  • Coordinates and reviews the federal and state tax returns.
  • Coordinates and reviews timely compliance filings with various agencies.
  • Provides financial analysis reports as needed.
  • Monitors compliance with debt covenants.
  • Monitors cost reimbursement contract’s billings and compliance.
  • Coordinates timely monthly and year end close process.
  • Presents financials to program management.
  • Ensures compliance with GAAP.
  • Maintains patient/client confidence and protects operations by keeping financial information confidential.
  • Other assignments as deemed necessary.

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in accounting or related field.
  • 5+ years of progressively responsible experience in the not-for-profit healthcare industry.
  • Advanced excel skills.

About Us:
Health and human service agency whose mission is to treat and prevent substance use disorders and mental illness. The organization recognizes, more now than ever, the need for a work life balance! Accounting staff are critical of the work that is done in the agency, ensuring that funds flow through the organization efficiently. Our leave time package for eligible staff is unmatched and allows employees the time off they need to rest, relax and enjoy life! The organization also understands the need to support and recognize staff diversity. In addition to our leave time package, we offer one floating holiday per quarter to allow staff the time off to observe and celebrate their cultures and beliefs! Also, we are consistently monitoring the economy and like agencies to adjust our pay grades at least once a year to, not only remain competitive, but to maintain livable wages. Continued education opportunities assist accountants in expanding their skills.

Benefits

  • Medical Insurance
  • Dental Insurance
  • Vision Insurance
  • Long & short-term disability
  • Discounted auto/home and renters’ insurance
  • 403b – Retirement
  • FSA & DCA
  • PFML
  • Employee Assistance Program
  • Bonuses & Referral
  • Eligibility for free classes to become a Licensed Counselor or Recovery Coach
  • Education days to use towards CEU’s
  • Free meals at select programs and when available
  • Unmatched Leave Time (FT employees can earn up to 3 weeks in first year)

job.title: SENIOR PROJECT ENGINEER- MANUFACTURING
job.description:
Senior Project Engineer – Manufacturing

NORTH HAVEN CT
BASE COMPENSATION RANGE: 150,000-155,000

We are seeking a Senior Project Engineer to lead the successful execution of complex new product development and industrialization initiatives within a highly engineered manufacturing environment. This role serves as a key technical leader responsible for driving projects from concept through full-scale production, ensuring alignment across engineering, manufacturing, supply chain, quality, and commercial teams.

The ideal candidate combines strong engineering expertise, manufacturing knowledge, and program leadership to deliver innovative products that meet performance, cost, and schedule objectives.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead the end-to-end execution of new product development programs, ensuring successful transition from concept and design through pilot builds and sustained production.

  • Coordinate cross-functional activities across engineering, manufacturing, quality, supply chain, and operations to meet program milestones and deliverables.

  • Drive the structured product development and stage-gate process, ensuring project timelines, technical requirements, and deliverables are achieved.

  • Evaluate design for manufacturability (DFM) and collaborate with engineering teams to optimize product designs for scalable production.

  • Support supplier identification, evaluation, qualification, and process approval, working closely with strategic sourcing and manufacturing partners.

  • Develop and maintain project cost models and product cost targets, tracking cost performance throughout the development lifecycle.

  • Coordinate prototype builds, pilot production, and pre-production validation activities to ensure readiness for serial manufacturing.

  • Monitor process development, qualification, and part approval activities, supporting resolution of technical or quality issues.

  • Identify and mitigate program risks, implementing corrective actions and escalation plans when necessary.

  • Provide regular program updates to leadership, communicating status, risks, and key technical decisions.

  • Collaborate with Sales and Marketing teams to understand customer requirements, provide technical input, and support product demonstrations or sample evaluations.

  • Participate in continuous improvement initiatives, leveraging lean manufacturing principles to enhance processes and project execution.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or related technical discipline.

  • Proven experience leading or supporting new product development and product launch initiatives in a manufacturing environment.

  • Strong understanding of manufacturing processes, sourcing strategies, quality systems, and project management principles.

  • Experience working with a broad range of manufacturing technologies, such as:

    • Forging

    • Stamping

    • Casting

    • Polymer injection molding

    • Precision machining

    • Metal injection molding

    • Heat treatment

  • Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional teams and drive projects to completion within aggressive timelines.

  • Strong analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills.

Work Environment

This position is onsite within a manufacturing facility, working closely with engineering, operations, and production teams to support product development and industrialization activities.

Physical Requirements

  • Ability to lift and transport items up to 20 lbs as needed.

  • Ability to operate standard computer and office equipment for extended periods.

  • Ability to remain stationary for portions of the workday while performing analytical and project management tasks.

  • Ability to communicate effectively with team members across the organization, both verbally and in writing.

Senior Project Engineer – Manufacturing

We are seeking a Senior Project Engineer to lead the successful execution of complex new product development and industrialization initiatives within a highly engineered manufacturing environment. This role serves as a key technical leader responsible for driving projects from concept through full-scale production, ensuring alignment across engineering, manufacturing, supply chain, quality, and commercial teams.

The ideal candidate combines strong engineering expertise, manufacturing knowledge, and program leadership to deliver innovative products that meet performance, cost, and schedule objectives.

job.description:
Senior Accountant

Location: Melville, NY
Schedule: Hybrid (2 days onsite / 3 days remote)
Compensation: $76,000 – $78,000 + benefits

Overview

A growing organization is seeking a Senior Accountant to join its finance team in Melville, NY. This position offers a hybrid work environment, allowing professionals to balance remote work with in-office collaboration.

The Senior Accountant will support the day-to-day accounting operations, financial reporting, and month-end close process while partnering with internal teams to ensure accurate and timely financial information.   • Prepare and post journal entries

  • Maintain supporting schedules and documentation for balance sheet accounts

job.title: PROGRAM MANAGER – AEROSPACE MANUFACTURING
job.description:
Program Manager – Engineering
Salary: $125,000 – $135,000
Location: Connecticut | Full-Time

A growing engineering and manufacturing organization is seeking a Program Manager to oversee complex technical projects from planning through execution. This role will work closely with engineering leadership and cross-functional teams to ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget, and aligned with customer requirements.

The Program Manager will report directly to the Vice President of Engineering and play a key role in coordinating resources, managing customer relationships, and driving project success in a fast-paced manufacturing environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead overall project planning, scheduling, and budget management, including manpower, equipment, materials, and cost allocation.

  • Coordinate with engineering, production, and other departments to ensure projects meet technical specifications, timelines, and budget targets.

  • Monitor project progress and identify potential risks or delays, implementing corrective actions when needed.

  • Develop alternative project plans and mitigation strategies to maintain delivery schedules.

  • Support sales and bid development efforts, contributing to project planning and governance during the proposal stage.

  • Manage contracts and customer relationships, ensuring compliance with service-level agreements and project requirements.

  • Maintain clear communication with customers and internal leadership regarding program status, risks, and solutions.

  • Oversee project team task assignments and monitor performance.

  • Conduct cost analysis and process optimization initiatives to improve efficiency and profitability.

  • Prepare documentation, reporting, and communications for both internal leadership and external customers.

  • Manage customer expectations and ensure successful delivery of project milestones.

Qualifications

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or a STEM-related discipline required.

  • Master’s degree in Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Management, or related STEM field preferred.

Experience

  • Minimum 3+ years of experience in project or program management.

  • Experience managing contracts and customer-driven programs.

  • Background working in fast-paced, complex environments with tight deadlines.

Skills

  • Strong understanding of manufacturing and production processes.

  • Experience with continuous improvement and operational efficiency initiatives.

  • Financial analysis and cost control experience.

  • Proficiency with project management tools (MS Project or similar).

  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.

  • Strong leadership, planning, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.

  • Excellent communication, organizational, and problem-solving abilities.

  • Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities.

Work Environment

  • Combination of office and manufacturing floor environment.

  • Required PPE includes safety glasses and safety shoes when in production areas.

  • Ability to lift up to 15 pounds.

Compensation & Benefits

  • Salary: $125,000 – $135,000

  • Comprehensive benefits package

  • Opportunity to work with a collaborative engineering and manufacturing team

  • Career growth within a dynamic technical organization

job.title: MoldMaker
job.description:
Mold Maker – Plastics Manufacturing
Location: Thomaston Connecticut
Full-Time | Competitive Pay + Benefits
43.00-46.00 per hour
1st shift- 6am – 2pm or 7am-3pm

A growing global manufacturer serving leading brands in consumer products, healthcare, and personal care is seeking an experienced Toolmaker / Mold Maker to join its production team. This position plays a critical role in maintaining the plastic injection molds and tooling that support high-volume manufacturing operations.

This is an excellent opportunity for a skilled trades professional looking to work in a stable, technologically advanced manufacturing environment with opportunities to further develop their craft.

Key Responsibilities

  • Maintain and troubleshoot plastic injection molds used in production.

  • Diagnose and resolve tooling issues affecting assembly equipment and molds.

  • Set up and operate tool room equipment including lathes, milling machines, and grinders.

  • Read and interpret blueprints and engineering drawings to modify or fabricate tooling.

  • Perform precision work on tight-tolerance components and miniature parts.

  • Create tooling features such as angles, radii, and complex geometries.

  • Support ongoing production needs and unscheduled tooling activities.

  • Mentor apprentices or junior technicians when needed.

  • Ensure tooling meets quality standards and production requirements.

Qualifications

  • High school diploma or GED required.

  • Journeyperson Toolmaker certification strongly preferred.

  • 3–5+ years of experience in a plastics or injection molding manufacturing environment.

  • Strong mechanical aptitude and problem-solving ability.

  • Ability to read and interpret engineering drawings and specifications.

  • Experience working with injection mold tooling and maintenance.

  • Strong mathematical and measurement skills.

Preferred Experience

  • Knowledge of plastic resins such as polypropylene and polyethylene.

  • Familiarity with injection molding processes and tooling repair.

  • Experience working with precision measurement tools and tight tolerances.

Physical Requirements

  • Ability to lift up to 50 lbs.

  • Comfortable working in a manufacturing environment using required PPE.

  • Ability to stand, walk, climb, and perform physical tasks associated with tool maintenance.

What’s Offered

  • Competitive compensation

  • Medical, dental, and vision benefits

  • 401(k) plan

  • Paid vacation and holidays

  • Overtime opportunities

  • Shift differentials for off-shifts

  • Stable global organization with strong growth

#toponehire

job.title: Shipping & Receiving Clerk
job.description:
We are seeking a Shipper/Receiver to support daily warehouse operations including receiving, storing, and shipping a variety of manufacturing materials, parts, and supplies. This role follows established procedures to ensure materials are accurately handled and documented.

Shipper / Receiver – 1st Shift
Pay: $23.52/hour
Schedule: Monday–Friday | 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM

Essential Duties and Responsibilities

  • Receive incoming raw, semi-finished, and finished materials from carriers.
  • Inspect shipments for damage and verify items against packing slips and purchase orders, including count, weight, and specifications.
  • Report shortages, damages, or discrepancies to supervision.
  • Locate and accumulate required materials, including wire and cable, to fulfill customer orders or internal requests.
  • Rewind finished cables and wires as needed to meet specified lengths.
  • Wrap, pack, palletize, strap, and otherwise prepare materials for shipment following company procedures and customer instructions.
  • Apply stencils, labels, tags, and other identification markings to materials and packaging.
  • Pre-pack standard wires and cables and store inventory in designated locations.
  • Move materials to inspection areas, departments, or storage locations using forklifts or other material handling equipment.
  • Issue materials from storage based on approved requisitions and transport them to the appropriate departments.
  • Maintain shipping and receiving documentation, records, and routine reports.
  • Prepare paperwork for returned or rejected materials and coordinate carrier pickups.
  • Accumulate returnable reels and other materials for return to vendors.
  • Report damaged materials, equipment issues, or unusual conditions to supervision.
  • Maintain a clean, organized work area and follow all environmental, health, and safety regulations.

Qualifications / Requirements

  • Must be 18 years of age or older
  • Ability to operate a forklift and other material handling equipment
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to follow written and verbal instructions
  • Ability to lift and move materials in a warehouse environment

job.title: AP Accountant
job.description:
Position Summary
The Accounts Payable Specialist is responsible for managing the company’s full-cycle accounts payable process, ensuring timely and accurate processing of vendor invoices and payments. This role supports the manufacturing operation by maintaining strong vendor relationships, ensuring compliance with company policies, and contributing to accurate financial reporting.
Key Responsibilities
Invoice Processing

  • Process high-volume vendor invoices related to manufacturing operations (raw materials, components, freight, utilities, and services).
  • Perform three-way matching of purchase orders, receipts, and invoices.
  • Verify accuracy of pricing, quantities, and approvals before posting invoices.
  • Resolve discrepancies with Purchasing, Receiving, and vendors.

Vendor Management

  • Maintain vendor records and ensure proper documentation (W-9s, payment terms, tax information).
  • Respond to vendor inquiries and resolve payment issues.
  • Reconcile vendor statements and investigate outstanding balances.

Payment Processing

  • Prepare weekly payment runs (ACH, checks, wire transfers).
  • Ensure payments are made according to agreed terms and company cash flow policies.
  • Process employee expense reimbursements when applicable.

Month-End Close Support

  • Assist with month-end closing activities related to AP.
  • Accrue expenses for received goods not yet invoiced.
  • Reconcile AP subledger to the general ledger.

Internal Controls & Compliance

  • Ensure compliance with internal accounting controls and company policies.
  • Assist with audits by providing requested documentation and explanations.
  • Maintain organized records of invoices, approvals, and payment confirmations.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Work closely with Purchasing, Production, and Receiving departments to ensure accurate invoice matching and timely payment.
  • Support process improvements in procurement-to-pay workflows.

Qualifications
Education

  • Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field preferred.

Experience

  • 2+ years of accounts payable experience, preferably in a manufacturing or inventory-based environment.
  • Experience with ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Microsoft Dynamics, or similar).

Skills

  • Strong understanding of three-way matching and PO-based invoicing.
  • High attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
  • Ability to manage high invoice volumes and meet deadlines.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting systems.
  • Strong communication and problem-solving skills.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with manufacturing purchasing workflows and inventory systems.
  • Knowledge of sales/use tax and 1099 reporting.
  • Experience with automated AP systems or invoice scanning tools.

The Leasing Agent will play a key role in maintaining occupancy while ensuring full compliance with LIHTC regulations, HDC guidelines, and Fair Housing laws.  This is a great opportunity to work with a respected property management team in a growing affordable housing portfolio

  • Coordinate move-ins and ensure proper completion of lease documentation

  • Experience with HDC housing lotteries / Housing Connect required

job.title: Quality Control Inspector
job.description:
Desired Skills and Experience:

  • Minimum of 2 years’ experience working as a quality inspector
  • Ability to learn and work with specialized inspection equipment (i.e.CMM, comparator, etc.
  • Proficient working with inspection tools: microscope, magnifier.
  • Able to work with effectively little or no direct supervision.
  • Ability to understand and execute First Article Inspections to meet requirements.
  • Excellent communication and writing skills.
  • Meticulous record-keeping and tremendous attention to detail
  • Knowledge of ISO compliance & certification procedures and processes.
  • Ability to work in a team environment
  • Establish and maintain a safe work environment following OSHA regulations.

 
Education and Experience:

  • High School Graduate or General Education Degree (GED): Required
  • A minimum of 3+  years’ experience in a manufacturing environment.
  • Excellent communication skills, the ability to stay organized, and multi-task.

Essential Duties

  • Evaluate and report Quality Control inspection and test results.
  • Analyze and interpret blueprints, data, manuals, and other materials to determine specifications, inspection and testing procedures, adjustment and certification methods, formulas, and measuring instruments required.
  • Inspect, test, or measure materials, products, and work for conformance to specifications.
  • Notify supervisors and other personnel of production problems and assist in identifying and correcting these problems.
  • Discuss inspection results with those responsible for products and recommend necessary corrective actions.
  • Record inspection or test data, such as weights, temperatures, grades, or moisture content, and quantities inspected or graded.
  • Observe and monitor production operations and equipment to ensure conformance to specifications and make or order necessary process or assembly adjustments.
  • Measure dimensions of products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring instruments such as rulers, calipers, gauges, or micrometers.
  • Collect or select samples for testing.
  • Check arriving materials to ensure that they match purchase orders and submit discrepancy reports when problems are found.
  • Read dials and meters to verify that equipment is functioning at specified levels.
  • Clean, maintain, repair, and calibrate measuring instruments and test equipment such as dial indicators, fixed gauges, and height gauges.

job.title: Quality Manager Littleton, MA – $140-150K + bonus
job.description:
Quality Manager
Littleton, MA – $140-150K + bonus

Position Summary
We are seeking a senior, hands-on Quality Manager to lead all aspects of manufacturing quality for our high-complexity medical imaging and therapeutic systems. Reporting directly to the Director of QA/RA, you will serve as the primary owner of manufacturing quality affairs, responsible for day-to-day execution on the production floor, driving robust quality systems, and leading continuous improvement initiatives.
The successful candidate will work cross-functionally with Manufacturing, Engineering, and Supply Chain leadership. You must be comfortable making independent quality decisions while aligning with the broader corporate Quality and Regulatory strategy. This role manages the QC Supervisor and Quality Engineers and is expected to be a visible leader capable of identifying risk, driving root cause analysis, and implementing sustainable corrective actions.
Essential Functions

  • Quality Leadership: Own and lead manufacturing-related quality activities across all production operations, ensuring consistent application of standards from in-process inspection to final release.
  • Decision Authority: Provide strong quality decision-making regarding product acceptance, deviations, and risk management.
  • Operational Oversight: Direct oversight of Quality Control (QC) activities, including inspection strategy, sampling plans, and disposition processes.
  • Process Governance: Own the Nonconformance Report (NCR) and MRB processes; ensure trends are analyzed and fed into CAPA and continuous improvement efforts.
  • Continuous Improvement: Lead manufacturing process improvement initiatives (Lean, Six Sigma) focused on quality, efficiency, and yield.
  • Team Management: Manage and develop a team of Quality Engineers and QC staff, acting as a mentor to elevate the team’s technical capabilities.
  • Compliance: Ensure all activities comply with FDA QMSR, ISO 13485, and applicable international regulations.
  • Audit Support: Partner with leadership to support internal and external audits, including FDA and notified body inspections.

Required Education & Experience

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, or related technical field).
  • Experience: 8+ years of progressive Quality experience specifically within medical device manufacturing.
  • Leadership: 3+ years of experience managing Quality Engineers and/or QC teams.
  • Technical Expertise: Deep experience owning NCR/MRB processes and shop-floor quality execution.
  • Regulatory Knowledge: Strong working knowledge of ISO 13485 and risk-based quality principles.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with low-volume, high-complexity medical capital equipment (e.g., Radiation Therapy, X-Ray, CT, MRI, or similar large-scale systems).
  • Lean Six Sigma certification.
  • Experience driving cultural change and “quality-first” mindsets in a fast-paced environment.

#toponehire

job.title: Associate Director (LMHC, LICSW, LMFT)
job.description:
Associate Director of Community Programs (LMHC, LICSW, or LMFT)
Full-Time | Community Behavioral Health | Field-Based

Salary: $90,000 – $100,000

A behavioral health organization is seeking an Associate Director of Community Programs to provide leadership, clinical supervision, and operational support for community-based behavioral health and substance use programs. This role supports multidisciplinary teams delivering services across community settings, including partnerships with justice system agencies and other community organizations.

The Associate Director will work closely with program leadership to ensure high-quality care delivery, regulatory compliance, and effective program operations. This position focuses primarily on clinical supervision, staff leadership, and program oversight, rather than maintaining a regular clinical caseload.

This role is community-based and involves travel to program locations, partner agencies, and other service sites within the region.

Schedule
Monday – Friday, approximately 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM
No on-call responsibilities.

Responsibilities

  • Provide clinical supervision and leadership to community-based behavioral health staff, care coordinators, and program team members

  • Support programs serving individuals with mental health and substance use treatment needs, including justice-involved populations

  • Supervise clinicians and program staff to ensure high-quality service delivery and adherence to clinical standards

  • Review clinical documentation and program processes to ensure quality, accuracy, and compliance

  • Assist with program operations and staff management across multiple community program sites

  • Identify operational challenges and implement process improvements to enhance service delivery

  • Ensure staff compliance with organizational policies, training requirements, and supervision standards

  • Collaborate with community partners including correctional facilities, probation, parole, and other referral sources

  • Provide ongoing mentorship and professional development support to clinical staff and program teams

  • Lead quality improvement initiatives to support positive outcomes for clients and communities served

  • Assist in developing program strategies, goals, and operational priorities

  • Monitor program performance to ensure compliance with regulatory and contractual requirements

  • Participate in community outreach efforts to strengthen referral networks and partnerships

  • Collaborate with care teams to determine appropriate levels of care and support coordination of services

  • Support accreditation and regulatory compliance initiatives

Qualifications

Education

  • Master’s degree in Social Work, Counseling, or a related behavioral health field

Licensure

  • Active LMHC, LICSW, or LMFT required

Experience

  • Minimum 2 years of supervisory or management experience

  • Minimum 2 years of experience in behavioral health and/or substance use disorder treatment

  • Experience in community-based behavioral health services, care management, or social work preferred

  • Experience working with justice-involved populations, community outreach, or interdisciplinary care teams preferred

Skills

  • Strong leadership and staff supervision abilities

  • Knowledge of behavioral health treatment planning and care coordination

  • Ability to manage program operations and support multidisciplinary teams

  • Excellent organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills

Benefits

  • Medical, dental, and vision insurance

  • Flexible spending accounts

  • Generous paid time off including vacation, sick, and holidays

  • Retirement savings plan with employer match

  • Life and supplemental life insurance

  • Employee Assistance Program

  • Tuition assistance

  • Financial wellness resources

  • Optional supplemental benefits including accident and critical illness coverage

  • Pet insurance

job.title: Business Development Manager
job.description:
Job Opportunity: Business Development Manager – Litigation

A leading  law firm seeks a Business Development Manager to lead growth initiatives for its Litigation practice.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Develop business plans, market analyses, and competitive insights to drive growth.
  • Collaborate with practice leaders to execute strategic business development plans.
  • Identify cross-selling opportunities and manage high-profile RFPs and marketing efforts.
  • Recommend improvements to business development processes.
  • Oversee litigation-related profile-raising events, content development, and lateral integration.

job.title: Healthcare Case Manager
job.description:
Healthcare Case Manager

  • ~$50-60k (paid hourly) 
  • Full time 9 am – 5:30 pm, 30 min unpaid lunch. Should be mostly M-F but should have some flexibility for evenings/weekends
  • Will be expected to manage a caseload of 100-250 cases

Location: Queens, NYC ONSITE 
FLSA: Non-Exempt

Position Summary:
The Healthcare Case Manager ensures quality, cost-effective care for patients by providing case management services to children, families, and adults. This role coordinates care with patients’ support systems and a multidisciplinary care team to ensure services meet individual patient needs.

Responsibilities:

Client Services and Direct Care

  • Conduct intake, comprehensive assessments/reassessments, and develop individualized care plans.

  • Coordinate with a multidisciplinary care team and patient support networks to monitor progress and promote positive health outcomes.

  • Build trusting relationships with patients and families and manage emotional distress during crisis situations.

  • Provide clinic-based health education alongside regular care visits.

  • Obtain prior authorization for pharmacy or medical services as needed.

  • Represent services to recruit patients from underserved populations.

Collaboration and Services

  • Work collaboratively with patients, families, and external service providers to ensure proper care and follow-up.

  • Identify and link patients to additional services (e.g., specialty care, entitlements, mental health, substance use treatment, community follow-up, assistance programs).

  • Respond promptly to inquiries via phone, email, or referrals.

  • Participate in continuous quality improvement (CQI) initiatives and internal/external trainings.

  • Communicate about missed or rescheduled appointments and any special assistance needs (e.g., interpreter, transportation) to ensure continuity of care.

Compliance

  • Maintain accurate, organized, and timely patient records and reports.

  • Ensure all documentation meets regulatory and internal requirements.

  • Adhere to policies and procedures, including local, state, and federal requirements.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in health, nursing, mental health, child and family studies, public health, or related human services field with 2 years of relevant experience (or bachelor’s in non-related field with 4 years relevant experience).

  • Experience in a primary care model with target populations preferred (children/families, serious mental illness, developmental disabilities, substance use disorders, LGBTQ+, HIV/AIDS, chronic hepatitis, immigrants, and/or underserved communities).

  • UAS-NY certification required within 1 month of hire.

  • Fluency in Spanish or an Asian language preferred.

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.

  • Ability to work collaboratively and independently.

  • Proficiency in software applications (e.g., Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, EMR systems).

  • Strong customer service skills and ability to resolve patient/provider concerns.

  • Flexibility to work some evenings and weekends.

  • Ability to multitask and manage multiple projects in a high-stress environment.

job.description:

An established community health organization in Manhattan is seeking a Family Nurse Practitioner (FNP) to join their outpatient primary care team. This role focuses on providing comprehensive, culturally competent care to diverse patient populations, including women’s health, the LGBTQIA+ community, and individuals living with or affected by HIV/AIDS. The ideal candidate is passionate about health equity, has experience working with underserved or vulnerable populations, and thrives in a collaborative, mission-driven environment. Please note: PA-C candidates will not be considered for this position.

  • Evaluate and manage acute and chronic medical conditions
  • Perform initial assessment and management of mental health conditions (e.g., depression, anxiety)
  • Order, interpret, and act on diagnostic tests using evidence-based practices
  • Prescribe appropriate treatments and medications within the NP scope of practice

  • Certification as a Family Nurse Practitioner or Adult Nurse Practitioner
  • Current and valid NYS NP license and DEA certification
  • Minimum of 2 years’ hands-on experience as an NP
  • Current BLS and infection control certification
  • Excellent bedside manner and communication skills
  • Proficiency in Spanish or an Asian language preferred
  • AAHIVM and/or LGBT health certification a plus

job.description:
Behavioral Health Clinician
$90-93k salary 
Location: Manhattan, NY
Schedule: Full-time | 9:00 a.m. – 6:00 p.m., some evenings or weekends required
Reports To: Senior Director of Support Services

About the Role

We’re seeking a compassionate and skilled Behavioral Health Clinician to provide mental health services to patients and families across all age groups in a primary care setting. This role involves conducting assessments, developing treatment plans, and providing counseling, psychotherapy, and crisis intervention. The ideal candidate is collaborative, empathetic, and comfortable working in a diverse and fast-paced environment.

What You’ll Do

  • Conduct psychosocial assessments and assign DSM-5 and ICD-10 diagnoses.

  • Develop and implement individualized treatment plans.

  • Provide psychotherapy and psychoeducation to patients through individual, family, or group sessions.

  • Facilitate appropriate referrals and coordinate care while maintaining patient confidentiality.

  • Deliver crisis intervention and safety planning for patients at risk.

  • Collaborate with primary care providers and participate in interdisciplinary case reviews.

  • Maintain timely, accurate documentation in the electronic medical record (EMR).

  • Contribute to quality improvement initiatives and policy development.

  • Stay within the scope of your license and uphold current standards of care.

What We’re Looking For

  • Licensure: Active New York State license required (LCSW, LMHC, or LMFT).

  • Education: Advanced degree in mental health counseling, social work, or related field.

  • Experience:

  • Working with individuals with serious mental illness, substance use disorders, developmental disabilities, or chronic health conditions.

  • Experience supporting diverse populations, including LGBTQ+, immigrant, and multilingual communities, is strongly preferred.

  • Skills:

  • Strong clinical judgment and organizational skills.

  • Excellent written and verbal communication.

  • Compassionate, patient-centered approach with the ability to build rapport quickly.

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office and electronic health records (e.g., eClinicalWorks preferred).

  • Languages: Fluency in Spanish or an Asian language is a plus.

  • Availability: Ability to work flexible hours, including some evenings and weekends.

    Why Join

    • Work with a multidisciplinary team dedicated to providing holistic, high-quality care.

    • Be part of a mission-driven environment that values diversity, inclusion, and collaboration.

    • Opportunity to make a direct impact on the behavioral health and wellbeing of underserved communities.

job.title: Tool Design Manufacturing Engineer
job.description:
The Tool Design Manufacturing Engineer (TDME) is responsible for designing, developing, and improving tooling, fixtures, jigs, and dies used in metal stamping/progressive / compound die and other manufacturing machining operations (welding, work holding for tool room CNCs, and press brakes). This role works closely with production, quality, and tool room personnel to ensure tooling meets performance, safety, cost, and delivery requirements while supporting continuous improvement initiatives. The TDME interacts with the costing engineer by providing tool layouts and designs needed for the quoting process of new parts. Additionally, there is strong collaboration and team work with the rest of the engineering department where tool designs are reviewed and assistance is problem solving is obtained.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities

  • Designs tooling to produce parts using CAD (Solid Works, Logo Press &/or CADKEY) to customer specifications under instruction to ascertain specifications and objectives, nature of technical problem, and possible solutions such as part redesign, substitution of material or parts, or rearrangement of parts and subassemblies.
  • Collaborates with Tool Room, Maintenance, & Production to implement improvements for employee safety, production quality, and overall process effectiveness utilizing continuous improvement techniques.
  • Drafts detail drawings for fabrication by Tool Room.
  • Develop purchasing specifications for component parts.
  • Requisitions tools, equipment, and supplies.
  • Participation in internal and customer product and print design reviews
  • Tool design for sheet metal punching, forming and fabricating
  • Supports Tool Room & Production to troubleshoot tooling issues.
  • Identifies process improvement opportunities.

Support new product introductions by designing and validating required tooling.

  • Oversee tooling modifications, repairs, and improvements to enhance performance and tool life.
  • Work with tool room machinists to ensure designs are manufacturable and meet shop capabilities.
  • Conduct design reviews and ensure compliance with engineering standards and safety requirements.
  • Support continuous improvement, cost reduction, and cycle time optimization initiatives.
  • Maintain accurate documentation, revision control, and tooling records.

? Applies technical knowledge to analyze, investigate and resolve assigned engineering problems.
? Design and implement manufacturing processes including production flow, machine operation methods and production equipment to support operators and drive process improvement.
? Create and modify work instructions as necessary to document processes critical to operations and quality establishing appropriate process controls.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Manufacturing Engineering, or related field (or equivalent experience).
  • 3+ years of experience in tooling design within a manufacturing environment.
  • Strong proficiency in CAD software (e.g., SolidWorks, AutoCAD, or similar).
  • Experience with fixtures, jigs, dies, and production tooling.
  • Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
  • Ability to work cross-functionally and communicate effectively with shop floor personnel.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in a precision machining or metal fabrication environment.
  • Familiarity with SPC, lean manufacturing, and continuous improvement methodologies.
  • Experience supporting ISO or quality management systems.
  • Hands-on tool room or machining background.

#zip

job.title: Estimator
job.description:
Mechanical / HVAC Estimator

We are looking for an experienced Mechanical Estimator to support commercial HVAC construction projects. This role will focus primarily on mechanical systems including HVAC equipment, ductwork, piping, and related infrastructure. The estimator will be responsible for reviewing project documents, preparing accurate cost estimates, coordinating with vendors and subcontractors, and helping ensure projects are set up for success before construction begins.

This is a great opportunity for someone with mechanical estimating experience who enjoys working closely with project teams and contributing to the early stages of commercial construction projects.

Responsibilities

• Review project plans, specifications, and drawings to understand HVAC and mechanical system scope.
• Prepare detailed estimates for HVAC systems including ductwork, mechanical equipment, piping, and associated materials.
• Perform material and labor takeoffs using estimating software such as FastDuct, FastPipe, or similar tools.
• Solicit and evaluate pricing from HVAC equipment vendors, suppliers, and subcontractors.
• Attend site walkthroughs and pre-bid meetings when necessary to better understand project requirements.
• Communicate with engineers, contractors, and internal teams to clarify scope and resolve questions during the bidding process.
• Work with Project Managers during turnover meetings to ensure a smooth transition from estimating to project execution.
• Review vendor and subcontractor proposals to ensure alignment with project specifications.
• Identify potential cost savings or value engineering opportunities when appropriate.
• Build and maintain strong working relationships with vendors, subcontractors, and customers.

Requirements

• 5+ years of estimating experience in commercial mechanical or HVAC construction.
• Strong understanding of HVAC systems, ductwork, mechanical equipment, and related construction practices.
• Experience using estimating software such as FastPIPE FastDuct, or similar.
• Ability to read and interpret mechanical drawings and construction documents.
• Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple bids at once.
• Comfortable communicating with vendors, subcontractors, and internal project teams.
• Proficiency with Microsoft Office, particularly Excel.

Preferred but not required
– Bachelors Degree 

Key Responsibilities:

  • Build and maintain strong relationships with customers, vendors, and internal teams through effective communication and support, earning customer trust and identifying bidding opportunities.
  • Thoroughly review customer proposals to understand project scope and ensure accurate execution, including interpreting blueprints, drawings, and specifications.
  • Clearly communicate questions or concerns to customers regarding proposals to ensure clarity and assess potential risks.
  • Attend bid walkthroughs and proposal meetings to stay informed about project details.
  • Collaborate with Project Managers (PMs) to transfer labor, equipment, and material requirements during turnover meetings.
  • Utilize software tools (FastPipe, FastDuct) to determine material takeoffs for projects.
  • Identify and engage the appropriate vendors and subcontractors to bid on projects, ensuring competitive bidding.
  • Review vendor and subcontractor drawings/submittals to ensure alignment with project specifications.

Experience:

  • At least 5 years of experience in commercial estimating, with a preference for experience in the HVAC, Plumbing, or Refrigeration industries.
  • Proficient with FastPipe, FastDuct, and/or other relevant software tools.

Skills and Abilities:

  • Exceptional verbal and written communication skills.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite, with advanced skills in Excel.
  • Strong critical thinking and problem-solving abilities, with a keen attention to detail.
  • Excellent organizational and negotiation skills.

Education and Qualifications:

  • A Bachelor’s Degree in Business or Mechanical Engineering is a plus.
