job.title: Business Development Associate
job.description:
Business Development Associate (Inside Sales) 
Employment Type: Fulltime 
Compensation: Competitive base salary + performance-based bonus

About Us
 We serve businesses throughout Connecticut with a strong focus on security, compliance, and customer relationships.
As we continue to grow, we’re building a focused inside sales function to unlock additional value within our existing customer base and local market–without the heavy travel demands of traditional outside sales.
Rewards & Perks

  • Competitive base salary

  • Performance-based commission plan of (up to $1,500 per quarter)

  • Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, and Life Insurance coverage

  • Paid time off and holidays

  • 401K Retirement Benefit Plan with a company match

  • Ongoing training and development

About the Role
This is a career building inside sales role for someone early in their sales journey who wants real ownership, not just call quotas.
As a Business Development Associate, you’ll work from our headquarters to identify and close our main product line of secure destruction services, together with cross-sell and expansion opportunities for our other service offerings.
You’ll combine research, outbound outreach, and consultative selling-using modern sales tools and a structured CRM process-to grow revenue within existing accounts and new prospects.
This role is ideal for a self-starter who enjoys learning the full sales cycle and wants exposure to B2B selling in a professional services environment.
What You’ll Bring
Required

  • 1-3 years of experience in inside sales, business development, account management, or a related role

  • Comfort with outbound but “warm” outreach (calls, emails, follow-ups)

  • Experience using CRM (specific platforms are teachable)

  • Strong communication, organization, follow-through and confidence

  • Coachability, resilience, and comfort hearing “no”

Nice to Have

  • Exposure to B2B services or recurring revenue businesses

  • Experience selling into operations, IT, or compliance teams

  • Familiarity with prospecting tools like Apollo, ZoomInfo, or similar.

Why This Role Is Different

  • Not a call center role – quality conversations over volume dialing

  • Real ownership – you help close deals, not just set meetings

  • Career growth – opportunity to grow into senior sales, account management, or outside sales

  • Worklife balance – inside sales model with very limited travel

What You’ll Do
Generate New Revenue Opportunities

  • Follow-up activities to help facilitate deal closure and expansion opportunities

  • Identify expansion opportunities within existing shredding customers and targeted prospects

  • Conduct outbound (but warm) calls and emails each week to introduce ITAD, storage, and scanning solutions

  • Qualify customer needs and propose practical, compliant solutions

Run a Clean, Measurable Sales Process

  • Track all activity, conversations, and deal stages in Insightly CRM

  • Maintain accurate pipeline data for forecasting and reporting

  • Move opportunities forward with clear next steps and follow-ups

Collaborate & Deliver for Customers

  • Partner closely with Customer Service and Operations to ensure smooth handoffs

  • Participate in occasional local site visits or customer maintenance meetings

  • Deliver professional proposals and solution summaries

How Success Is Measured

  • Ability to create viable opportunities from warm leads

  • Creating cross-sell opportunities from outreach to existing customers

  • Regular follow-through to maximize opportunity close rate

  • Revenue growth from warm leads and cross-selling opportunities

 #zip

job.title: Accountant
job.description:
Position Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented Staff Accountant with experience in a manufacturing environment and strong ERP system skills. This role will support day-to-day accounting operations, assist with cost and inventory accounting, and ensure accurate financial reporting. The ideal candidate is hands-on, systems-savvy, and comfortable working cross-functionally with operations and supply chain teams.

Key Responsibilities

General Accounting

  • Prepare and post journal entries (accruals, prepaids, allocations)

  • Perform monthly bank, account, and balance sheet reconciliations

  • Assist with month-end and year-end close processes

  • Maintain the general ledger and ensure data accuracy

  • Support internal and external audit requests

Manufacturing & Cost Accounting Support

  • Assist with inventory accounting, including raw materials, WIP, and finished goods

  • Support standard costing updates and variance analysis

  • Reconcile inventory sub-ledger to the general ledger

  • Participate in physical inventory counts and cycle counts

  • Analyze production and material usage reports

ERP Systems & Reporting

  • Utilize ERP systems (such as Sage, SAP ERP, Oracle NetSuite, Microsoft Dynamics 365, or Infor CloudSuite) to process transactions and generate reports

  • Maintain data integrity within ERP financial and inventory modules

  • Assist in ERP system improvements, testing, and upgrades

  • Develop and maintain recurring financial and operational reports

  • Troubleshoot ERP-related discrepancies and support end users

Accounts Payable & Receivable Support

  • Review and reconcile vendor and customer accounts

  • Support invoice processing, payment runs, and cash application

  • Monitor aging reports and follow up on outstanding balances

Qualifications

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field

Experience

  • 2–4 years of accounting experience, preferably in a manufacturing environment

  • Exposure to cost accounting and inventory management preferred

  • Hands-on experience with ERP systems required

Technical Skills

  • Strong ERP proficiency, particularly in financial and inventory modules

  • Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (pivot tables, lookups, basic formulas)

  • Experience with reporting tools or data analysis software is a plus

Core Competencies

  • Strong attention to detail and accuracy

  • Analytical and problem-solving mindset

  • Ability to manage deadlines in a fast-paced manufacturing setting

  • Effective communication and teamwork skills

  • Proactive and process-improvement oriented

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience participating in ERP implementations or system conversions

  • Understanding of standard costing methodologies

  • CPA eligibility or pursuit of professional certification is a plus

job.title: Data Engineer
job.description:
Summary

The Data Engineer will help build and maintain the data pipelines, models, and infrastructure that power analytics, business-intelligence, and machine-learning initiatives across the company. You’ll work alongside senior engineers and cross-functional partners to turn raw data into reliable, well-documented datasets that drive informed decisions and innovative products. This role reports to the Senior Manager, Data Engineering.

Duties and Responsibilities

  • Build E2E Azure Databricks-based data solutions.
  • Design, develop, and maintain scalable ETL and streaming data pipelines on Azure Databricks, leveraging Apache Spark, Delta Lake, and Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS Gen2) to enable reliable lakehouse architectures and ensure efficient ingestion, transformation, and storage of data
  • Build and optimize data models and schemas for analytics, reporting, and operational data stores
  • Build and optimize Delta Lake / Lakehouse patterns (Bronze/Silver/Gold), including schema evolution and time travel
  • Develop high-quality PySpark / Spark SQL transformations, optimize joins, partitioning, caching, and shuffle behavior.
  • Implement and maintain data quality frameworks, including data validation, monitoring, and alerting mechanisms.
  • Collaborate closely with data architects, data analysts, BI engineers and product teams to align data engineering activities with business goals.
  • Contribute to CI/CD pipelines supported by version control, linting, automated testing, security scanning, and monitoring
  • Troubleshoot and resolve complex Azure Databricks platform data infrastructure and pipeline issues, ensuring minimal downtime and optimal performance.
  • Follow coding standards, participate in code reviews, and document your work to foster knowledge sharing.

Qualifications

Education and/or Experience: 

Required:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field
  • A minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience designing, building, and operating data solutions.
  • Knowledge of Databricks architecture and core components, including Databricks Lakehouse, Delta Lake, Databricks SQL, Apache Spark Clusters, Unity Catalog, Databricks Workflows (Jobs), and Databricks Notebooks
  • Proficiency with Python, SQL, and Apache Spark for data processing
  • Proven experience building reusable, metadata-driven data ingestion frameworks using Python and Scala
  • Familiarity with cloud data-platform components such as object storage, metadata / data catalog services, batch/streaming/CDC ingestion & processing
  • Proven experience with data modeling, schema design, and performance tuning of large-scale data systems
  • Experience working with AI & BI engineers to deliver high-quality data products to stakeholders
  • Skilled at crafting compelling data narratives through tables, reports, dashboards, and other visualization tools
  • Understanding of data engineering best practices: code repositories, CI/CD pipelines, test automation, monitoring, and alerting systems
  • Strong problem-solving and analytical skills with excellent attention to detail
  • Excellent communication skills and experience collaborating with technical and business stakeholders

Preferred:

  • Experience building data pipelines in an Azure Databricks environment

job.title: Senior Data Engineer
job.description:
Summary

The Senior Data Engineer will design, build, and maintain the scalable data pipelines, models, and infrastructure that power analytics, business intelligence, and machine?learning products across the company. Partnering closely with business, product, and analytics teams, you will translate complex requirements into elegant, reliable data solutions and help drive the delivery of innovative data products. This role reports to the Senior?Manager, Data Engineering.

Duties and Responsibilities

  • Build E2E Azure Databricks-based data solutions.
  • Design, develop, and maintain scalable ETL and streaming data pipelines on Azure Databricks, leveraging Apache Spark, Delta Lake, and Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS Gen2) to enable reliable lakehouse architectures and ensure efficient ingestion, transformation, and storage of data
  • Build and optimize data models and schemas for analytics, reporting, and operational data stores
  • Build and optimize Delta Lake / Lakehouse patterns (Bronze/Silver/Gold), including schema evolution and time travel
  • Develop high-quality PySpark / Spark SQL transformations, optimize joins, partitioning, caching, and shuffle behavior.
  • Implement and maintain data quality frameworks, including data validation, monitoring, and alerting mechanisms.
  • Collaborate closely with data architects, analysts, data scientists, and product teams to align data engineering activities with business goals.
  • Leverage cloud data platforms (Azure, AWS or GCP) to build and optimize data storage solutions, including data warehouses, data lakehouses, and real-time data processing.
  • Develop automation processes and frameworks for CI/CD supported by version control, linting, automated testing, security scanning, and monitoring
  • Contribute to the maintenance and improvement of data governance practices, helping to ensure data integrity, accessibility, and compliance with regulations such as GDPR.
  • Provide technical mentorship and guidance to junior team members, promoting best practices in software engineering, data engineering, and agile development.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve complex Azure Databricks platform data infrastructure and pipeline issues, ensuring minimal downtime and optimal performance.

Qualifications

Education and/or Experience: 

Required:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field
  • A minimum of 5 years of hands-on experience in data engineering, designing and building scalable data pipelines, ETL/ELT processes
  • A minimum of 5 years of hands-on experience designing, building, and operating data solutions 
  • Strong knowledge of Databricks architecture and core components, including Databricks Lakehouse, Delta Lake, Databricks SQL, Apache Spark Clusters, Unity Catalog, Databricks Workflows (Jobs), MLflow, and Databricks Notebooks
  • Extensive experience with cloud data platforms in Azure, AWS, or Google
  • Strong proficiency with Python, SQL, and Apache Spark for data processing
  • Proven experience building reusable, metadata-driven data ingestion frameworks using Python and Scala
  • Hands-on experience with modern data-platform components (object storage, Lakehouse engines, orchestration tools, columnar warehouses, streaming services).
  • Proven experience with data modeling, schema design, and performance tuning of large-scale data systems.
  • Deep understanding of data engineering best practices: code repositories, CI/CD pipelines, test automation, monitoring, and alerting systems.
  • Skilled at crafting compelling data narratives through tables, reports, dashboards, and other visualization tools
  • Strong problem-solving and analytical skills with excellent attention to detail.
  • Excellent communication skills and experience collaborating with technical and business stakeholders.

Preferred:

  • Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering
  • Experience building data pipelines in an Azure Databricks environment
  • Hands-on experience integrating Azure Databricks with Azure DevOps, Azure Blob Storage / ADLS Gen2, Azure Key Vault, and Azure Data Factory
  • Familiarity with enterprise data modeling tools such as ERwin Data Modeler, including the ability to interpret and apply logical and physical data models to analytical and lakehouse architectures
  • Experience migrating to—or building—data platforms from the ground up
  • Experience with Infrastructure as Code (IAC) and Governance as Code
  • Familiarity with machine-learning workloads and partnering on feature engineering
  • Experience working in an Agile delivery model

Other Skills and Abilities:

The following will also be required of the successful candidate:

  • Strong organizational skills
  • Strong attention to detail
  • Good judgment
  • Strong interpersonal communication skills
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Able to work harmoniously and effectively with others
  • Able to preserve confidentiality and exercise discretion
  • Able to work under pressure
  • Able to manage multiple projects with competing deadlines and priorities

job.title: Service Technician role
job.description:
Position Title: Service Technician
Department: Service
Reports To: Service Manager
Compensation Type: Salary
Position Type: Full-Time
Base compensation: $70,000

Position Summary

The Service Technician is a key member of the Service Department for this organization. This role is responsible for installation, maintenance, troubleshooting, evaluation, repair, technical support, and customer training related to imaging systems and associated equipment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide incoming technical support by troubleshooting issues and guiding customers as needed.

  • Maintain and update open service cases in a timely manner.

  • Support in-office service operations during non-travel periods, including customer support queue management.

  • Perform in-house evaluations and repairs of equipment.

  • Conduct on-site services including:

    • System installations

    • Preventative maintenance

    • Equipment evaluations and repairs

    • System relocations and upgrades

    • End-user training

  • Coordinate closely with the Service Coordinator to schedule service visits and communicate effectively with customers.

  • Maintain detailed and accurate service documentation in CRM systems, including reports, manuals, and communication logs.

  • Manage domestic and international travel logistics as required.

  • Develop written work instructions and technical documentation as needed.

  • Support factory repair and in-house service teams.

  • Collaborate with engineering and product development teams to provide field feedback, suggest improvements, and identify recurring issues.

  • Stay current with industry trends, X-ray technology advancements, and technical developments.

  • Perform additional duties as necessary to support company goals and objectives.

Qualifications & Education

Required Experience & Technical Skills:

  • Minimum of 2 years of experience servicing X-ray devices or similar high-tech equipment.

  • Experience working with high and low voltage electronics.

  • Strong understanding of mechanical assemblies.

  • Ability to read and interpret schematics and wiring diagrams.

  • Proven expertise in troubleshooting and repairing electronic and mechanical components.

  • Familiarity with X-ray technology and related systems.

  • Proficiency in software diagnostics and system integration.

  • Skilled in CRM, ERP, and general office software tools.

Professional Skills & Attributes:

  • Excellent verbal communication and customer interaction skills.

  • Strong interpersonal abilities, including conflict resolution.

  • Demonstrated creative and critical problem-solving capabilities.

  • Self-motivated with the ability to work independently.

  • Strong commitment to high-quality customer service.

  • Ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional and international teams.

  • Capable of meeting or exceeding team priorities and objectives.

Education:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Electronics, X-ray Imaging, or a related field preferred.

Additional Requirements

  • Ability to travel up to five days per week.

  • Willingness to work occasional weekends.

  • Ability to manage frequent domestic and international travel.

job.title: IAM Engineer
job.description:
Summary

The Identity & Access Management Engineer (IAM Engineer) is responsible for identifying, delivering and supporting the technology used to deliver the firm’s overall Identity & Access Management program, which is designed to ensure the Firm’s user identities, accounts, credentials and system access are fully and completely managed for all system users. The Engineer is responsible for the current technology in place (Okta Verify/SSO, Microsoft/Azure AD, Delinea, etc.) and ensuring successful operations, interoperability and general well-being. The incumbent will work proactively with IAM Manager and IAM Architect and  various IT and business departments to implement services that meet the firm’s current and future IAM needs.

The IAM Engineer is a proven technologist and a hands on problem solver, as well as an effective internal consultant, who will regularly advise others on access and security/risk related issues. The incumbent must possess domain competencies in a number of related  disciplines, including security, risk, access control, overseeing backend technology with Okta, ARS/AD/Azure (on premises and Cloud based) authentication, multi factor authentication and entitlements reviews.

Duties and Responsibilities

  • Work with the IAM Manager to build out and oversee the IAM function’s technical controls and its related activities including planning, solutioning, testing, reporting and delivering IAM services.
  • Oversee the implementation of all current solutions to ensure they are configured appropriately and are delivering maximum value for the Firm. Review current documentation such as Procedures, run books, and Knowledge Base Articles used by the Service Desk. Review and/or establish Best Practices where applicable
  • Engage and interact with other IT Departmental Engineers to ensure future efforts result in continued uninterrupted delivery of all IAM services.
  • Demonstrate extensive understanding of IAM concepts such as directory services, SSO, federation, MFA, provisioning, access certification, roles and SOD.
  • The analysis, design, implementation, and maintenance of all layers of IAM applications, including Authorization / Authentication and Account Creation / Management / Provisioning / Retirement in data repositories. Including; strategy, organizational design, process re-engineering and technology implementation.
  • Functional areas and work experience should include; fine-grained access control, policy driven security, Identity Governance, Access Management, and Privileged access management, user provisioning/de-provisioning, and federation.
  • Provide support with respect to requirements gathering, project management and delivery of one or more Identity platforms, such as SailPoint (Identity IQ), Okta, and Saviynt.
  • Serve as the central point of contact for information security and IAM policy and process related issues.
  • Address Vulnerabilities, Pentest findings and audit issues in a timely manner.
  • Participate in a 24x7x365 on-call rotation
  • Stay abreast of industry trends, solution landscape and market conditions and update peers and management accordingly.
  • Other duties, as assigned.

Qualifications

Education and/or Experience: 

Required:

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent combination of education and/or experience.
  • Minimum of 3 years of experience in a Technical IAM related role, ranging from technical tool evaluation/selection, implementation and operations. Cloud based utilization and cloud adoption a must.
  • Experience with Microsoft Active Directory (Azure AD) and GPO management, Windows Desktop and Server environments.
  • Experience with IT Security and Governance Risk and Compliance functions and their needs.
  • Working knowledge of IT management frameworks such as Control Objectives for Information and Related Technology (COBIT) and/or Information Technology Infrastructure Library (ITIL).
  • Experience developing, deploying, integrating and supporting IAM services within a larger IT organization showcasing strengths in teamwork and establishing successful partner ships.

Preferred:

  • Multiple years of Legal Industry or Financial Services experience
  • Experience initiating new technologies and delivery high level services
  • Experience interacting with senior/executive members and presenting material in a simple straight forward manner
  • Industry specific training or certification

Other Skills and Abilities:

The following will also be required of the successful candidate:

  • Strong organizational skills
  • Strong attention to detail
  • Good judgment
  • Strong interpersonal communication skills
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Able to work harmoniously and effectively with others
  • Able to preserve confidentiality and exercise discretion
  • Able to work under pressure
  • Able to manage multiple projects with competing deadlines and priorities

job.title: AI Change Management Analyst
job.description:
AI Change Management Analyst

The AI Change Management Analyst is responsible for assisting in the development and execution of a firm-wide change management and communications strategy for Artificial Intelligence (AI) adoption. This role will create and implement communication and change management plans tailored to AI initiatives, ensuring smooth adoption and meaningful transformation across the firm as well as oversee all internal AI-related communications and manage firm-wide forums and collaboration sites specific to AI. The Analyst will also help create and implement change management and communications plans for all IS projects, working with the project team.  Additionally, the Analyst will manage collaboration among internal Information Services (IS) teams and business professional groups when responding to AI- and technology-related questions in Requests for Proposal (RFPs), client surveys, and Outside Counsel Guidelines (OCGs), ensuring consistent, accurate, and timely submissions. Play a critical role in IS’s change management process by developing and executing change management and communication plans for AI-driven projects. Carefully assess the impact of AI initiatives on different audiences and support the implementation of a comprehensive firm-wide AI change management and communications strategy. Work with others on the change management team to improve the profile of the IS organization in the firm by educating and informing attorneys and business professionals about ongoing initiatives. Prepare clear, timely, and engaging communications designed to build confidence, reduce uncertainty, and drive adoption.
Ensure adherence to established change management and communication best practices, including stakeholder collaboration, audience analysis, and effective training and enablement strategies. Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of AI-related communications, providing actionable feedback and making adjustments to enhance clarity, minimize resistance, and accelerate adoption. Provide excellent client service by working closely with the Pitches and Pursuits group within Clients & Markets to shape responses to AI and technology-related questions in RFPs and client questionnaires. Partner with the OCG team to interpret client requirements and respond to AI-specific provisions within Outside Counsel Guidelines, ensuring the firm demonstrates thought leadership and compliance in the evolving AI landscape.

About The Role:

  • Assist the Change Management Manager in the development and implementation of a departmental-wide approach to change management, adhering to best practices.
  • Design and implement change management and communication plans for AI initiatives and major IS projects that significantly impact attorneys and business professionals across the firm.
  • Partner with subject matter experts to develop effective strategies and content for AI change management plans and communications.
  • Create a wide range of communication materials tailored to diverse audiences, leveraging multiple channels including Teams, the intranet, email, and in-person engagement.
  • Collaborate closely with the Communications and Marketing group to develop AI-focused communication materials and to assess the most effective communication vehicles for firmwide use.
  • Partner with business professionals across departments to support responses to AI- and technology-related inquiries in RFPs, client questionnaires, and Outside Counsel Guidelines (OCGs).
  • Identify and implement innovative approaches to enhance AI communications and elevate the visibility of AI initiatives across the firm.
  • Utilize existing firm software to establish processes and procedures that support AI communications and change management activities.
  • Manage day-to-day departmental communications related to AI and broader IS initiatives, ensuring timely and consistent distribution.
  • Draft, edit, and deliver change management plans and communication content, tailored to specific messaging objectives and target audiences.
  • Develop and maintain internal and external communication strategies that position the firm’s AI and IS initiatives consistently and effectively. Craft messaging for AI initiatives that includes internal and external versions designed to complement and reinforce one another.
  • Demonstrate professionalism, high-quality service, and a solutions-oriented mindset in all interactions with attorneys, business professionals, clients, and vendors.
  • Manage multiple assignments and priorities effectively to meet established deadlines.
  • Stay informed on emerging trends in AI, legal technology, and professional communications; actively participate in relevant industry organizations and forums.

Required Skills
What You Will Bring/Your Qualifications:

  • Outstanding written and verbal communication skills.
  • Knowledge and expertise in AI, change management strategies and best practices.
  • Ability to turn complex technical communications into meaningful content for both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Requires strong project management capabilities with a results-oriented, pragmatic approach to problem solving and quality delivery.
  • Possesses knowledge and expertise in IT service life-cycle management models and frameworks (e.g., ITIL) and process improvement frameworks (e.g., Six Sigma, TQM).
  • Possession knowledge of change management models (e.g. ADKAR)
  • Ability to work effectively with all levels of Information Services, the Communication and Marketing group, attorneys and staff.
  • Ability to manage multiple, competing deadlines.
  • Experience with Microsoft Office suite
  • Experience with Generative Artificial Intelligence tools
  • Strong attention to detail.
  • Exceptional interpersonal skills, including teamwork, facilitation and negotiation.

Required Experience
Education:

  • BA/BS required.

Experience:

  • 5+ years experience in change management/training and/or communications development and management required.
  • Prior IT experience preferred.
  • Proven ability to successfully run complex change program communications.

#toponehire

job.title: Financial Controller
job.description:
DESCRIPTION:
Financial Controller
Boston, MA

Overview
The Financial Controller is responsible for managing and overseeing all accounting and financial operations for a growing construction and remodeling company. This role ensures accurate financial reporting, strong job cost control, healthy cash flow, and profitability tracking across all projects.
The Controller works closely with ownership, project managers, and operations to provide financial insight that supports decision-making and protects company margins. This position goes beyond traditional bookkeeping and plays a key leadership role in managing project financial performance from contract award through project completion.

Responsibilities
Core Responsibilities
1. Job Profitability Control (MOST IMPORTANT)

  • Maintain accurate job cost structures
     

Monitor:

  • Estimated vs Actual costs
  • Labor productivity
  • Change order profitability
  • Produce monthly project profit reports
  • Flag problem jobs early
     

Goal:

  • No financial surprises at project close.

2. Cash Flow Management

  • Weekly cash position reporting
  • Forecast incoming vs outgoing cash (8–12 weeks ahead)
  • Control billing timing
  • Reduce AR aging
  • Manage retainage tracking
  • Construction companies fail from cash flow — not lack of work.
     

3. Project Billing Oversight

  • Progress billing & schedule of values
  • AIA billing when required
  • Change order billing tracking
  • Ensure PMs bill on time
     

4. Financial Reporting to Ownership
Deliver monthly dashboard including:

  • Gross profit by project
  • Company gross margin
  • Overhead ratio
  • Net profit
  • Backlog value
  • Cash forecast

5. Systems & Process Improvement

  • Standardize job cost codes
  • Improve PM financial accountability
  • Implement financial workflows
  • Integrate PM software with accounting
     

6. Compliance & Risk Protection

  • Subcontractor insurance tracking
  • Lien waiver management
  • Sales tax & 1099 reporting
  • Coordinate CPA & year-end closing
     

7. Leadership

  • Train PMs on financial responsibility
  • Attend weekly operations meetings
  • Increase profit margins
  • Prevent underbidding patterns
  • Improve billing speed
  • Give owner real financial visibility

Experience
Required Experience & Education — Financial Controller (Construction & Remodeling)

job.title: Property Manager – Greater Philadelphia Portfolio
job.description:
Property Manager – Greater Philadelphia Portfolio

Overview:
Join a premier, publicly traded real estate investment trust recognized as an industry leader in the ownership and redevelopment of high-quality retail assets. While we operate on a national scale with a multi-billion dollar portfolio, our internal culture remains nimble, innovative, and entrepreneurial. We value team members with high initiative who want to be involved in every facet of the business.
We are seeking an experienced Property Manager, to oversee operations for a portfolio of retail properties in the Greater Philadelphia region. This is a dynamic role for a professional who thrives in a team environment and wants to implement best practices to drive maximum productivity and value.
Responsibilities:

  • Operations Management: Direct day-to-day operational needs, including R&M, landscaping, waste, security, and janitorial services, ensuring all properties meet elite corporate standards.
  • Tenant Relations: Act as the primary point of contact for merchants; resolve lease and maintenance issues while gathering market intel to support regional strategy.
  • Financial Oversight: Develop and manage annual operating budgets, prepare monthly variance reports, and execute quarterly reforecasts. Manage all controllable expenses without compromising asset integrity.
  • Capital Projects: Determine specifications and lead the competitive bidding process for large-scale maintenance, capital improvements, and service agreements.
  • Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner with Leasing, Tenant Coordination, and Marketing teams to expedite landlord work, space delivery, and grand openings.
  • Collections & Reporting: Manage Accounts Receivable (collections) and ensure timely submission of activity, roof, and lighting inspection reports.

Qualifications:

  • Experience: Minimum of 5 years in retail shopping center management, specifically managing a multi-property portfolio.
  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field or equivalent professional experience.
  • Financial Acumen: Strong proficiency in budgeting, financial statement analysis, and lease interpretation.
  • Communication: Exceptional interpersonal and written communication skills with a proven ability to manage diverse stakeholders and service personnel.
  • Technical Skills: Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook); experience with property management software is a plus.
  • Flexibility: Ability to work beyond standard business hours as needed and perform comprehensive on-site inspections.

job.title: Sr. Process Engineer
job.description:
Job Title: Sr. Process Engineer – Assembly & Automation
Location: West Haven, CT
Department: Manufacturing / Operations
Job Type: Full-Time
 

Position Summary
We are seeking a highly motivated Process Engineer to lead and support our assembly operations with a strong focus on process optimization, automation, and Lean manufacturing. The ideal candidate will play a critical role in driving operational excellence, improving assembly line performance, and leading continuous improvement initiatives to enhance safety, quality, delivery, and cost.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and optimize assembly processes, ensuring alignment with quality, productivity, and cost targets.
  • Identify and implement automation opportunities (robotics, vision systems, material handling, etc.) to increase efficiency and reduce manual labor.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams including design, quality, maintenance, and operations to launch new products and processes into production.
  • Lead and facilitate continuous improvement initiatives using Lean tools such as Kaizen, 5S, Standard Work, SMED, Value Stream Mapping, and Root Cause Analysis.
  • Analyze and resolve process issues through structured problem-solving (e.g., DMAIC, 8D, PDCA).
  • Develop and maintain process documentation, including work instructions, process flow diagrams, PFMEAs, and control plans.
  • Monitor KPIs such as cycle time, OEE, scrap, and downtime; identify trends and take corrective actions.
  • Champion Lean culture within the organization by training, mentoring, and engaging team members at all levels.
  • Evaluate new equipment and technologies; develop business cases and support capital projects from concept through implementation.
  • Ensure compliance with safety, quality, and environmental standards in all process engineering activities.

Qualifications
Required:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical, Industrial, or Manufacturing Engineering or related field.
  • 5+ years of experience in process or manufacturing engineering, preferably in a high-volume assembly environment.
  • Proven experience in automation integration and Lean manufacturing principles.
  • Strong knowledge of assembly tooling, fixtures, and workstations.
  • Proficiency in using engineering tools such as AutoCAD/SolidWorks, Excel (advanced), and statistical analysis software (e.g., Minitab).
  • Solid understanding of manufacturing KPIs, Six Sigma basics, and process validation.
  • Strong project management, communication, and leadership skills.

Preferred:

  • Six Sigma Green or Black Belt certification.
  • Experience with PLC programming, robotics, or MES systems.
  • Experience working in cross-cultural or multi-site environments.

Work Environment

  • Majority of work performed in a plant/manufacturing floor environment with exposure to production machinery, noise, and moving equipment.
  • Occasional travel to suppliers or other company sites may be required.

job.title: Assistant Controller
job.description:
Responsibilities
 

  • Own and lead the group month-end close and financial consolidation processes across multiple legal entities and currencies.
  • Collaborate with other function leads, including Accounts Receivable and Trade, Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) and Inventory, as well as Marketing and General & Administrative (G&A) expenses, to resolve issues and improve the month-end timeline and quality.
  • Prepare timely and accurate group-level financial statements, including the Profit and Loss (P&L), Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow Statement.
  • Ensure accurate and compliant accounting treatment across all entities in accordance with IFRS, including intercompany eliminations and foreign exchange translation impacts.  – NICE TO HAVE

  • At least 7 years of experience in financial reporting and controllership, ideally within consumer goods sector

job.title: Senior Cloud Security Engineer
job.description:
Our AM Law 100 client is currently seeking a Senior Security Engineer to join the Architecture team in our Information Security department. The Senior Security Engineer is responsible for engineering and implementing solutions to enhance the Firm’s security infrastructure, collaborating with the Information Security team, internal IT, and business units. This role focuses on securing cloud-based environments through initiatives such as Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM), DevSecOps practices (e.g., automated security testing in CI/CD pipelines), Identity and Access Management (IAM/IDM) administration, and participation in Information Security Architecture reviews. Key responsibilities include implementing and maintaining robust security controls for technology infrastructure and cloud platforms, while ensuring secure integration of emerging technologies, including AI systems.

The role also serves as an escalation resource for the Information Security Operations team, providing advanced expertise and support. As part of a small team, you will provide security guidance for the technology environment, aiming to mature the security control framework, develop tailored solutions for cloud workloads, and enhance overall security posture. You will act as a security advocate, advising key stakeholders on technology risk management and balancing security with business needs through effective mitigation strategies, ensuring safe adoption of cloud and related technologies.

Responsibilities

  • Work in active partnership with key stakeholders to perform security architecture risk reviews.
  • Develop and implement advanced-level Cloud Security solutions.
  • Support and advance the overall Information Security technology roadmap.
  • Provide recommendations for advancing the Information Security program, security policies, and security control standards to enhance operational practices.
  • Create and maintain Information Security standards applicable to all technologies in the portfolio.
  • Provide requirement, guidance, and vision to the vendor community to cultivate the appropriate combination of technology and feature capabilities to meet current and future security requirements.
  • Execute on infrastructure threat and vulnerability management processes.
  • Ensure security systems are upgraded by monitoring security environment, identifying security gaps and evaluating and implementing enhancements.
  • Define, develop and maintain metrics and measurements for information security controls and processes.
  • Respond to security-related issues, problems, crises, and critical situations to support resolution and minimize downtime.
  • Act as a senior-level point of contact for incident investigations and minor security events (e.g., unauthorized access, non-compliance with Firm policies, fraud, service disruptions, etc.) to determine malfunctions, breaches, and remediation steps.
  • Responsible for Information Security technology selection process to include requirements consolidation into RFI/RFP/RFQ, testing, POC, selection and deployment.
  • Support audits against internal and industry process, quality, and security standards; drive initiatives and remediation efforts to correct non-conformance.
  • Provide Information Security consulting on security related issues.
  • Collaborate with peers to identify and implement improvement initiatives across the Firm, processes and toolsets.
  • Manage multiple, parallel projects using formal project planning techniques.
  • Ability to work evening and weekend hours as needed or directed. Some travel may be required.

Qualifications

  • High School Diploma or equivalent required; Associate’s Degree or Bachelor’s Degree preferably in Engineering, Information Technology, Computer Science, or similar strongly preferred
  • Minimum of five (5) years in a professional technical Information Technology role required
  • Minimum of three (3) years of hands-on experience in Information Security with Cloud Security exposure required
  • Specialized Cloud Security certification(s), CISSP, Microsoft Azure AZ-104 & AZ-500, CCSP, CCSK, or CPT/CEH desired
  • SailPoint Identity and Access Management (IAM/IDM) vendor-specific certification(s) desired 

#toponehire

job.title: Senior Buyer (Aerospace Manufacturing)
job.description:
Senior Buyer

Department: Operations
Reports To: Supply Chain Manager
Location: Farmington, Connecticut
Compensation: $100,000 (commensurate with experience)

Position Summary

We are seeking an experienced and highly analytical Senior Buyer to join our Operations team within a fast-paced aerospace manufacturing environment. This role is responsible for managing complex procurement activities, supporting new product introductions, developing supplier partnerships, and ensuring full compliance with aerospace regulatory requirements including EAR, ITAR, and DFARS.

The ideal candidate brings strong supply chain expertise, aerospace regulatory knowledge, and the ability to drive process improvements while supporting production readiness and operational excellence.

Key Responsibilities

  • Issue, manage, and track purchase orders for raw materials, outside special services (OSS), and production-related materials.

  • Develop and maintain strong supplier relationships while negotiating pricing, lead times, and terms.

  • Support New Product Introductions (NPI) through supplier selection, supply chain development, and readiness planning.

  • Assist in the development and documentation of processes, including customer approvals where required.

  • Conduct process capability evaluations and Manufacturing Readiness Level (MRL) assessments.

  • Plan and execute rate readiness strategies to support production scaling.

  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives within the supply chain.

  • Develop and implement business models for insourcing initiatives.

  • Procure capital equipment and machinery required for insourced special processes, ensuring proper approvals.

  • Maintain and manage an approved supplier list for customer-controlled and regulated processes.

  • Ensure compliance with aerospace regulatory frameworks including EAR, ITAR, DFARS, and customer-specific requirements.

  • Support audits and maintain documentation in alignment with quality standards.

Qualifications

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree (BS/BA) from an accredited college or university.

Experience

  • Minimum of 5–7 years of progressive supply chain or purchasing experience.

  • Aerospace manufacturing experience strongly preferred.

  • Demonstrated experience operating within regulated environments (EAR, ITAR, DFARS).

Skills & Competencies

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

  • Strong interpersonal and conflict resolution abilities.

  • Exceptional organizational skills with high attention to detail.

  • Advanced analytical and problem-solving skills.

  • Proven ability to manage and develop supply chain partnerships.

  • Strong understanding of aerospace regulatory requirements and quality systems.

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and ERP/MRP systems.

Training Requirements

  • Employee Orientation

  • Annual Safety Training

  • Annual Quality Procedures Training

  • Job-Specific Skills Training

  • Training as mandated by the State of Connecticut

Working Conditions

  • Work environment includes both office and manufacturing settings.

  • Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) required in manufacturing areas:

    • Safety glasses with side shields

    • Safety shoes

  • Maximum lift requirement: 15 lbs.

Why Join Us

This is a key role within a growing aerospace organization where the Senior Buyer will directly impact operational performance, supplier strategy, and production readiness. The position offers strong visibility within Operations and the opportunity to contribute to strategic supply chain initiatives.

 

#toponehire

job.title: Benefits Administer (Manufacturing Company)
job.description:
Benefits Administrator
Pawcatuck, CT
 

A well-established, global manufacturing organization is seeking a detail-oriented and service-driven Benefits Administrator to oversee employee benefits programs with accuracy, confidentiality, and care.

This is a high-visibility, in-office role reporting directly to the CHRO and responsible for managing health, welfare, retirement, and leave programs for a multi-site organization.

Position Summary

The Benefits Administrator will manage the day-to-day administration of employee benefit programs including medical, dental, disability, COBRA, 401(k), and leave of absence programs. This role ensures compliance, data integrity, and a high level of employee service while partnering with internal leadership and external benefit providers.

Key Responsibilities

Benefits Administration

  • Administer medical, dental, vision, disability, life insurance, COBRA, and 401(k) plans

  • Process enrollments, status changes, terminations, and beneficiary updates

  • Maintain accurate benefit records in HRIS and payroll systems

  • Serve as liaison with external brokers and carriers

Retirement & Compliance

  • Coordinate 401(k) enrollments, contributions, and reporting

  • Support annual renewals, audits, and compliance activities

  • Ensure compliance with ERISA, COBRA, ACA, HIPAA, and related regulations

Leave Administration

  • Administer FMLA and other Leave of Absence programs

  • Track eligibility, documentation, and return-to-work coordination

  • Provide clear communication and guidance to employees and managers

  • Ensure regulatory compliance with federal and state leave laws

Employee Support & Communication

  • Act as a trusted resource for employee benefits inquiries

  • Support open enrollment and benefits communication initiatives

  • Coordinate wellness efforts and employee education sessions

  • Maintain strict confidentiality and professionalism

Reporting & Systems

  • Utilize HRIS and payroll systems (Paycor experience preferred)

  • Generate reports using Excel for audits, renewals, and analysis

  • Ensure strong data integrity and documentation standards

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field (or equivalent experience)

  • 3+ years of hands-on benefits administration experience

  • Strong understanding of health and welfare plans, 401(k) administration, and leave management

  • Demonstrated experience administering FMLA and other leave programs

  • Strong Excel and Office 365 proficiency

  • Paycor experience a plus

  • Highly organized, service-oriented, and comfortable managing deadlines

  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

Compensation

  • $90,000 – $100,000 base salary, commensurate with experience

  • Comprehensive benefits package

  • Stable, established organization with executive-level visibility

    ***4 work from home day
    ***Bonus potential 
    ***Room for career growth

job.title: Electrical Estimator
job.description:
A growing electrical contracting firm is seeking an experienced Senior Electrical Estimator to join a fast paced, collaborative estimating department. The ideal candidate will bring advanced estimating expertise—particularly with design/build electrical projects—and a proactive, detail driven approach to supporting preconstruction efforts.

Position Summary: 
The Senior Electrical Estimator is responsible for preparing accurate, comprehensive cost estimates and bid packages for electrical construction projects of varying size and complexity. This role requires strong analytical ability, industry knowledge, and the capacity to manage multiple estimates simultaneously.
You will collaborate closely with fellow estimators, project managers, suppliers, and prospective clients to develop high quality proposals, early project viability assessments, and pre construction cost analysis. This position also contributes to project handoff, post project review, and overall continuous improvement efforts within the estimating department.

Essential Responsibilities:

  • Review and interpret project specifications, drawings, and scope documents to determine labor, materials, and equipment requirements.
  •  Perform detailed quantity takeoffs using multiple estimating methodologies (comprehensive, historical, conceptual, and median approaches).
  •  Identify qualified suppliers and subcontractors; solicit and evaluate quotes for accuracy and alignment with project requirements.
  • Prepare complete estimates and proposal documents, including inclusions, exclusions, and letters of clarification.
  • Collaborate with Project Management and Operations teams to communicate estimate details and ensure a smooth transition from pre construction to execution.
  • Support project teams during construction by assisting with lighting package approvals, pricing updates, and development of change orders.
  • Ensure timely and accurate completion of all assigned estimates and client-required documentation.
  • Participate in pre? and post project reviews to improve estimating accuracy and operational processes.
  • Perform additional duties as required to support departmental and organizational goals.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office, especially Excel, is preferred.

Qualifications & Preferred Experience

  • High school diploma required; additional specialized technical training strongly preferred.
  • Ability to read, write, and communicate effectively in English.
  • Minimum 5 years of electrical estimating experience, or equivalent experience as a licensed journeyman electrician.
  • Design/build estimating experience is highly desirable and strongly prioritized.
  • Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple deadlines in a fast paced environment.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work collaboratively across teams.
  • Demonstrated ability to proactively identify needs, solve problems, and drive tasks to completion.

The Patient Billing & Insurance Specialist is a crucial part of the healthcare team as they are the person who works within the Insurance and Billing Department to help ensure that the Patient is getting the best care available.

job.title: Manager of Learning and Development
job.description:
The Manager of Learning and Development will provide highly effective Firm-wide training programs for legal and non-legal staff located in all of our offices throughout the United States. This role is essential for fostering a culture of continuous learning and ensuring that all staff are proficient in using all firm software and resources to support their work effectively. This role offers the right candidate the opportunity to leverage their technical expertise, passion for teaching, and understanding of the legal environment, in order to elevate the firm’s technological fluency and client service excellence. The Manager of Learning and Development will design and deliver targeted learning experiences that empower attorneys and professional staff’s development. This role champions the value of digital proficiency across the firm, advocates for user needs, and ensures our attorneys and professional staff get the most out of the firm’s technology investments.

Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Primarily responsible for delivering New Hire and ongoing training classes and providing personalized technology coaching to lawyers and professional staff on the tools, devices, and services the Firm offers via various learning formats (e.g., virtual sessions, video, eLearning, classroom, 1:1, and presentations).
  • Supervising training team including delegation of duties for weekly new hire training, scheduling, specialty training programs, projects, performance, timesheets, and PTO.
  • Implementing and maintaining new and ongoing training initiatives and programs for all employees to help ensure their further development and growth.
  • Responsible for development and maintenance of the GM Training Curriculum: a master document containing all programs, instructional methodologies, sequence and duration for both onboarding and specialty trainings.
  • Design, develop, and update training programs: Creating performance-based courses, work instructions, and training aids to ensure effective learning and application of technology tools.
  • Managing workflows for and responding to training requests, including training hotlines, email addresses, and calendars.
  • Drafting, proofreading, evaluating, and finalizing training outlines and learning materials.
  • Ensuring training environment (classroom, conference rooms, and virtual settings) is set for effective learning.
  • Developing and delivering engaging training programs in a variety of formats – including instructor-led training, webinars, one-on-one coaching, and self-paced learning resources – to support diverse learning styles and schedules.
  • Collaborate with various stakeholders to customize training and content to meet the needs of various practice groups, administrative departments and user roles across the firm.
  • Contributing to and maintaining a robust knowledge base, including instructional videos, guides, and other documentation to support continuous learning and just-in-time assistance.
  • Supporting the implementation of new technologies or changing user experiences, ensuring training materials and support align with established workflows and user needs.
  • Identifying gaps in technology proficiency that may impact client service or operational efficacy and collaborate with stakeholders on targeted remediations.
  • Supporting technology projects by collaborating with Information Technology teams to develop and deliver curriculum and communications which prepares users and drives adoption.
  • Tracking and analyzing training effectiveness through feedback, assessments, and usage data to continuously improve learning outcomes.
  • Staying current with legal technology trends and best practices to ensure training content remains relevant.
  • Traveling to other offices for new hire training, project rollout training, and support.
  • Remaining flexible with duties and shift hours to help cover training needs from other regions.
  • Maintaining records and documentation: keeping track of attendance, collecting feedback, and continuously refining training methods.
  • Maintaining Firm-wide and regional office training schedules.

Collaboration:

  • Collaborating with IT teams on tech-driven projects and process improvements.
  • Participating in technology projects to help test, evaluate, and troubleshoot new tools and services. Work with Tech Support teams to support onboarding activities, office upgrades, and tech events.
  • Collaborating with leadership and teams to identify training priorities and strategies.
  • Attending and contributing to trainers’ meetings and initiatives.
  • Building and nurturing relationships across the firm to promote a culture of digital fluency and positive user experiences with technology.
  • Working with IT and administrative leadership to identify training priorities and strategies.

Required and Preferred Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent positional experience.
  • Minimum of 3-5 years delivering technology training in a law firm.
  • Proficiency with core legal technologies (iManage, Compare, Elite, PowerPDF/Acrobat, e-signature, dictation/transcription, Word styling programs, docketing systems, etc.) with the ability to apply them effectively in legal workflows.
  • Advanced skills in Microsoft Windows, Microsoft Office 365, and programs of the Office Suite including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams, with the ability to train others in their use.
  • Proficiency with learning management platforms and instructional/training material creation tools (Litmos, Scribe, Canva)
  • Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain complex tools and concepts clearly to users at all levels.
  • Strong writing skills, with a demonstrated ability to create engaging, user-friendly materials tailored to specific audiences.
  • Proven problem-solving skills, with a proactive, service-oriented approach to addressing user challenges.
  • Highly organized, with the ability to manage multiple projects, deadlines, and priorities in a fast-paced environment.
  • Collaborative team player with the ability to work independently, take initiative, and follow through on commitments.
  • Flexibility to work occasional overtime and weekends, including occasional travel, as needed.

job.title: Registered Vascular Technologist
job.description:
Registered Vascular Technologist (RVT/RVS) – Full Time
Location: Methuen, MA
Salary: $90,000–$110,000 + $5,000 Sign-On Bonus!

Position Summary:
We are seeking an experienced Registered Vascular Technologist (RVT/RVS) to join a high-volume outpatient vascular lab. As the sole technologist at the site, you will independently perform a full range of vascular ultrasound examinations, support venous procedures including ablations, and ensure the highest standards of diagnostic imaging. This role requires expertise in hemodynamics, vascular pathology, and accreditation compliance, with a strong focus on patient care.

Responsibilities:

  • Perform comprehensive vascular duplex and physiologic ultrasound exams, including carotid, abdominal aorta, renal, mesenteric, arterial, venous, and reflux studies.

  • Provide ultrasound support for venous procedures, including ablation therapies.

  • Manage daily operations of the vascular lab, including workflow, exam documentation, and patient care.

  • Ensure compliance with accreditation standards and quality assurance activities.

  • Provide preliminary results, maintain accurate documentation, and communicate findings with referring clinicians.

  • Maintain and troubleshoot ultrasound equipment, ensuring proper function and calibration.

  • Educate patients and staff, offering guidance on procedures and technical protocols.

  • Participate in continuing education to stay current in vascular ultrasound practices.

Requirements:

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Vascular Ultrasound (or related allied health/health-science field) or Associate’s degree with formal vascular ultrasound training.

  • Experience: 5–10 years performing a full range of vascular ultrasound exams, including experience supporting venous treatments/ablations in a hospital or high-volume outpatient setting.

  • Certification: RVT or RVS required.

  • Ability to work independently in a fast-paced outpatient environment.

  • Strong communication skills with patients and physicians.

Preferred:

  • Experience as the sole technologist in an outpatient facility.

  • Knowledge of accreditation standards and vascular ultrasound best practices.

  • Commitment to professional development and continuing education.

job.title: Planner II – Marlborough, MA -$85K
job.description:
Planner II – Littleton, MA – $85K

Description

Position Summary

The Planner II is responsible for end-to-end planning and execution of material and production plans for complex, long-lead, engineered-to-order medical systems and service assemblies. This role operates at the system and project level, translating MRP outputs, engineering releases, and SIOP commitments into executable production plans that support on-time system delivery, service fulfillment, and regulatory compliance. The Planner II acts as a cross-functional integrator, working closely with Manufacturing, Purchasing, Engineering, Quality, and Program Management to manage schedules, material readiness, and execution risk across assigned systems or service programs.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and maintain system-level production plans aligned to approved SIOP and master schedules
  • Create, release, and manage work orders, jobs, and build packages in ERP/MRP systems
  • Plan and sequence production activities across manufacturing cells, commissioning, and test phases
  • Own material readiness for assigned systems, including long-lead and critical components
  • Analyze MRP outputs to identify shortages, constraints, and risks
  • Coordinate with Purchasing on PO placement, expediting, and supplier recovery actions
  • Maintain accurate planning parameters (lead times, order policies, safety stock where applicable)
  • Support BOM integrity by validating planning alignment to released engineering configurations
  • Serve as the primary planning interface for assigned systems or service programs
  • Partner with Engineering to manage ECO impacts to active builds and schedules
  • Work with Manufacturing Supervisors to balance capacity, priorities, and labor constraints
  • Support Program Management with schedule inputs, risks, and recovery plans
  • Track execution against plan and proactively identify schedule and material risks
  • Lead or support mitigation plans for shortages, delays, or scope changes
  • Escalate issues through Tier I / Tier II operational governance as appropriate
  • Maintain clear ownership and documentation of planning assumptions and decisions
  • Maintain planning-related KPIs (e.g., WO on-time release, material availability, schedule adherence)
  • Support Tier meeting reporting and visual management boards
  • Identify opportunities to improve planning processes, data accuracy, and system utilization
  • Contribute to standard work development and planning best practices

 

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Operations Management, Engineering, or related field
    (or equivalent experience in manufacturing planning)
  • 3–6 years of experience in production planning, material planning, or supply chain execution
  • Experience working in engineered-to-order, project-based, or regulated manufacturing environments
  • Strong working knowledge of ERP/MRP systems (e.g., Salesforce, Oracle, SAP, Epicor, NetSuite, etc.)
  • Proficiency in Excel (pivot tables, formulas, basic data analysis)

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in medical device, capital equipment, aerospace, or defense manufacturing
  • Familiarity with SIOP / Master Scheduling concepts
  • Understanding of BOM structures, ECO workflows, and configuration control
  • APICS/ASCM certification (CPIM, CSCP) or progress toward certification
  • Experience supporting service operations or installed-base programs

osition: Licensed Practical Nurse

Overall Responsibility
We’re looking for someone who has demonstrated excellent patient and family-centered care, is a team player and has a strong work ethic. job.title: Project Manager
job.description:
Position: Project Manager 

Pay Rate: $60-$70 per hour
Duration: 10 Months 
Location: Pittsburgh, PA (local or willing to relocate)
 

Project Manager

Role Overview

We are looking for a Project Manager to lead delivery across two parallel initiatives: a multi-phase Collateral Management platform build and the implementation of a new Corporate Actions vendor solution. This role is execution-focused, with heavy coordination across internal teams, vendors, and global stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

  • Own project planning, governance, and execution across both programs
  • Develop and maintain detailed delivery plans, milestones, and dependency tracking
  • Produce executive-level reporting, including steering committee updates and status decks
  • Coordinate internal teams, vendors, and global stakeholders across the US, UK, and India
  • Track testing progress, risks, and readiness in partnership with dedicated QA teams

Program Focus Areas

Collateral Management

  • Phase 1 delivery (cash VM, cleared futures/options, triparty UMR) targeting summer go-live
  • Phase 2 expansion with increased functionality and complexity later in the year
  • Heavy data, testing, and vendor coordination requirements

Corporate Actions

  • Implementation of a new vendor platform to enhance corporate actions capabilities
  • Establish initial delivery plan with Product, then focus on execution tracking and communication
  • Support a year-end go-live with at least one client onboarded

Qualifications

  • Proven experience as a Project Manager delivering complex financial services initiatives
  • Strong planning, governance, and cross-team coordination skills
  • Experience managing vendors and distributed global teams
  • Comfortable working in Jira-based delivery environments
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Nice to Have:

  • Knowledge of collateral management or corporate actions processing
  • Experience delivering data-heavy or post-trade platforms

Role Overview

We are seeking a Business Analyst to support the definition, delivery, and governance of Investment Accounting data sets used across IBOR, Finance, and client reporting platforms. This role partners closely with Product, Engineering, and Operations to translate business requirements into clear, testable data specifications and ensure high-quality data delivery across systems.

job.title: Controller
job.description:
Controller

Position Overview
The Controller manages the accounting staff, is responsible for the oversight of all accounting policies, procedures, operations, and audits; oversees and manages payroll and the financial aid programs; and is a key member of the team responsible for financial reporting and analysis. As a strategic partner to the Director of Finance and Operations, the Controller must be able to provide leadership in identifying and implementing best practices for all accounting and financial functions while promoting a team and service-oriented environment.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Participate fully as a member of the school’s community and the Business Office with an emphasis on effective collaboration in support of a culture of respect, trust, teamwork, accuracy, productivity and continuous improvement.
  • Oversee the School’s internal control structure as it relates to the policies, procedures and documentation of all accounting and financial reporting activities, exercise prudent financial judgment.
  • Manage the accounting department maintaining a high quality of work in the office; provide training, direction, motivation, and opportunities for skills development.
  • Ensure that the school complies with all applicable accounting procedures and standards in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) and the Financial Accounting Services Board (FASB).
  • Prepare timely and accurate financial statements for administration, board of trustees, and banks.
  • Oversee generation and distribution of monthly reports for administrators and budget managers. Respond to requests for financial information from budget managers and members of the school community.
  • Oversee, coordinate, and perform daily operations for general accounting, student billing, accounts receivable, accounts payable, cash management, fixed assets, payroll and benefits accounting.
  • Collaborate with the development office to reconcile and report on fundraising activity.
  • Oversee endowment accounting, discounts and allowances accounting, net asset releases and year-end analysis.
  • Oversee and maximize the department’s utilization of the Blackbaud, fixed assets, and after school accounting systems and reporting.
  • Plan, facilitate, and coordinate annual independent audits including the financial audit, workers compensation audit and the 403(b)-audit including preparation of reports, reconciliations, and financial statements.
  • Assist in preparing all required tax returns, government reports, and surveys including Form 5500 for the 403(b)-retirement plan and the health and welfare plans.
  • Participate as an ex-officio member of the Finance, Audit, and Investment committees
  • Other tasks and duties, as assigned.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in accounting or finance required
  • CPA or advanced degree preferred
  • A minimum of seven years’ relevant experience in progressively responsible positions within a not for-profit accounting environment, preferably in private education; three of those years in a supervisory role.
  • Excellent communication skills, both orally and in writing.
  • Demonstrated ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information with discretion.
  • Ability to follow policies and procedures of the school. 
  • Must be able to complete a satisfactory background check.

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities

  • Demonstrated ability identifying and implementing “best practices” for financial and administrative management
  • Advanced computer skills and thorough knowledge of software applications, in particular Google Workspace, Microsoft Excel (e.g. formulas, links within worksheet and workbooks, pivot tables, and macros) and Word, as well as payroll and accounting software applications such as ADP and Blackbaud
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills; ability to effectively communicate complex financial or accounting information to non-accountants
  • Excellent analytical, strategic, mathematical and quantitative skills
  • Must demonstrate strong attention to detail and accuracy
  • Strong organizational skills, ability to establish and manage multiple priorities, and ability to work under pressure while maintaining the highest professional standards
  • Ability to manage, motivate and evaluate staff; team building and change management experience preferred
  • The ability to work collaboratively with a diverse group of faculty, staff, parents and other constituents with fairness, respect, consistency and integrity
  • Creative ability, a positive outlook, an excellent work ethic and a sense of humor
  • Demonstrated ability to embrace and share the school’s mission
  • Must be able to sit for long periods at a desk working on the computer

Salary & Benefits:
Salary range: $100,000-$140,000, commensurate with experience.
This is a full-time 12-month position.

We thrive to provide a competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package that supports our faculty and staff, including: health insurance, prescription drug benefit, vision, healthcare/dependent care flexible spending account (FSA), long term disability, life insurance, employee assistance program (EAP), 403(b) with employer matching, tuition reduction for employees’ children, up to 30 paid holidays, sick time and personal time, and early dismissal on Fridays.

About:
The school creates a Jewish educational experience in which each child, from 15 months through eighth grade, is known, belongs, is engaged, and is inspired. Faculty, staff, and students benefit from a highly collaborative and mutually supportive environment in which individual strengths are recognized, cultivated, and essential to the success of the community.
Individuals who work at the school must thrive in a culture grounded in the following shared values:

  • Believing there is no limit to better
  • Providing honest and kind feedback
  • Failing forward
  • Leaning into complexity
  • Being kinder than necessary

#toponehire
