job.title: Data Science Engineer
job.description:
We are seeking a versatile Data Science Engineer to join our team. You will be responsible for designing, building, and operationalizing advanced data-driven solutions that support business decision-making and product innovation. This role spans the entire data lifecycle: from architecting robust ingestion pipelines in Azure Data Factory to developing and deploying machine learning models within Microsoft Fabric.

Collaborating closely with the Senior Manager of Enterprise Applications & Data, you will lead discussions with cross-functional teams to design appropriate data architectures and build robust pipelines using Azure cloud services. This position requires a unique blend of technical depth in data engineering and the analytical skills necessary to extract meaningful insights from complex datasets. You will ensure that the underlying data infrastructure is performant, governed, and scalable.

This role combines strong statistical and machine learning expertise with robust software engineering and data engineering practices. The ideal candidate can develop models end-to-end—from data ingestion and feature engineering through deployment, monitoring, and optimization in production environments.

MAJOR RESPONSIBILITES:

  • Uses Data Pipeline Development to design, build, and maintain enterprise-scale ETL/ELT pipelines using Azure Data Factory and Fabric Data Factory.
  • Leverages Microsoft Fabric (OneLake, Lakehouse, and Warehouse) to unify disparate data sources for downstream science workloads to build and optimize data workflows.
  • Uses Model Engineering to develop, train, and tune machine learning models using Synapse Data Science (Notebooks) and MLflow while monitoring performance, data drift, and system reliability.
  • Uses Feature Engineering to transform raw data into curated datasets using PySpark and SQL to optimize model performance.
  • Implements CI/CD patterns for machine learning, ensuring models are versioned, monitored, and easily redeployed.
  • Implements data quality checks, monitoring, and validation processes to ensure data integrity.
  • Performs exploratory data analysis to uncover trends, patterns, and actionable insights.
  • Translates business data into quantitative frameworks and measurable outcomes.
  • Communicates findings and model performance for both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Optimizes query performance and data processing workflows for efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
  • Creates and maintains technical documentation for data pipelines, models, and processes.

ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:

  • Fabric Management: Creates, updates, and secures Fabric items, specifically Lakehouses, Warehouses, Notebooks, and Dataflows Gen2 within the OneLake platform.
  • Advanced Orchestration: Manages data orchestration using advanced knowledge of Azure Data Factory pipelines, activities, triggers, and Self-hosted Integration Runtimes.
  • Data Manipulation: Utilizes a strong command of Python (Pandas, Scikit-learn, PySpark) and SQL to create dataflows and notebooks for ingestion and analytics.
  • Architecture Best Practices: Implements Star Schema and Medallion Architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold) principles to ensure data scalability.
  • Quality Assurance: Participates in code reviews and contributes to the evolution of best practices for the team.

QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Computer Science or a related field preferred
  • Minimum of three (3+) of work experience as a data science engineer with a proven track record.
  • Proven hands-on experience with the Azure Data Stack (ADLS Gen2, Azure SQL, Key Vault).
  • Experience with Power BI for visualizing model outputs and creating user dashboards.
  • Proficiency in SQL, Python, and PySpark.
  • Experience with data pipeline development and AI/ML applications.
  • Knowledge of data warehousing, data lake architecture, and Microsoft Fabric platform.
  • Familiarity with machine learning, AI, and Generative AI technologies.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
  • Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment.
  • Familiarity with Azure DevOps or GitHub Actions for automated deployments.
  • Microsoft Certifications (e.g., DP-600: Fabric Analytics Engineer or DP-700: Fabric Data Engineer).

#toponehire

job.title: Enterprise Applications Support Engineer
job.description:
JOB SUMMARY:

We are seeking an Enterprise Applications Engineer to support the Firm’s critical applications and enterprise workflows. While collaborating closely with the Senior Manager of Enterprise Application and Data, this individual will assist in the implementation of, perform updates to, and provide support and optimization for the enterprise applications used by the Firm.

This highly skilled individual will bridge the gap between the Firm’s business applications and our technical infrastructure while acting as a key liaison between IT, vendors, and other business stakeholders to facilitate all phases of application support from incident resolution to system enhancements and integrations. In this role, they will oversee the daily operations of those applications (including but not limited to) matter management systems, timekeeping, accounting and billing, document management platforms, and other integrated business applications for ongoing optimizations.

MAJOR RESPONSIBILITES:

  • Collaborates closely with firm departments , focusing on managing enterprise applications from research, implementation to ongoing support.
  • Provides tier 2 and tier 3 technical support for enterprise applications including document management systems, time and billing platforms, and related intellectual property technology solutions as an escalation resource.
  • Monitors system performance and proactively identifies and resolves technical issues to minimize downtime.
  • Troubleshoots application errors, performs root cause analysis, and implements corrective actions.
  • Manages user access, permissions, and security configurations across all enterprise applications.
  • Develops and maintains system integrations between enterprise applications using APIs, middleware, and custom scripts.
  • Creates and maintains automated workflows and business rules within enterprise applications.
  • Assists in the configuration and customization of applications to meet evolving business needs.
  • Supports the implementation of new features, modules, and system upgrades.
  • Provides training and ongoing support to end users on enterprise application functionality and best practices.
  • Creates and maintains comprehensive technical documentation including system configurations, integration specifications, and troubleshooting guides.
  • Develops user guides, knowledge base articles, and training materials for various stakeholder groups.
  • Conducts training sessions and workshops for new system features and enhancements for technical team(s).
  • Participates in application implementation projects from planning through go-live and post-implementation support.
  • Identifies opportunities for process optimization, automation, and system efficiency improvements.
  • Collaborates with vendors and third-party providers to resolve technical issues and implement new capabilities.
  • Maintains project documentation and ensures deliverables meet established timelines and quality standards.
  • Builds and maintains seamless data flows between legal applications and enterprise systems like iManage, Aderant, Okta, Microsoft 365, etc.

ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:

  • Provides technical support and maintenance for mission-critical enterprise applications with minimal downtime.
  • Develops and maintains SQL queries, reports, and data extracts for business intelligence and operational needs.
  • Meets project deliverable deadlines and service level agreements for incident resolution.
  • Coordinates with vendors for issue escalation, patch management, and system upgrades.
  • Monitors application metrics and implements proactive maintenance procedures for performance issues and improvements.
  • Maintains version control and change management documentation for all system modifications.
  • Performs application testing including unit testing, integration testing, and user acceptance testing support for new installs, updates, and patches.
  • Creates and updates documentation for system controls, updates and support for the enterprise applications and custom integrations.
  • Performs other duties as assigned.

QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Computer Science or a related field preferred.
  • Minimum of five (5+) years of work experience in the field of application support for legal applications, or equivalent combination of education and work experience.
  • Minimum of five (5+) years of experience installing, supporting, and upgrading back-end databases and enterprise class, multi-user software applications (either on-premises or cloud-based)
  • Possesses strong understanding of enterprise software architecture, legal technology systems, and the ability to collaborate effectively with various teams to support strategic objectives.
  • Ability to solve more complex problems by analyzing variables and applying appropriate solutions learned through professional experience and proficiency.
  • Knowledge of document management (iManage or NetDocuments), financial applications (Aderant), and IP management software (CPi and IPDAS).
  • Highly proficient in MS Outlook, Word, and Excel as well as basic computer and database management skills.
  • Proficiency in SQL database management and query development.
  • Experience with scripting languages (PowerShell, Python, or similar) for automation, (Okta workflow knowledge helpful)
  • Knowledge of enterprise application architecture, web services, and API integrations
  • Familiarity with Azure, AWS, or other cloud platforms.
  • Understanding of authentication protocols (SAML, OAuth, Active Directory)
  • Experience with reporting tools, (Power BI, Tableau, etc.).
  • Exceptional oral and written communication skills and the ability to effectively communicate workflow and project updates.
  • Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work effectively with various levels of constituencies.
  • Ability to work independently and with a team.
  • Flexibility and capacity to respond calmly and efficiently in stressful situations.

#toponehire

job.title: Operating Systems Security Engineer
job.description:
Responsible for the design, implementation, management, and support of the firm’s enterprise endpoint and server operating systems and associated security infrastructure.  This engineering position will focus on information security related technologies related to our core operating systems.  In addition to a focus on information security, this role will also ensure the performance, immutability, and high-availability of those technologies and resulting services. 

Serves as a key member of the Computer Security Incident Response Team. Assists in the development and maintenance of processes and procedures around investigating and remediating security incidents involving malware, ransomware and/or data loss.

Assists with the implementation of new or additional endpoint, server and associated security environments to improve infrastructure service, both locally and remotely. Assists with resource and workload management and capacity planning. Effectively manages product life-cycles, providing input for budget preparation.  Monitors and controls processes, and implements systems solutions to provide stable, secure, and optimally performing platforms.
 

About this role:

Provides full requirements in-take, design, planning, configuration, documentation, deployment and top-level support ownership of the following infrastructure technologies:

  • Accountable and responsible for Endpoint and Server Security products and projects.  This includes OS and software applications needed to provide a secure and stable desktop and server environment.
  • Development of endpoint and server OS and images including OS hardening and configuration control practices.   Use of Citrix technology in conjunction with endpoint and server Operating Systems, and laptop build design.
  • Provide support and backup on other desktop technologies as time permits, including Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager, Active Directory, Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) for deployment of patches to workstation.
  • Working with the Infrastructure Services Manager develops documented firm standards for endpoint and server security. Ensures that adherence to those standards is monitored and followed by all members of the Systems Infrastructure group.
  • Engineers new endpoint and server security build solutions for new hardware releases and to support developing business needs.
  • Assists in efforts to optimize infrastructure performance to the end-user.
  • Tests and coordinates deployment of Microsoft Security patches for workstations and servers.
  • Assists in maintaining a testing environment for development.
  • Maintains awareness of technology developments and makes recommendations to Infrastructure Management.
  • Develops roadmaps for the adoption of cutting edge endpoint and server security technologies and practices.
  • Participates in negotiations of software licensing and support agreements.
  • Participates in cross-functional team projects to implement new or updated technology.
  • Assists with resolution of escalated incidents and participates in problem management activities.
  • Cross-trains other IT staff in the use or maintenance of technology.
  • Assumes additional responsibilities as assigned.

Required Skills
What You Will Bring/Your Qualifications:

  • Mastery-level knowledge of multiple technology areas, as listed above.
  • Knowledge of key endpoint and server operating systems and associated security technologies in use at the firm including Windows 11, Windows Server 2016 and above, Trellix,  SCCM, SentinelOne, Absolute, Windows Security, Microsoft Defender, Third Party Log Management systems (including Splunk and Microsoft Sentinel), associated security services and systems. 
  • Strong technical skills and ability to learn new technologies.
  • Excellent analytical skills, including the ability to perform advanced research that involves interpretation and analysis from a variety of sources, including sources and/or data that needs to be developed.
  • Demonstrated ability to write and edit technical documentation.
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills and interpersonal skills as necessary to communicate and coordinate with others on both routine and non-routine matters.
  • Ability to effectively lead and develop others in their work and to resolve problems independently in accordance with defined policies and procedures.
  • Demonstrated ability to make significant decisions and recommendations on complex issues to achieve long-term results and strategic objectives.
  • Experience working in high-pressure professional services organization or other similar organization requiring attention to detail and sensitivity to confidential and proprietary information.

 
Required Experience

Education:

§  BA/BS or equivalent work experience.
Experience:
§  4-8+ years of experience in endpoint and server operating systems, and enterprise systems integration with significant specialization in associated security systems and related areas of technology such as Microsoft operating system and Active Directory.  

#toponehire

job.title: Information Governance Operations Lead
job.description:
The Information Governance Operations Lead is an integral part of the firm’s Information Governance team. This role will be responsible for overseeing and assisting with supervision of the day-to-day operations of the Information Governance Operations team, including file management (both physical and electronic), file intake, file releases, destruction requests, data access requests, case room management, and records retention, while identifying opportunities to enhance the Information Governance program by utilizing a thorough understanding of information management, security, and privacy principles. This role will be located in our New York office. Please note that this role may be eligible for a flexible working schedule that allows for a hybrid and in-office presence. 

Responsibilities & Qualifications

Other key responsibilities include:

  • Assisting with educating firm personnel regarding the appropriate locations where data should be stored, understanding the firm’s systems and software tools, and acting as a resource regarding various information governance questions for staff, attorneys, and other users
  • Leading and performing all aspects for all information governance processes including file intakes, releases, destructions, and data access requests for the office, ensuring compliance with firm policies and ensuring completion of the relevant process, and responding to questions from staff and other users and provides instruction and training as needed
  • Implementing records retention policies and procedures, ascertaining which files are subject to retention, communicating with attorneys and clients regarding the disposition of files, and maintaining meticulous records regarding file disposition
  • Assisting as necessary with the organization, classification, preparation, space coordination, and collection of files within systems and databases and the central filing area, case rooms, file cabinets, offsite storage facilities, or wherever files may be found
  • Assisting with developing, maintaining, and coordinating appropriate inventory systems to track persons responsible for the files, room usage, file status and location, and other factors related to effective space management and Information Governance Operations oversight
  • Protecting and maintaining any highly sensitive, confidential, privileged, financial, and/or proprietary information that the firm retains 

We’d love to hear from you if you:

  • Demonstrate excellent leadership skills (i.e., organizing, planning, problem-solving, and decision-making) necessary for effective supervision
  • Possess extensive knowledge of information governance, data security and privacy principles, best practices, and procedures
  • Exhibit the ability to oversee work of others to ensure compliance and accuracy

And have:

  • A Bachelor’s degree; a minimum of five (5) years’ relevant Information Governance or Records experience may be considered in lieu of a degree
  • A minimum of five (5) years’ relevant experience, preferably at a law firm

Benefits & Additional Information

Successful candidates will not only be provided with an outstanding career opportunity and welcoming environment, but will also be provided with a generous total compensation package with bonuses awarded in recognition of both individual and firm performance. Eligible employees can participate in the firm’s comprehensive benefit program which includes:

  • Healthcare, life and disability insurance
  • A generous 401k plan
  • At least 11 paid holidays per year, and a PTO program that accrues 23 days during the first year of employment and grows with tenure
  • Well-being programs (e.g. mental health services, mindfulness and resiliency, medical resources, well-being events, and more)

job.title: Information Governance Senior Analyst
job.description:
The Information Governance Senior Analyst is an integral part of the firm’s Information Governance team and will be responsible for coordinating all primary functions of the Information Governance Operations team, including file management (both physical and electronic), file intake, file releases, destruction requests, data access requests, case room management, and records retention, while coordinating all major functions of the department with minimal supervision; developing plans, organizing files and workflow, and assuming responsibility for the completion of major projects. This role will be located in our New York office. Please note that this role may be eligible for a flexible working schedule that allows for a hybrid and in-office presence.

Responsibilities & Qualifications

Other key responsibilities include:

  • Identifying opportunities to enhance the Information Governance program by utilizing a thorough understanding of information management, security, and privacy principles
  • Consulting firm personnel on the appropriate locations where data should be stored; acting as a resource regarding various information governance questions for staff, lawyers, and other users, and providing instruction and training as needed
  • Coordinating and performing all aspects of all Information Governance processes including file intakes, releases, destructions, and data access procedures for the office, ensuring compliance with firm policies, and ensuring completion of the relevant process
  • Coordinating the execution of and ensuring compliance with records retention procedures, ascertaining which files are subject to retention, and maintaining meticulous records regarding file disposition
  • Organizing boxes, following offsite storage procedures, and preparing files both physically and within the records management system and other databases
  • Protecting and maintaining any highly sensitive, confidential, privileged, financial, and/or proprietary information that the firm retains 

We’d love to hear from you if you:

  • Demonstrate extensive knowledge of information governance, data security, and privacy principles, best practices, and procedures
  • Display excellent communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Possess thorough knowledge of the physical and electronic resources of the department and firm and the ability to articulate the role of effective space management in the overall administration of the office

And have:

  • A bachelor’s degree or an equivalent; a minimum of five (5) years of relevant Information Governance or Records experience may be considered in lieu of a degree
  • A minimum of five (5) years of relevant Information Governance or Records experience, preferably in a law firm’s Information Governance or Records Department
  • Experience in other law firm positions having significant document organizational responsibilities may be considered in lieu of directly related Information Governance or Records experience

Benefits & Additional Information

Successful candidates will not only be provided with an outstanding career opportunity and welcoming environment, but will also be provided with a generous total compensation package with bonuses awarded in recognition of both individual and firm performance. Eligible employees can participate in the firm’s comprehensive benefit program which includes:

  • Healthcare, life and disability insurance
  • A generous 401k plan
  • At least 11 paid holidays per year, and a PTO program that accrues 23 days during the first year of employment and grows with tenure
  • Well-being programs (e.g. mental health services, mindfulness and resiliency, medical resources, well-being events, and more)
  • Professional development programs
  • Employee discounts
  • Affinity groups, networks, and coalitions for lawyers and staff

job.title: Family Nurse Practitioner
job.description:
Position: Family Nurse Practitioner
Compensation: $130,000 – $140,000 per year (base)
Additional Compensation:

  • Experience differential: $500 added to base for each year of prior clinical experience (capped at 15 years)

  • Sign-on bonus: $15,000

    Overview:

  • The Family Nurse Practitioner provides comprehensive healthcare, including diagnosis, treatment, and management of common and chronic conditions. Focus is placed on health promotion, disease prevention, patient education, and collaboration with interdisciplinary care teams. This role may include precepting students or staff and participating in community outreach and professional development activities.

Key Responsibilities:

Clinical / Technical:

  • Perform health assessments, physical exams, and diagnostic testing

  • Develop and implement individualized care plans

  • Prescribe medications and treatments within scope of practice

  • Provide patient education, counseling, and preventive care

  • Make referrals and collaborate with healthcare team members

  • Provide coverage for patient phone calls and follow-ups as assigned

  • Maintain documentation and meet productivity and quality standards

Age-Specific Competencies:

  • Care for patients across all age groups: neonates, pediatrics, adolescents, adults, and geriatrics

  • Adjust care approaches based on age-specific needs and communication styles

Organizational / Managerial:

  • Maintain punctuality, reliability, and professional appearance

  • Manage time and resources effectively

  • Participate in performance improvement initiatives and special projects

  • Uphold patient and staff confidentiality and regulatory compliance

Professional Development / Education:

  • Participate in ongoing learning, workshops, and conferences

  • Act as a preceptor or mentor to staff and students

  • Maintain collaborative practice agreements as required by law

  • Engage in professional organizations and community initiatives

Communication / Relationships:

  • Demonstrate professionalism, courtesy, and respect in interactions with patients, families, and staff

  • Collaborate effectively with interdisciplinary teams and community agencies

  • Report to the medical director or supervisory leadership as required

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s and Master’s degree in Nursing

  • Graduate of an accredited Nurse Practitioner program

  • Current NY State RN license and NP certification/registration

  • Current BCLS certification

  • Strong clinical experience as a Family Nurse Practitioner

  • Basic computer skills; familiarity with electronic health records preferred

  • Bilingual in Spanish preferred

Additional Skills:

  • Effective problem-solving, organizational, leadership, and interpersonal skills

  • Commitment to patient-centered care and health promotion

job.title: X-Ray Technician
job.description:
Role: X-Ray Technician 
Location:  Freehold, NJ 07728
Pay: $33-37/hour
Schedule: Monday through Friday 9AM-5PM
No weekends or evenings  

Requirements: 

  • 2 years of X-Ray Tech and Medical Assistant experience
  • Current NJ State X-Ray Tech Certification required
  • Bachelors or Associates Degree in Radiologic Technology, Radiology, or related field
  • Knowledge in Fluoroscopy
  • Knowledge in any EMR (practice uses eClinicalWorks, NaviNet, Helio – training provided)

Responsibilities:

  • Dual role: X-Ray Tech & Medical Assistant
  • Operate radiologic equipment to produce images of the body for diagnostic purposes
  • Position radiologic equipment and adjust controls to set techniques according to specification of examination
  • Take x-rays following established radiologic requirements and regulations to ensure patient care and safety
  • Position x-ray equipment and adjust controls to set exposure factors such as time and distance
  • Use beam-restrictive devices and patient-shielding techniques to minimize radiation exposure to patient and staff
  • Position patient on examining table and set up and adjust equipment to obtain optimum view of specific body area as requested by physician
  • Process exposed radiographs using film processors or computer-generated methods
  • Explain procedures to patients to reduce anxieties and obtain cooperation
  • Determine patients’ x-ray needs by reading requests or instructions from physicians
  • Prepare and set up x-ray room for patients
  • Make exposures necessary for the requested procedures, rejecting and repeating work that does not meet established standards
  • Operate digital picture archiving communications systems
  • Transport patients to and from exam rooms
  • Preparing progress notes for patient intake and gathering information to ensure documentation is efficiently and accurately recorded and maintained in patient charts
  • Greets patients, introduces themselves, and escorts the patient to examination room
  • Screens and documents patients for appropriate information and documents in eClinicalWorks: chief complaint, history of present illness, current medications, past medical history, allergies, surgical history, hospitalizations, family history, social history, review of systems, and vitals
  • Prepares patients for physical examination
  • Instructs patients in diagnostic tests protocols
  • Assure that sterile or non-sterile supplies such as medications, supplies, or other required equipment, are present and in working order
  • Complete quality control activities, monitor equipment operation, and report malfunctioning equipment to supervisor
  • Maintain a current file of examination protocols
  • Provide other technologists with suggestions of additional view, alternation positioning, or improved techniques to ensure the images produced are of the highest quality
  • Fields interoffice phone calls and responds to emails/communications from physicians and staff
  • Monitor and clear the fax inbox, assigning documents to the appropriate secretary
  • Prepares charts according to daily and/or weekly schedule ensuring all records are accurate and available prior to next visit
  • Performs other duties as assigned by the manager

job.title: Project Accountant
job.description:
CSS has partnered with a well-established, family-oriented commercial construction subcontractor in New England to identify a talented Project Accountant.

This respected company, with over 100 years of history in the region’s commercial subcontracting sector, specializes in divisions including drywall, acoustical ceilings, painting, electrical, mechanical, and specialty interior finish products. It is renowned for its strong family culture and consistent high performance on projects.

The Project Accountant will report directly to the COO and take ownership of financial operations tied to all projects. This includes cash management, accounts payable, contract billings, and job cost reporting. This is a hybrid flexible position with a blend of remote and onsite work. 

Key responsibilities include:

  • Assisting with the transition from the current CF Data system to Viewpoint Spectrum (a major initiative).
  • Maintaining project and general ledger accounting in Viewpoint Spectrum once implemented.
  • Supporting the consolidation of outside divisions into a centralized accounting process.
  • Managing project-related records (contracts, change orders), preparing financial reports and analyses, and providing recommendations to improve project performance.
  • Reviewing and approving vendor/subcontractor invoices, ensuring accurate cost coding.
  • Overseeing subcontractor compliance per contract terms.
  • Investigating variances and discrepancies in contract billing, budget-to-actual reports, time approvals, and unbilled project costs.
  • Closing out project accounts upon completion.
  • Serving as the key liaison between project management and accounting teams for information requests and reporting.
  • Assisting with ERP automation tools and cross-platform integrations.
  • Compiling information and reports for the CFO, President, and Board as needed.

Required qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Business, Construction Management, Engineering, or a related field.
  • Strong knowledge of project contracts and change order documentation.
  • Excellent communication and analytical skills.
  • At least 3 years of experience in project accounting within the construction industry.
  • MUST HAVE hands-on experience and detailed knowledge of Viewpoint Spectrum software — this is a non-negotiable requirement for the role.
  • Experience with systems implementation (e.g., ERP transitions) is a plus.

This is an excellent opportunity to join a stable, high-performing organization with a collaborative culture, while playing a pivotal role in modernizing their financial systems. If you meet the Spectrum expertise requirement and are interested, please reach out with your resume and relevant experience details.

 

#toponehire

job.title: Accounting Manager
job.description:
Type: Full Time, Direct Hire
Location: Boston, MA 
Work Model: Hybrid (Onsite Every Tuesday)
Compensation: $120,000-140,000 + 10% Bonus 

Complete Staffing Solutions (CSS) has partnered with a fast-growing company in the environmental services industry to find a skilled Accounting Manager to join their Boston-based team. This is a hybrid role, offering the chance to take ownership of accounting operations in a supportive, high-growth environment.

Why Join Them?

This is an exciting opportunity to step into a newly created role at a company scaling rapidly, with over $200M in revenue and 20 portfolio acquisitions in the past two years. Backed by a top 1% private equity partner, the company offers significant potential for growth and career advancement. You’ll work closely with a seasoned leadership team, including the CFO and Controller, to drive financial excellence and build systems that support continued expansion.

What You’ll Do:

As the Accounting Manager, you will oversee key aspects of accounting operations, partnering closely with the Controller and other leaders to ensure financial accuracy and compliance.

Responsibilities include:

  • Managing the month-end close process and ensuring timely financial reporting, with a focus on inventory and cost of sales.
  • Preparing detailed variance analyses and narratives on actuals, forecasts, and budgets.
  • Reviewing and approving journal entries, account reconciliations, and lease accounting processes.
  • Monitoring inventory accounting and ensuring accurate system reporting.
  • Assisting in the implementation and standardization of accounting systems and processes across multiple locations.
  • Researching and applying accounting policies to operational challenges.
  • Collaborating with cross-functional teams and leadership to support business initiatives.

What You’ll Bring:

We’re looking for a team player who’s detail-oriented, adaptable, and ready to roll up their sleeves.

Key qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field (required).
  • At least 6 years of accounting/finance experience, with 1+ years in a supervisory role.
  • Background in public accounting (required).
  • CPA license (preferred but not required).
  • Experience in inventory or manufacturing accounting (a plus).
  • Familiarity with accounting software such as QuickBooks or Sage (helpful).
  • Excellent communication skills and ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment.

What Sets This Role Apart:

  • Collaborative Culture: A welcoming, team-focused environment where relationships matter.
  • Growth Opportunity: A chance to make your mark in a rapidly scaling company backed by a respected private equity partner.
  • Hybrid Flexibility: Enjoy a balanced work schedule with remote flexibility.

job.title: Condo Portfolio Manager
job.description:
We have partnered with an elite Condo Management company out of Stamford who is looking for a Property Manager to oversee a portfolio of 15 properties, work directly with their boards and handle all tenant relations and financials.

CMCA & CAM certifications are MUST HAVES for this role

Salary range of $90-110K

The ideal individual has excellent customer service skills, is a self-starter and highly motivated, and enjoys working in a fast-paced environment. Prior experience in the property management field or experience on a property Board of Directors is a plus.

Under limited supervision, the property manager is the primary contact to oversee and coordinate all property management, maintenance, and limited construction activities associated with small to midsized, multiple-building/unit condominium, co-op and town house buildings.

The property manager is responsible for supervising the properties to ensure that they are promptly and efficiently managed with respect to maintenance, accounting and owner satisfaction. 

DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 

  • Oversee the management of buildings, including maintenance services and outside contractors/vendors
  • Direct contact for any/all resident concerns/requests pertaining to any/all building related issues
  • Contact and place service orders with maintenance staff or specialty vendors/contractors 
  • Assist with creating and maintaining appropriate budgets for each of the condo associations and oversee accounting and financial statements.
  • Monitor contracts for compliance and control costs
  • Coordinate with account manager to ensure administrative document conformity to include insurance compliance and expiration(s) for all buildings, assessment collections and follow-up with assessment delinquencies ensuring that proper notices have been distributed
  • Solicit bids for maintenance contractors and construction projects, create scope spreadsheets, and participate in the selection of contractors 
  • Perform quality assurance and control inspections for each of the buildings to ensure proper maintenance, adherence to contract specifications, and compliance with industry standards 
  • Maintain a thorough knowledge of all property management documents (Bylaws and Declarations, Rules and Regulations, certificates of insurance, vendor contracts) and operate the properties in accordance with these documents. 
  • Perform miscellaneous job-related duties as assigned that include assistance with general property management issues and policies including small administrative projects and tasks as assigned.
  • Answer and appropriately direct concerned owner phone calls and inform residents of policies/procedures.
  • Handle emergency issues afterhours associated with properties or as needed.   

 

REQUIREMENTS:

  • Must have a minimum of 1 year experience with condominium property management and have worked directly with Board of Directors.  Must have CAM license
  • Excellent customer service, open and helpful communication, and exceptional organizational skills
  • Excellent verbal and written communication and customer service skills.  
  • Be able to create budgets, track financials, and communicate updates.
  • Effective leadership, mentoring and coaching skills.
  • Valid driver’s license and reliable transportation
  • Ability to work independently, self-manage, and motivate.
  • This is a full time position.  Salary is based on experienced  

 

QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Education:  Bachelor Degree and Community Association Management (CAM) certification or higher in property management. 
  • 2 year minimum in condominium property management with management, customer service, supervisory experience, administrative, and accounting experience. 
  • Engineering, architectural, construction or similar experience a plus. 
  • Proficient in all MS Office applications.

job.title: DMS Engineer
job.description:
The DMS Engineer is responsible for the administration, maintenance, optimization, and support of the firm’s NetDocuments document management system.  This role focuses on ensuring the stability, performance, and scalability of NetDocuments services, including the execution and monitoring of Toolkit jobs, configuration management, integrations and lifecycle management.  The engineer will also leverage Litera CAM to manage user provisioning, entitlements, and workspace governance.  The ideal candidate brings strong technical expertise, problem-solving capabilities and a commitment to delivering secure, efficient, user-focused solutions.

Duties and Responsibilities

  • Serve as the subject matter expert for NetDocuments, including configuration, integration, testing, debugging, and implementation.
  • Monitor all components of the NetDocuments environment for performance, availability, and stability.
  • Manage and maintain NetDocuments components such as ndOffice and ndMail.
  • Support integrations across Office, ndOffice, ndMail, workflow systems and other applications.
  • Oversee and execute NetDocuments Toolkit jobs.
  • Troubleshoot Toolkit job issues, optimize performance, validate logs, and coordinate remediation as necessary.
  • Monitor CAM logs and ensure successful synchronization with NetDocuments and other integrated systems.
  • Develop automation scripts and workflows to streamline DMS operations and reduce manual processes.
  • Assist with data migrations, metadata reconciliation, and legacy system decommissioning when applicable.
  • Monitor, troubleshoot and resolve DMS application issues.
  • Primary contact with NetDopcuments and other vendors as appropriate to resolve problems and define the Firm’s roadmap for document management.
  • Provide off-hours support for DMS Applications issues on a rotating basis. Be able to respond to calls within ½ hour and access the system within 1 hour.
  • Other duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Education and/or Experience: 

Required:

  • A minimum of 7 years of direct engineering, implementation and administration experience with NetDocuments.
  • A minimum of 5 years of experience working in the Windows Server Environment
  • A minimum of 5 years of SQL Server experience.
  • A minimum of 5 years Toolkit experience.

Preferred:

  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science or related filed.
  • Experience administering Litera CAM.

Other Skills and Abilities:

The following will also be required of the successful candidate:

  • Strong organizational skills
  • Strong attention to detail
  • Good judgment
  • Strong interpersonal communication skills
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Able to work harmoniously and effectively with others
  • Able to preserve confidentiality and exercise discretion
  • Able to work under pressure
  • Able to manage multiple projects with competing deadlines and priorities

job.title: Payroll Manager – Middlesex County
job.description:
Payroll Manager – $140K +/- + bonus

Middlesex County – (Hybrid/Remote)
Industry: Global Industrial Technology & AI 
Employment Type: Full-Time
About the Company
We are a global leader inthe industrial automation space, providing the “eyes” for the modern factory. Our cutting-edge technology—ranging from advanced sensors and barcode readers to AI-powered deep learning software—is used by the world’s largest manufacturers and logistics providers to ensure quality and optimize complex production lines.
With a history of innovation spanning over four decades, we serve nearly every industry imaginable, including automotive, consumer electronics, and e-commerce. As apublicly-traded global organization, we pride ourselves on a high-performance culture of excellence, creative problem-solving, and a relentless commitment to automating the world.
Essential Functions

  • Strategic Oversight: Play a key role in shaping the long-term vision for the payroll function; assess current systems and identify global best practices for administration and automation.
  • Operations Management: Lead the payroll team and oversee end-to-end processing to ensure accurate, timely payments to global employees, including all required country reporting and tax filings.
  • Compliance & Governance: Ensure all operations align with internal policies, regional laws (state/province/local), and maintain strict adherence to Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) requirements.
  • Financial Reconciliation: Approve all payroll operational steps and funding cycles in close partnership with internal HR and Finance departments.
  • Equity & Stock Administration: Oversee the processing of equity grants (including stock options and RSUs), ensuring global tax compliance in coordination with equity vendors and internal stakeholders.
  • Mobility Support: Partner with legal and external vendors to manage the payroll implications of global and regional employee mobility.
  • Audit Management: Act as the primary point of contact for internal and external audit teams during quarterly and annual payroll reviews.

Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

  • Systems Expertise: Significant experience with ADP Workforce Now is required.
  • Tech-Forward Mindset: A passion for leveraging technology to drive team optimization and process efficiency.
  • Leadership: Proven ability to coach, develop, and mentor payroll professionals.
  • Vendor Management: Experience managing and holding external service providers accountable to high performance standards.
  • Problem Solving: Strong analytical instincts for resolving complex payroll and tax issues.
  • Adaptability: Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, rapidly changing corporate environment.
  • Communication: Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to present financial concepts to non-finance stakeholders.

Education and Experience

  • Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Business, or a related field.
  • Experience: Minimum 7 years of relevant payroll experience, preferably within apublicly-traded, global technology company.
  • Leadership: At least 3 years of direct people management experience.
  • Global Scope: Prior experience managing international payroll cycles is highly preferred.

Company Culture
We believe in a high-performance culture where innovation and results are celebrated. Our team is comprised of creative problem-solvers who thrive in an environment that is fast-paced, intellectually stimulating, and collaborative. We work hard to maintain our market-leading position and place a high value on recognizing milestones, fostering professional growth, and maintaining a spirited team atmosphere across our worldwide offices.

job.title: Manager of Accounting
job.description:
Summary
We are seeking an experienced Accounting Manager to lead accounting operations in a multi-site manufacturing environment. This role is responsible for overseeing the monthly close, managing consolidations across multiple entities, and supervising a team while ensuring accuracy, compliance, and strong internal controls.
Key Responsibilities

  • Manage and oversee the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes, ensuring timeliness and accuracy
  • Lead consolidations for multiple entities and sites, including intercompany transactions and eliminations
  • Supervise, mentor, and develop accounting staff; provide clear direction and performance feedback
  • Ensure compliance with GAAP, company policies, and internal control requirements
  • Partner with operations and finance leadership to support manufacturing accounting needs (inventory, cost accounting, variances, etc.)
  • Review and approve journal entries, account reconciliations, and financial statements
  • Support audits (internal and external) and serve as a key point of contact for auditors
  • Drive process improvements, standardization, and system efficiencies across entities
  • Utilize SAP to manage and optimize accounting and reporting processes

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance (CPA or CMA a plus)
  • 5+ years of progressive accounting experience, preferably in a manufacturing environment
  • Proven experience supporting multi-site and/or multi-entity organizations
  • Strong background in financial consolidations
  • Demonstrated ownership of a fast-paced, accurate close process
  • Prior experience supervising or managing accounting staff
  • Hands-on experience with SAP (strongly preferred)
  • Strong analytical, organizational, and communication skills

Preferred Experience

  • Manufacturing or cost accounting experience
  • ERP implementation or optimization experience
  • Experience scaling processes in a growing or complex organization

job.title: Medical Director (Family/Internal Medicine)
job.description:
Medical Director of Adult Medicine
Location: Hartford CT
Work Schedule: Monday–Friday, 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM; no evenings or weekends
Reports to: Chief Medical Officer
Direct Reports: ~40 staff, including Nurse Manager, Practice Manager, 8-11 providers (mid-levels, optometrists, podiatrists), Infectious Disease Director, Assistant Clinical Director (if applicable)

Position Summary

This is a 50% clinical, 50% administrative leadership role. As the top clinical leader for the department, you will oversee all clinical staff and operations, drive quality and performance improvement initiatives, and lead strategic clinical and business objectives in partnership with the executive team and CMO.

Key Responsibilities

Clinical Leadership

  • Provide direct and indirect leadership including recruitment, training, supervision, evaluation, and quality oversight of clinical staff

  • Maintain an active patient caseload; providers typically see 14-16 patients per 8-hour shift (Director’s clinical volume may vary)

  • Collaborate on population health initiatives and performance evaluations, including peer review and provider development

  • Serve as a clinical backup during call rotations and emergency coverage

  • Support credentialing and privileging of clinical staff

  • Promote ongoing clinical education and ensure compliance with documentation, coding, and billing standards

  • Foster relationships with clinical partners and specialists

Administrative Leadership

  • Develop and implement service line business plans and clinical operations strategies

  • Oversee scheduling, staffing, space management, and technology use to optimize clinical operations

  • Use data analytics to monitor and improve clinical and operational outcomes

  • Address patient grievances in collaboration with risk management

  • Identify and implement new or improved clinical services

  • Ensure compliance with accreditation and licensing requirements (Joint Commission, NCQA, Department of Public Health)

  • Collaborate on recruitment, onboarding, retention, and engagement of clinical staff

  • Provide clinical input on organizational committees and strategic initiatives

Community Engagement

  • Represent the organization positively in the community through boards, commissions, and public events

  • Maintain deep knowledge of healthcare trends, particularly in the FQHC environment

  • Uphold CHS Core Values, Compliance Program, and policies

Qualifications

Required:

  • MD or DO from accredited medical school and residency in Family or Internal Medicine

  • Board certified in Family or Internal Medicine

  • Active Connecticut medical license

  • Minimum 5 years practicing as a physician

  • CPR certification (or eligible within 90 days)

  • Experience with CPT-4 and ICD-10 coding

  • Minimum 3 years clinical administrative/management experience

Preferred:

  • 7+ years outpatient/ambulatory care experience in multi-provider settings

  • 5+ years senior or director-level management

  • Familiarity with Primary Care Medical Home standards or behavioral health integration principles

  • Experience with socioeconomically diverse populations

  • Knowledge of federally qualified health center (FQHC) operations

Skills & Competencies

  • Strong leadership, mentoring, and performance management skills

  • Strategic thinker with high analytical ability

  • Ability to handle long hours and occasional weekend work

  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills

  • Maintains professionalism, integrity, and discretion under pressure

  • Adaptable in fast-paced, changing environments

  • Ability to travel between work sites as needed

  • Data-driven decision-maker

#toponehire

job.title: Adult Medicine Physician
job.description:
Adult Medicine Physician – Outpatient Primary Care
Location: Hartford, CT area
Salary Range: $169,774 – $248,230 (commensurate with experience)
Open to full-time or part-time!

Clinic Hours:
Monday – Thursday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM
Friday: 9:30 AM – 5:00 PM
Closed for lunch daily: 12:30 PM – 1:00 PM

A mission-driven, community-based outpatient clinic is seeking an Adult Medicine Physician to join its primary care team. This is a full-scope, ambulatory primary care role focused on delivering high-quality, patient-centered care to a diverse adult population.

This organization is NHSC Loan Repayment Program eligible and offers a collaborative, data-informed environment with a commitment to underserved care and clinical excellence.

What You’ll Do:

  • Manage your own panel of patients with both chronic and acute needs

  • See patients during scheduled visits, same-day visits, and urgent care appointments

  • Collaborate closely with care teams to address clinical and social determinants of health

  • Refer patients to specialists, order diagnostics, and coordinate follow-up

  • Document all care in a timely, accurate, and compliant manner

  • Board Certification in Internal Medicine, Family Medicine, or Infectious Disease

  • Licensed (or license-eligible) in Connecticut

  • DEA and CT Controlled Substance Registration (or eligibility)

  • A strong commitment to providing culturally competent care

  • Excellent communication skills and a collaborative spirit

  • Comfort using electronic health records and data tools

  • 3 years’ experience in outpatient or urgent care preferred

  • Spanish/English bilingual skills strongly preferred

Compensation & Perks:

  • NHSC Loan Repayment Program eligibility

  • Competitive salary + productivity bonus

  • Paid malpractice and CME

  • Health, dental, and vision insurance

  • 401(k) with up to 4% employer match

  • Generous PTO and 9 paid holidays

  • Employer-paid life, short-term, and long-term disability insurance

  • Employee Assistance Program

  • Paid leave options for bereavement, maternity, military, and more

job.title: Legal Assistant
job.description:
About the Role
Our client, a leading construction and infrastructure law firm, is seeking an experienced, detail-oriented Litigation Secretary to support the firm’s Construction and Infrastructure Litigation practice areas. The Litigation Secretary will be responsible for providing case management, administrative, and secretarial support to assigned attorneys. The ideal candidate will have extensive construction claims experience, a working knowledge of the Federal, State, and County court systems and be proficient with the Microsoft Office suite, including strong Microsoft Word skills. This position will be 4-5 days on-site in the New York City office and will report to the Legal Support Services Supervisor.
Compensation is competitive and commensurate with experience, with a base salary range of $85,000 to $130,000 annually.
Key Responsibilities:
Execute precise e-filings across New York State, Federal, and Appellate courts, ensuring strict adherence to specific jurisdictional rules and deadlines
Format and finalize high-volume legal documents, including pleadings, briefs, and appellate filings, featuring automated Tables of Contents and Tables of Authorities
Collaborate closely with the Docketing team to monitor and meet all court-mandated and discovery-related dates
Lead the conflict check process, prepare engagement letters, and maintain digital case files in compliance with firm-wide Information Governance policies
Maintain case management of client files adhering to the firm’s Information Governance policies and administrative best practices
Manage calendar, travel, and support logistics for meetings, depositions, mediations, arbitrations, and trials, including venue arrangements and necessary services (e.g., catering, technology, court reporters)
Strategically delegate administrative tasks to support staff and provide high-level backup for other litigation secretaries to maintain firm-wide productivity
Experience & Qualifications:
High School Diploma or equivalent is required, Bachelor’s degree preferred
A minimum of five to seven years of litigation secretary experience directly supporting fast-paced litigation attorneys is required. Previous experience supporting construction litigation claims experience is preferred
Recent experience and strong working knowledge of legal terminology, legal and court proceedings, including State and Federal court filing processes/systems
Extensive experience with managing tracked changes, formatting complex, multi-paged documents, such as pleadings, briefs, tables of contents, tables of authorities, and liens
Experience supporting multiple attorneys simultaneously
Strong ability to multi-task, prioritize work, and communicate workload challenges to attorneys and/or administrative manager
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office and various legal software applications (e.g., iManage, Litera Compare, ChromeRiver, etc.)
Superior communication and interpersonal skills, enabling credible and diplomatic interactions with all levels of the firm and clients, and effective collaboration
Exceptional organizational, grammar, spelling, and proofreading skills to support fast-paced administrative responsibilities
The successful candidate must be proactive, customer service oriented, energetic and reliable. Impeccable integrity, excellent judgment, and sensitivity to others are essential. Enthusiasm, diverse interests, and a good sense of humor are also useful and appreciated.
Equal Opportunity Employer
Our client is an Equal Opportunity Employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, genetic information, protected veteran status, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, disability status or any other category protected by local, state or federal law. This policy applies to all aspects of employment lifecycle including recruitment, placement, promotion, transfer, compensation, benefits, social and recreational activities.
Our client is a Fair Chance employer and will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records for employment. Applicants will not be asked to provide information about any criminal history until a conditional offer of employment is extended. Employees have access to highly sensitive, confidential and valuable information of clients and others, and certain criminal history may pose a significant risk to the firm’s business operations and reputation.

job.title: Jr Software Engineer
job.description:
Summary of Position

The Junior Software Engineer will be responsible for assisting the Lead Software Engineer in designing, developing, testing, production transfer, release, maintenance and improvement of all software modules or programmable device firmware designed clients products.

Duties and Responsibilities
  • Work with Sales and Marketing as well as current technology partners to determine the initial customer design requirements and then turns those requirements into new design or feature improvement projects/tasks.
  • Assist in designing and developing GUI, device firmware and device driver software for clients systems.
  • Assist in developing communication interfaces for X-ray imaging cameras and other imaging devices.
  • Work closely with the hardware development team on systems-level design issues.
  • Support Engineering and R&D teams to test, debug, and improve current software/firmware designs.
  • Support production during design transfer from development to production.
  • Design and execute Verification/Validation (V&V) plans, report, and document them for quality system and compliance purposes on given development tasks.
  • Maintain organized software/firmware development design control artifacts, archives, and released software archive.
  • Release software/firmware after V&V tests to Production and Service.
  • Support “Customer Service” (Service) to analyze and resolve software, device firmware or other device driver level issues.
  • Performs all other duties as required to meet the company’s goals and objectives.

 

Qualifications and Education Requirements
  • BS in Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or related sciences (Biomed. Eng, Physics, Math, EE) minimum.
  • Ability to support multiple projects/products simultaneously and work closely with the hardware development team.
  • Understanding of the full product development cycle with electro-mechanical aspects of a system: design, develop, test, release, production.
  • Understanding of hardware/software design trade-offs and integration.
  • Demonstrated experience in object-oriented software design, development, testing, and debugging with Microsoft programming tools and technologies.  MS Visual Studio .NET/C# preferred, WPF a plus.
  • Skilled in debugging, troubleshooting, and working with large codebases.
  • Familiarity with Microsoft SQL Server and SQL syntax.
  • Good written and verbal communication skills, particularly in communicating complex technical details with both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Use of Office (Word, Excel, and PowerPoint) and/or similar productivity tools in Windows PC environment to generate documents.
  • Knowledge or experience with image processing algorithms for life science or industrial imaging systems is a plus.
  • Knowledge or experience with defect tracking systems (Jira) and software version control tools (Git, Bitbucket) is a plus.

#toponehire

job.title: SENIOR MANUFACTURING ENGINEER
job.description:
Senior Manufacturing Engineer – Aerospace Supply Chain
Location: Connecticut | Full-Time | Hybrid Travel to Suppliers
BASE COMPENSATION: 130,000-135,000

A growing aerospace manufacturing organization is seeking an experienced Senior Manufacturing Engineer to serve as a key technical liaison between customers and suppliers supporting complex aerospace engine components. This role is highly cross-functional and focused on supplier development, manufacturing problem-solving, and ensuring suppliers meet demanding aerospace quality, cost, and delivery requirements.

This position is ideal for a seasoned engineer who thrives in a fast-paced aerospace environment and enjoys working directly with suppliers to solve challenging manufacturing and quality issues.

Key Responsibilities

  • Serve as the primary technical interface between customers and suppliers for complex aerospace components.

  • Research, identify, evaluate, and onboard new suppliers capable of meeting technical, quality, cost, and delivery requirements.

  • Work directly with suppliers to develop manufacturing processes, improve production methods, and resolve technical issues.

  • Investigate supplier nonconformances, quality escapes, and delivery delays, performing root cause analysis and implementing corrective actions.

  • Clearly communicate customer specifications, engineering requirements, and quality expectations to suppliers.

  • Lead suppliers through corrective action plans, process improvements, and technical troubleshooting.

  • Assess supplier capabilities, capacity, risk exposure, and overall performance.

  • Recommend alternative sourcing strategies when supplier performance or capabilities do not meet requirements.

  • Collaborate with internal teams including engineering, quality, procurement, and program management.

  • Review and interpret engineering drawings, specifications, and customer technical requirements to ensure manufacturability and compliance.

  • Support business decisions related to cost, schedule, and risk management.

  • Travel to supplier facilities as required to support audits, development initiatives, and issue resolution.

Qualifications

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Aerospace Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related technical discipline required.

Experience

  • 10+ years of experience in manufacturing engineering, supplier development, supplier quality, or supply chain engineering within the aerospace industry.

  • Experience working with OEMs or Tier 1 / Tier 2 aerospace suppliers.

  • Strong knowledge of AS9100, NADCAP, and aerospace quality systems.

  • Proven experience resolving manufacturing, quality, and supplier performance issues.

Technical Skills

  • Strong ability to interpret engineering drawings, specifications, and aerospace technical requirements.

  • Proficiency with Microsoft Office and ERP systems.

  • Experience evaluating supplier manufacturing capabilities and production processes.

Core Competencies

  • Strong problem-solving and root cause analysis skills.

  • Ability to independently investigate complex technical challenges and drive solutions.

  • Solid business acumen, balancing cost, schedule, and risk considerations.

  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills with customers, suppliers, and internal teams.

Why This Role

This is a highly visible engineering position with the opportunity to directly impact supplier performance, product quality, and the successful delivery of complex aerospace programs. The role offers exposure to advanced aerospace manufacturing processes and the chance to work with leading suppliers across the industry.

 

#toponehire

job.title: Senior Buyer (Aerospace)
job.description:
Senior Buyer  (1st Shift)

Supply Chain | Aerospace Manufacturing
Location: Southwick, MA
Compensation: $96,300 – $110,000 (Salary)

A well-established aerospace manufacturing organization is seeking an experienced Senior Buyer – Indirect to support supply chain operations on 1st shift. This is a hands-on role responsible for sourcing, supplier management, cost control, and process improvement within a regulated manufacturing environment. The position also includes leadership responsibilities and project oversight.

Role Overview

The Senior Buyer will manage indirect procurement activities while partnering closely with Operations, Engineering, Quality, and Production teams. This individual will lead supplier negotiations, ensure material availability, mitigate supply risk, and support continuous improvement initiatives. The role balances individual contribution with team leadership and mentoring.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage indirect purchasing activities to support manufacturing operations

  • Identify, evaluate, and negotiate with suppliers to achieve optimal cost, quality, and delivery

  • Create and manage purchase orders; monitor status to ensure on-time delivery

  • Analyze vendor performance and implement cost-saving and risk-reduction strategies

  • Maintain accurate purchasing and supplier documentation within ERP systems

  • Resolve supplier quality, delivery, and rejection issues

  • Support weekly production planning meetings with material status updates

  • Coordinate logistics and transportation activities as needed

  • Partner cross-functionally on new products, process changes, and supplier selection

  • Lead projects and contribute to supply chain process improvements

  • Train, mentor, and support buyers; assist with onboarding new team members

  • Track KPIs, identify bottlenecks, and support workflow optimization

  • Ensure compliance with safety, quality, and regulatory requirements

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience

  • Prior experience in aerospace manufacturing

  • Strong background in indirect or raw material purchasing

  • Solid understanding of manufacturing processes and supply chain operations

  • Proven negotiation and supplier relationship management skills

  • Strong analytical, organizational, and communication skills

  • ERP system experience and proficiency in Microsoft Office

Responsibilities

Experience:
  • Minimum 2–3 years of experience in aerospace inspection, quality control, or precision manufacturing
  • Hands-on experience with GD&T interpretation and blueprint reading
  • Familiarity with AS9100/ISO quality standards (preferred)

Skills

  • Advanced measurement techniques
  • Technical drawing interpretation
  • Quality assurance and compliance
  • Documentation and reporting
  • Communication and teamwork

job.title: Lead Software Engineer
job.description:
Summary

We are seeking a highly skilled Lead Software Engineer with expertise in building full-stack applications using React, .NET, Next.js, and Azure. This role will be responsible for leading technical development, conducting code reviews, and ensuring high-quality software delivery. The ideal candidate is a hands-on leader who can guide a team while also contributing directly to the codebase.

Duties and Responsibilities

  • Architect, develop, and maintain scalable full-stack applications using .NET, Python, React/Next.js on Azure.
  • Lead technical decision-making, providing expertise on best practices, architecture, and modern software development methodologies.
  • Conduct code reviews, ensuring high code quality, security, and performance optimization.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including product managers and designers, to define, design, and ship new features.
  • Mentor and coach junior and mid-level engineers, fostering a culture of growth and technical excellence.
  • Monitor and improve application performance, optimizing database queries, front-end rendering, and API interactions.
  • Ensure security best practices in web applications, including authentication, authorization, and data protection.
  • Work with DevOps teams to implement CI/CD pipelines and automate deployments on Azure.
  • Stay up to date with emerging technologies and advocate for their adoption where appropriate.

Salaries vary by location and are based on numerous factors, including, but not limited to, the relevant market, skills, experience, and education of the selected candidate. If an estimated salary range for this role is available, it will be provided in our Target Salary Range section. Our compensation package also includes bonus eligibility and a comprehensive benefits program. Benefits information can be found at Sidley.com/Benefits.

Target Salary Range

$147,700 – $191,580 if located in Boston, California, New York, Illinois, Washington, D.C., or reporting to a supervisor in Illinois.

Qualifications

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each Essential Duty and Responsibility (the “Essential Duties”) satisfactorily.  The requirements listed below are representative of the minimum knowledge, skill, and/or ability required.  Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the Essential Duties.  If you need such an accommodation, please email staffrecruiting@sidley.com (current employees should contact Human Resources).

 

Education and/or Experience:

Required:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
  • A minimum of 5 years of experience in full-stack software development.
  • Expertise in React, .NET, Next.js, Python, and Azure.
  • Strong experience in code reviews, software design patterns, and scalable application architecture.
  • Proficiency in RESTful APIs and microservices architectures.
  • Hands-on experience with databases, such as SQL Server, PostgreSQL, or NoSQL solutions.
  • Experience with version control systems (Git) and Agile development methodologies.

Preferred:

  • Experience with containerization (Docker, Kubernetes, Azure Container Apps) on Azure.
  • Knowledge of serverless computing and cloud-native applications.
  • Familiarity with unit testing frameworks and automated testing best practices.
  • Certifications in Azure or relevant technologies.

Other Skills and Abilities:

The following will also be required of the successful candidate:

  • Strong organizational skills
  • Strong attention to detail
  • Good judgment
  • Strong interpersonal communication skills
  • Strong analytical and problem solving skills
  • Able to work harmoniously and effectively with others
  • Able to preserve confidentiality and exercise discretion
  • Able to work under pressure
  • Able to manage multiple projects with competing deadlines and priorities
  • A self-starter who desires to show ownership and commitment to the job
  • Strong customer service skills

 #toponehire

A global, privately held manufacturing organization is seeking a Senior / Business Unit Controller to provide financial leadership for a complex, multi-entity operation. This is a highly visible role with significant ownership, reporting directly to corporate finance leadership and partnering closely with senior executives.

This position is ideal for an experienced Controller or VP-level finance leader looking for broader scope, international exposure, and meaningful influence within a growing organization.

Position Overview

The Senior / Business Unit Controller is responsible for overseeing financial reporting, controls, compliance, and performance management across domestic and international operations. This role combines strategic leadership with hands-on oversight and plays a key role in supporting operational excellence and long-term decision-making.

The role includes leadership responsibility for accounting teams in the U.S. and Europe.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead monthly, quarterly, and year-end close processes for multiple entities

  • Ensure accuracy, integrity, and compliance of financial reporting and internal controls

  • Provide financial analysis and insights to support executive and operational decision-making

  • Lead, mentor, and develop international accounting teams

  • Drive continuous improvement across accounting processes, systems, and controls

  • Support cash flow management, forecasting, and ad hoc reporting initiatives

  • 10+ years of progressive accounting experience in a complex manufacturing environment

  • Proven experience leading and developing accounting teams

  • Experience supporting multi-entity, multi-currency operations

Compensation & Benefits

This role is being handled with discretion. Company details will be shared during the interview process.

Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to protected characteristics.

Required Qualifications

  • 2+ years of real estate mortgage loan processing experience
  • Prior experience working within a bank or credit union
  • Strong understanding of secondary market guidelines and secondary mortgage processing
  • Hands-on experience with Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae loan requirements
  • Proficiency with loan origination systems and mortgage-related software
  • Ability to quickly learn and adapt to new systems and technology in a paperless environment
  • Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with conventional, conforming, and secondary market loans

  • Ability to manage multiple loans simultaneously in a fast-paced environment

Work Environment

  • Predominantly paperless, technology-driven workflow
  • Collaborative team environment supporting lending, underwriting, and closing functions

job.title: FP&A Manager
job.description:
Position Overview

Key Responsibilities
Cash Flow Management

  • Develop and maintain detailed weekly, monthly, and quarterly cash flow forecasts with precision expected in a PE-owned environment.
  • Monitor liquidity and funding needs to support production schedules, capital investments, and growth initiatives.
  • Partner with Accounting and other functions to ensure accurate reporting and proactive management of cash requirements.
  • Support CFO and PE stakeholders with scenario modeling, cash bridge analyses, and liquidity risk assessments.

 
Working Capital Optimization

  • Drive continuous improvement in the cash conversion cycle (CCC) by optimizing receivables, payables, and inventory performance.
  • Collaborate with Operations and Procurement to establish best practices in order-to-cash, procure-to-pay, and inventory management, ensuring optimal working capital utilization.
  • Lead initiatives to unlock working capital and improve free cash flow, in line with PE performance metrics.
  • Report and analyze working capital KPIs, providing actionable recommendations for improvement.
  • Assist the preparation of reports to the investors and lenders, assist in the monthly operations review and board meeting presentation.   

 
OPEX Oversight, Financial Planning and Reporting

  • Monitor OPEX across departments and manufacturing sites, ensuring alignment with budgets and cost-efficiency targets.
  • Proactively lead periodic reviews with various functions to ensure the business understands cost drivers and improvement opportunities.
  • Identify and explain variances versus forecast and prior year, with a focus on operational levers and cost savings.
  • Support the annual operating plan, reforecast cycles, and board reporting requirements.

 
Team Management

  • Evaluate the current FP&A team structure, skills, and workload to ensure alignment with business needs
  • Provide coaching, development, and clear direction to strengthen team capabilities and performance
  • Team up with the director to build an effective, collaborative FP&A team that delivers timely, high-quality financial insights

 
Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in finance, accounting, economics, or related field; MBA, CPA, or CMA preferred.
  • 8 to 10 years of progressive FP&A experience, with direct exposure to PE-backed manufacturing or industrial companies.  Proven track record of building up a functional team that thrives in a fast-paced environment.
  • Strong knowledge of manufacturing finance, including cost, OPEX, production cycles, and supply chain drivers.
  • Advanced Excel and financial modeling skills; experience with ERP systems (JDE, Oracle, NetSuite, or similar).
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, able to interact effectively with other finance leaders, various functions within the company and other external parties.
