Role Overview
We are seeking a seasoned EHS and Facilities Supervisor with a strong background in manufacturing or industrial environments. This role is responsible for the dual oversight of our Environmental, Health, and Safety (EHS) management systems and the operational integrity of our facility. You will be the primary lead for regulatory compliance, risk mitigation, and the continuous improvement of our physical infrastructure.

Key Responsibilities
Environmental, Health & Safety (EHS) Leadership

  • System Administration: Own and administer the site EHS management system in full compliance with OSHA, EPA, and all applicable state and local regulations.
  • Policy Development: Create, implement, and maintain comprehensive EHS policies, procedures, and detailed work instructions.
  • Risk Management: Lead hazard identification, formal risk assessments, and robust corrective action programs.
  • Incident Investigation: Conduct thorough incident investigations and root cause analyses to ensure timely and effective corrective actions.
  • Compliance & Auditing: Coordinate internal audits, site inspections, and serve as the primary point of contact for all regulatory agency interactions.
  • Safety Culture: Drive continuous improvement initiatives aimed at reducing incidents, near-misses, and overall environmental risk.

Facilities Management

  • Maintenance Oversight: Supervise routine facility maintenance, emergency repairs, and preventive maintenance programs to ensure zero downtime.
  • Contractor Coordination: Manage relationships and performance for all external contractors and specialized service providers.
  • Project Support: Provide safety and facility expertise for capital improvement projects, ensuring all upgrades meet technical and regulatory standards.

Required Qualifications

  • Experience: 7+ years of relevant EHS experience within a manufacturing, industrial, or highly regulated environment.
  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Health & Safety, Industrial Hygiene, Safety Engineering, Environmental Science, or a related technical field.
  • Certifications: Professional certifications such as ASP (Associate Safety Professional), CSP, CHMM, or CIH are highly preferred (or actively in progress).

Skills & Competencies

  • Deep technical knowledge of federal and local environmental and safety laws.
  • Proven ability to lead incident investigations and implement long-term safety solutions.
  • Strong project management skills with the ability to oversee complex facility repairs and upgrades.

job.title: Dental Hygienist
job.description:
Position: Dental Hygienist
Pay Rate: $42-$50 per hour
Duration: 3 Months
Location: Norwalk CT
Monday- Friday with one Saturday per month
Open to full time or even part time 2 days a week

Job Summary:

Provides prescribed dental hygiene/periodontal services to patients in a caring and professional manner.  Perform all necessary oral prophylactic services including initial examination, x-rays, scaling, polishing, fluoride treatments, pit and fissure sealant, subgingival root planning and curettage. Educate patients in the proper methods of oral hygiene and plaque control.

 

Clinical

  • Provides oral prophylaxis, scaling and root planning, sealants, dental and periodontal charting and topical preventive treatments for the patient population.  To meet productivity targets quarterly
  • Provides appropriate individualized patient education for maintenance of oral health.  Emphasizes preventive techniques and compliance with recall intervals.
  • Provides radiographic services.
  • Reviews patient’s medical history prior to start of visit. Maintains accurate documentation of dental hygiene services provided and a hygiene assessment including periodontal charting by completion of the electronic dental record at the end of each visit.
  • Maintains the treatment area, equipment, instruments and supplies in proper working order daily.  Strictly follows safety and infection control standards.
  • Shares responsibility for the maintenance of the logbook required documentation and task list including the autoclave with the dental assistant at a fixed site.
  • Ensures all electronic charting, documentation, and telephone encounters are in compliance with agency standards.

 

Administrative

  • Participates in the assessment of dental services and quality assurance program.
  • Maintains regular communication with the dental director, staff dentists and office manager. 

 

 

Qualifications:

  • RDH degree from accredited dental hygiene school required.
  • Licensure – State of Connecticut dental hygiene license.
  • Minimum of 1 year clinical experience preferred
  • Interest in providing care to diverse patient population.
  • Commitment to preventive dental care.
  • For Mobile Dental program: Reliable access to an automobile and a valid driver’s license or reliable access to transportation to assigned work areas.   
  • Written and oral bilingual fluency in language spoken by our patient population (for example, English/Spanish) strongly preferred.
  • Requires as a condition of employment current American Red Cross CPR for the Professional Rescuer and AED (CPR/FPR/AED) certification. The only acceptable alternative is current American Heart Association BLS/AED for Healthcare Providers certification.

job.title: Technical Support Specialist
job.description:
The Deskside Technical Support Specialist is responsible for providing desktop and mobile hardware and software support to high-profile end-users. The role involves resolving a wide variety of requests, inquiries, and complaints using extensive technical knowledge of hardware and software.
 
This individual will serve as the primary IT point of contact, supporting high-touch, high-profile customers, liaising with stakeholders, and driving service excellence through day-to-day actions.
 
Strong customer service skills, including empathy, clear communication, and prompt issue resolution, are essential, as they directly impact the user experience by fostering trust, reducing downtime, and ensuring that high-profile clients feel supported and valued at every interaction.
 
The successful candidate will have a strong knowledge of the end-user computing ecosystem and an ability to quickly learn applications and services. A high level of emotional intelligence (EQ) is necessary to succeed in this role, as it enables the specialist to navigate high-pressure situations, respond with empathy, and build strong relationships with high-profile users.
 
Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Assist users in resolving computer-related issues such as inoperative hardware or software, including network connections. Resolve customer inquiries efficiently and effectively through active listening and personalized techniques with a consistent focus on service excellence.
  • Hardware support functions include, but are not limited to, end-user support, desktop support, laptop support, office setups, and mobile device support. Perform computer imaging and post-image configurations, preventative maintenance, and basic printer troubleshooting while coordinating with vendors for repairs.
  • Software support functions include, but are not limited to, troubleshooting applications standard within the firm’s environment, installation and configuration of software, updates and preventative maintenance, and support of third-party applications in coordination with vendors.
  • Identify inefficient IT processes, recommend improvements, and implement solutions using Microsoft tools such as PowerAutomate, PowerShell, and PowerApps to enhance support efficiency and internal IT collaboration.
  • Manage deployed and inventory assets, maintaining accurate asset records for each user and each piece of equipment. Coordinate and perform hardware moves, changes, and exchanges.
  • Provide break/fix support for telephony equipment, including handsets, telephone cords, and telephone base units.
  • Assist with projects such as hardware or software upgrades, office moves, and special events.
  • Manage and process hardware warranty claims and issues, and facilitate warranty repairs with third-party vendors.
  • Maintain accurate records of user requests and incidents in the IT Service Management System.
  • Perform other duties as required.

Qualifications:
Education and/or Experience:
Required:

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience
  • A minimum of 4 years of experience providing direct and exceptional user support in a high performance culture, including:
    • Practical, working knowledge of how to support and troubleshoot Zoom, Intune, Okta, ProofPoint, O365 Applications (Outlook, Word, PowerPoint, SharePoint, Teams), NetDocs, Citrix, ServiceNow
    • Experience supporting Windows-based computers, including Microsoft Windows 10/11
    • Experience supporting iOS, Android and MDM solutions
    • Experience working with vendors on support cases

Preferred:

  • Law Firm or Professional Services experience
  • A+, ITIL and Microsoft Office Application certifications
  • PowerAutomate/PowerShell/Python scripting
  • Experience with RPA tools, Microsoft Graph and Microsoft PowerApps
  • Knowledge of AI Prompt Engineering, with an understanding of how to craft effective prompts to optimize interactions with AI tools

Other Skills and Abilities:
The following will also be required of the successful candidate:

  • Strong organizational skills
  • Strong attention to detail
  • Good judgment
  • Strong interpersonal communication skills
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Able to work harmoniously and effectively with others
  • Able to preserve confidentiality and exercise discretion

job.title: Distribution Operator
job.description:
Hours Sunday – Thursday 3:00pm- 12:00AM
Pay $19 /hr 
TTH

Operations

  • Pulls orders and examines outgoing shipments to ensure shipments meet specifications, checking for correctness and accuracy of count (Perfect Order.)
  • Operates forklift to load and unload trucks, move, convey, transport, or locate product within the facility, using electronic equipment, scanning bar coded product and locations. Clean and perform routine maintenance of forklifts daily.
  • Operates various bar code-oriented equipment to complete assigned tasks within the facility.
  • Using hand-held and tethered data collection devices, compares identifying information and counts, weighs, or measures items of incoming and outgoing shipments to verify information against bill of lading, orders, or other distribution documents.
  • Arranges all finished product and incoming materials in the warehouse in an orderly manner, according to warehouse location plan.
  • Examines incoming shipments, rejects damaged items, records shortages, and reports the damages and shortages to supervisor.
  • Conducts inventory cycle counts as directed and assists in taking periodic physical inventory.
  • Reports and repacks according to SOP’s any damage which occurs, including damage to the finished product, to the supervisor.
  • Responsible for working scheduled hours to include Saturday and/or Sunday to support the shuttle of product/material between the DC and Production at Canal St (24/7.)
  • Completes small package paperwork, as required, for UPS, FedEx, etc. performing computer data entry for small packages where applicable.
  • Must maintain regular and consistent attendance.
  • #toponehire

job.title: Supplier Quality Engineer
job.description:
SUPPLIER QUALITY ENGINEER
? North Haven, CT
? Compensation: $110,000 – $120,000
WWW.MOSSBERG.COM 

Under the direction of the Director of Quality, the Supplier Quality Engineer is responsible for planning and executing supplier quality activities, including supplier evaluation, development, maintenance, and continuous process improvement. This role plays a critical part in driving quality excellence across the supply chain.

Key Responsibilities

  • Maintain and continuously improve the Supplier Quality Manual

  • Guide RII personnel in daily priorities and troubleshooting

  • Analyze and monitor supplier quality escapes and corrective actions

  • Participate in critical supplier reviews and performance evaluations

  • Prepare and present supplier quality metrics and KPIs

  • Monitor annual supplier surveys and certification expirations

  • Examine products to ensure conformance to quality standards

  • Perform testing using precision measuring instruments (electronic and manual)

  • Evaluate product characteristics such as color, finish, dimensions, hardness, and density

  • Prepare and analyze root cause and corrective action reports

  • Monitor incoming inspection results and manage M.R.B. data and disposition

  • Maintain the Material Review Area to ensure timely product flow

  • Conduct quality system, process, and product audits at supplier locations

  • Maintain all quality records (electronic and hard copy, as required)

  • Develop, modify, and recommend sampling and inspection procedures

  • Collaborate with suppliers to improve performance, implement process controls, and develop quality assurance plans

  • Oversee Receiving Inspectors to ensure efficient departmental workflow

  • Prepare supplier scorecards and maintain supplier risk rating systems

  • Prepare operations reviews covering Supplier Quality KPIs, action plans, and audit schedules

  • Perform additional duties as assigned

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical Engineering or equivalent

  • Strong working knowledge of Quality Management Systems (QMS) and industry standards

  • Minimum of 3+ years of relevant experience in a similar role

  • Experience in metals manufacturing strongly preferred

Working Conditions

  • Office environment within a manufacturing facility

Physical Requirements

  • Ability to lift, move, and transport items weighing up to 20 lbs

  • Ability to operate a computer and office equipment for a minimum of 6 hours per day

  • Ability to remain stationary for extended periods and move throughout the facility as needed

  • Ability to detect and correct errors on paper and computer screens

  • Ability to communicate effectively verbally, nonverbally, and in writing

Additional Information

  • Management reserves the right to assign or reassign duties at any time

  • Job description may be modified as business needs evolve

  • Must be able to perform essential job functions in accordance with ADA, FMLA, and applicable regulations

  • Regular, punctual attendance is required

  • Experience with Lean Manufacturing or a willingness to learn and participate is preferred

  • #toponehire

job.title: Dermatologist (MD/DO)
job.description:
Dermatologist – Outpatient

Schedule: Full-Time
Compensation Range: $330-554k based on experience
Sign on bonus/relocation

Position Overview

A large, mission-driven healthcare organization in Massachusetts is seeking a Board-Certified or Board-Eligible Dermatologist to join a growing outpatient dermatology practice. This role offers the opportunity to deliver high-quality, patient-centered dermatologic care within a collaborative, multidisciplinary environment that values clinical excellence and equitable access to care.

The ideal candidate will provide a broad range of general dermatology services and contribute to the continued growth of the dermatology program. Practice focus may be tailored based on clinical interests.

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide comprehensive outpatient dermatologic care, including medical, surgical, and cosmetic dermatology

  • Diagnose and manage a wide range of dermatologic conditions

  • Collaborate with multidisciplinary clinical teams

  • Deliver culturally competent, patient-centered care to a diverse patient population

  • Participate in program development and quality initiatives as applicable

Practice Highlights

  • Full-time outpatient role

  • Ability to shape the practice around areas of interest (medical, procedural, cosmetic, or complex dermatology)

  • Strong clinical support, including nursing and medical assistant staff

  • Access to modern facilities and advanced technology

  • Collegial, team-based environment

Qualifications

  • MD or DO from an accredited medical school

  • Board Certified or Board Eligible in Dermatology

  • Eligible for medical licensure in Massachusetts

  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills

  • Commitment to high-quality, equitable patient care

Compensation & Benefits

  • Competitive, market-aligned compensation structure

  • Potential sign-on bonus (negotiable)

  • Partial relocation assistance available

  • Comprehensive benefits package, including health, dental, vision, and retirement

  • Paid CME time and allowance

  • Generous paid time off

  • Professional liability insurance with tail coverage

job.title: Director of Sales – Sales Director – Food Manufacturing – 150K+
job.description:
Sales Director – Food Manufacturing – 150K+

Director of Sales works with the President to develop, communicate and implement the sales strategy for the company. The Director manages the sales team, providing guidance and assistance to help them meet sales objectives. Working independently and with the President, the Director of Sales identifies annual, quarterly, and monthly sales revenue objectives by key account, region, product line, salesperson, and broker.  
 
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Develops and presents annual sales plan for the company identifying key revenue opportunities by key account, region, product line, salesperson and broker. 
  • Monitors company performance to sales objectives through scheduling and conducting monthly, quarterly and annual meetings with the leadership team focused on actual results to plan and the identification of causes for sales shortfalls.  Recommends and implements remedial actions to ensure forecasted sales are attained.
  • Conducts market and competitor research; stays up to date on the latest industry trends and best practices through competitive intelligence;  finds new leads and gets referrals through networking and contacts.
  • Manages sales team within the organization, setting sales revenue objectives and reviewing performance on a monthly, quarterly, and annual basis.  Assists individual salespeople in developing and executing strategies and tactics to achieve sales goals within their regions.  Manages performance of salespeople through regular ride-along mentoring sessions and, where appropriate, models customer-centric sales techniques to help develop skills.
  • Identifies and qualifies distributors for strategic sales partnerships.  Negotiates rates of compensation with brokers; monitors and reviews performance and  relationships to protect the best interests of Boston Salads.
  • Collaborates with production and distribution to ensure customer satisfaction
  • Uses broad knowledge of sales concepts, experience, and analytical skills to resolve complex issues in creative and effective ways and to provide leadership and direction to the company’s year-over-year revenue and profit growth objectives.
  • Performs other special projects, as necessary.

REQUIRED EDUCATION, EXPERIENCE AND APTITUDES

  • Bachelor’s degree in business administration, marketing or related area preferred
  • 7+ years of professional-level food manufacturing sales experience; 3 years of experience managing a sales team; proven record of developing and executing successful sales strategies and achieving sales targets
  • Familiarity with key players in the appropriate industries and talk finances is a huge plus
  • Demonstrated skill at developing effective relationships with key customer decision makers; strong management of varied distributor relationships
  • Ability to analyze data, track KPIs, adjust strategies as needed
  • Computer systems savvy; adept at learning new systems and applications; hands-on and data driven
  • Excellent communicator, both internally and with customers and other business partners
  • As a member of a federally regulated industry, requires pre-employment drug testing, including marijuana.

#toponehire

job.title: Endoscopy Technician
job.description:
Pay: $20-25/hour, depending on experience

Schedule:
Sunday Off
Monday 7:00AM – 5:30PM
Tuesday 10:30AM – 9:00PM
Wednesday 10:30AM – 9:00PM
Thursday Off
Friday 6:30AM – 5:00PM
Every other Saturday, with another day off during the week 

Requirements: 

  • GI Technician Certification preferred
  • BLS Certification
  • 1 year of medical assistant experience or endoscopy/OR experience

Responsibilities:

  • Assist with endoscopic procedures and patient care under the direction of RN
  • Preparation of endoscopic equipment, assisting doctors and nurses during procedures, decontamination and care of endoscopic equipment and supplies
  • Monitoring supplies for reordering, tracking equipment sent out for repair, maintaining decontamination room logbooks
  • Coordinates the efforts of the technicians in all Ors and the Soiled Workroom and reports problems or potential problems to the Nurse Manager
  • Assures correct documentation is recorded
  • Maintains patient confidentiality
  • Ensures procedure room is set up for the day
  • Set up the OR endoscopy area and decontamination room for the day
  • Decontaminates endoscopy room and decontamination room surfaces between each patient and at the end of the day
  • Inspects scopes and accessories daily for proper mechanical functioning before each procedure
  • Leak test scopes after each procedure, before manual cleaning
  • Ensures prompt and thorough room turnover, cleaning, and disinfecting of scopes and accessories
  • Assists RN with patient preparation for and during endoscopy procedure
  • Directs patients to changing area before procedure
  • Assists the physician with biopsy specimens
  • Assists with patient recovery
  • Communicates patient status to appropriate health care member
  • Assists to ambulate patients according to the suites policy and procedure in accordance with the physician’s orders
  • Walks patients to changing area and to car when directed by RN
  • Assists patients on and off stretchers
  • Decontaminates stretcher and remakes for next patient
  • Restocks OR, decontamination room, clean and soiled utility rooms, pre-op and post-op areas, patient changing areas, employee locker room and rest room
  • Ensures adequate number of supplies on hand and anticipates future needs and orders appropriately
  • Assists other health care members as needed and when asked
  • Adheres to procedures, instructions, and established routines
  • Reports concerns and errors to Nurse Manager
  • Provide courteous service to patients, families, and staff
  • Adheres to protective required wear when assisting and performing decontamination and any other time when exposure to blood born pathogens or chemicals present a hazard
  • Other duties as assigned

#toponehire

job.title: Operations Manager
job.description:
Job Title: Operations Manager

Reports To: CEO
Location: Guilford, CT (On-site)

Summary of Position

The Operations Manager is responsible for planning, organizing, and controlling materials, workflow, and shipments related to current, pending, and future sales orders. This role oversees production planning, purchasing, inventory control, warehousing, and shipping while ensuring operational efficiency, on-time delivery, quality, safety, and cost control. The Operations Manager serves as a key cross-functional liaison between departments to support business objectives and customer commitments.

Duties and Responsibilities

  • Drive performance to meet or exceed established KPIs related to On-Time Delivery (OTD), Quality, Environmental, and Safety standards.

  • Design and implement plans for the effective use of labor and material resources to achieve operational targets.

  • Supervise Production, Purchasing, and Warehouse/Shipping teams to ensure operations remain on schedule and within budget.

  • Partner with Quality Assurance to ensure product quality and compliance with specifications.

  • Develop and maintain rolling quarterly production plans aligned with customer demand and material availability.

  • Lead daily shop-floor stand-up meetings to review prior-day performance, resolve issues, and establish daily priorities.

  • Prepare and maintain operational reports, including schedule adherence, downtime, scrap/waste, NCPs, cycle times, and ship-to-promise performance.

  • Establish and implement departmental and organizational policies and procedures to maximize output and efficiency.

  • Monitor, record, and analyze materials movement and operational expenditures.

  • Maintain optimal inventory levels to support on-time customer deliveries.

  • Establish procedures and oversee physical inventories and cycle count programs to ensure high inventory accuracy.

  • Ensure compliance with company standard practices and operating procedures across all materials and operations functions.

  • Oversee procurement of components, materials, and equipment required for production.

  • Manage purchasing, receiving, warehousing, and distribution operations through effective policies and controls.

  • Maintain warehouse facilities by planning layouts, inspecting equipment, and coordinating repairs or replacements.

  • Plan and coordinate logistics and transportation to meet customer delivery requirements.

  • Ensure timely release of work orders and production tickets.

  • Plan and execute implementation of Engineering Change Notices (ECNs) impacting purchasing, production, and inventory.

  • Perform other duties as required to support operational excellence and business goals.

Qualifications and Education Requirements

  • Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited university; Business or related field preferred.

  • 5+ years of experience in an operations or management role within a manufacturing or distribution environment.

  • Expert-level experience in production planning, warehouse operations, inventory control, purchasing, and materials management.

  • Strong leadership presence with the ability to lead by example and promote accountability and engagement.

  • Proven ability to manage priorities and perform under tight and changing deadlines.

  • Excellent communication, organizational, and cross-functional collaboration skills.

  • Self-directed, detail-oriented, and results-driven with strong problem-solving abilities.

  • Ability to work independently while effectively leading teams to completion.

  • Experience building and managing relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including sales, quality, engineering, and executive leadership.

Compensation & Benefits

  • Base Salary: $150,000

  • Annual Bonus: Target 20%

  • Long-Term Incentive: 20% long-term investment participation

  • Comprehensive benefits package included

#toponehire

job.title: FInance Manager-
job.description:
The Finance Manager plays a critical leadership role in overseeing the financial operations, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and process improvement. This position manages a strong accounting team. The Finance Manager leads the preparation and analysis of financial statements, supports budgeting and forecasting efforts, and collaborates closely with the Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) team.

Skills desired:
GAAP reporting, regulatory compliance, and financial analysis, insurance-specific accounting standards, budgeting cycles.

The ideal candidate brings Accounting, Analysis, and process improvement skills to the table!

Leadership & Team Development

  • Provides direct leadership to assigned team of accountants, career progression, etc.

Financial Reporting & Analysis

  • Leads the process to generate and analyze monthly financial statements for Finance management.
  • Manages the process of entries to general ledger accounts and ensures compliant and timely journal entries.
  • Tracks monthly accruals, monitoring for accuracy/trends, and participates in year-end closing.
  • Leads the team in supporting executive management with financial reports and ad hoc analysis based on trends, business needs, or other prioritization.
  • Provides written analysis of large variances and identifies trends/discrepancies, with root-cause analysis. Proposes actionable steps to correct variances/discrepancies and outlines mitigation process to reduce variances in the future.

Budgeting & Forecasting Support

  • Collaborates with FP&A team and assigned business lines to optimize budgeting and forecasting.
  • Identifies variances from budget, performs root-cause analysis, determines best action to correct, and informs senior management.
  • Proactively anticipates business line needs and delivers relevant financial data to guide operational and strategic decisions.
  • Collaborates with FP&A to ensure financial results are accurately reflected across geographies and business lines.

Compliance, Controls & Process Improvement

  • Works with internal stakeholders to resolve accounting discrepancies while upholding AAA Five Diamond Service Standards.
  • Researches complex accounting issues and ensures adherence to internal policies and external standards such as GAAP.
  • Identifies procedural gaps that could lead to fraud and recommends safeguards.
  • Proposes improvements to accounting systems and practices to boost efficiency and accuracy.

Professional Development & Industry Awareness

  • Maintains professional and technical knowledge. Promotes a culture of learning by disseminating insights from industry developments to the broader team.
  • Tracks emerging financial reporting practices and updates internal processes accordingly.
  • Assesses when to use internal vs. external resources and advises leadership on cost-effective solutions.
  • Evaluates and manages relationships with external service providers to support Club initiatives.

Qualifications

Education Bachelor’s in accounting required
Certificates CPA required
Experience 7+ years of accounting experience required; 5+ years preparing financial statements

Requirements Advanced familiarity with the Microsoft Office 

Ability to develop various models to analyze the cost / benefit of entering into a new business venture.

Demonstrated customer service attitude required, with ability to build effective relationships with senior and staff accountants, along with all levels of senior management.

Drive and persistence to handle technical accounting issues in a fast-paced/deadline-oriented environment.

. . . Pay Statement and Pay Transparency Laws: rate of pay listed is on a per hour basis. You will be entitled to health insurance once employed for 60 days and you must work 30 hours per week, on average, per month to enroll and maintain health insurance through Complete Staffing Solutions. job.title: Legal Client Services Manager
job.description:
Legal Client Services Manager

Location: Los Angeles, CA

Client: Top-Tier Global Law Firm (Confidential)

Position Overview
Our client, a leading global law firm serving major financial institutions and companies worldwide, is seeking a Legal Client Services Manager. This is a unique opportunity to drive operational excellence and innovation within a high-profile disputes practice.

As part of the firm’s global Legal Project Management (LPM) function, you will oversee the end-to-end delivery of complex matters, streamline workflows, and implement client-facing innovations, including data visualization and Power BI dashboards. This role is pivotal in guiding local counsel teams, leading strategic financial analysis, and collaborating across Finance, Billing, and Business Development functions.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership: Collaborate with leadership to strengthen the firm’s legal operations and LPM capabilities, influencing Partners and lawyers to adopt best practices and LPM principles.

Operational Management: Manage the full lifecycle of legal matters, including arranging kick-off and steering meetings, defining scope, and validating project milestones and deliverables.

Financial Oversight: Manage matter budgets and financials, tracking progress and escalating changes in scope or budget to ensure effective corrective action. Collaborate with legal teams to draft budget proposals and track actuals against estimates.

Process Improvement: Utilizing lean principles, contribute to the continuous improvement of LPM methodology, governance, and training. Lead post-project reviews and “lessons learned” workshops.

Stakeholder Management: Act as the primary interface for project stakeholders, including clients, co-counsel, opposing counsel, and third-party vendors. Build strong collaborative relationships between the firm’s legal teams and local counsel across jurisdictions.

Innovation & Tooling: Utilize project management tools to draft artifacts such as project plans, risk logs, and status reports. Support the creation of LPM collateral for bids and pitches to highlight the firm’s innovative service delivery.

Team Management: Provide line management and mentorship to junior team members and provide LPM guidance and training to lawyers and clients.

Qualifications & Experience

Experience: 5+ years of relevant work experience, including client-facing roles. Significant experience managing project teams and supporting complex, multi-jurisdictional matters in a dedicated project management capacity is required.

Education: Strong academic record required; a Law Degree or relevant Postgraduate Degree is advantageous.

Certification: Formal project management qualification is essential (e.g., PMP, APMG).

Technical Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Visio, Project) and Power BI is required. Knowledge of extranets, e-billing software, and legal tech tools is expected.

Core Competencies:

Excellent financial analysis skills with an understanding of law firm performance metrics.

Strong change management experience.

Ability to manage multiple concurrent priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Demonstrated ability to negotiate with and persuade senior business stakeholders.
. . . Pay Statement and Pay Transparency Laws: rate of pay listed is on a per hour basis. You will be entitled to health insurance once employed for 60 days and you must work 30 hours per week, on average, per month to enroll and maintain health insurance through Complete Staffing Solutions. Once both qualifiers are met you must complete the enrollment packet and your benefits will start on the first of the subsequent month. . . job.title: SR Accountant, Real Estate
job.description:
Position Summary

This role requires strong GAAP knowledge, hands-on experience with lease accounting, and proficiency in MRI. The ideal candidate will be detail-oriented, analytical, and comfortable working in a fast-paced, multi-entity environment.

  • Support construction and development accounting, including job cost tracking and capitalization
  • Reconcile balance sheet accounts and investigate variances
  • Assist with audits, tax preparation support, and internal reporting requests
  • Collaborate with property management, asset management, and operations teams to ensure accurate financial reporting
  • Identify process improvements and support accounting best practices

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (required)
  • 5+ years of accounting experience, preferably within real estate, property management, or construction
  • Strong experience with full-cycle accounting
  • Proven experience supporting multi-location and multi-entity accounting environments
  • Solid understanding of GAAP
  • MRI or Yardi experience is required
  • Experience with lease accounting strongly preferred
  • Experience preparing or reviewing CAM reconciliations is a plus
  • Advanced Excel skills and strong analytical abilities
  • Excellent attention to detail, organizational, and communication skills

Preferred Skills

  • CPA or CPA-eligible a plus
  • Ability to manage competing deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong problem-solving and process improvement mindset

The ideal candidate will be detail-oriented, analytical, and comfortable working in a fast-paced, multi-entity environment.

job.title: Senior Platform & Automation Engineer
job.description:
The Platform & Automation Senior Engineer is responsible for managing and maintaining enterprise automation platforms, including but not limited to ServiceNow, to ensure efficient and effective operations. This role involves accountability in configuring platforms, implementing new functionalities, maintaining system stability, and ensuring data integrity. The engineer collaborates with various stakeholders to understand their requirements, suggest, define, and create technical solutions, and support ongoing projects and operations.

About this role:

  • Engages with stakeholders to gather requirements, design and deliver solutions that align with customer needs.
  • Responsible for system performance and for ensuring high availability, reliability, and optimal performance of automation platforms, including ServiceNow.
  • Responsible for configuring and customizing platform modules, such as Incident, Problem, Change, Asset, and Configuration Management.
  • Creates and configures notifications, define and implement workflows, business rules, UI actions, and automation to improve efficiency.
  • Proposes, designs and implements enhancements to automation platforms to optimize functionality and improve the user experience.
  • Leverages the AI component of Service now to gain business efficiencies.
  • Develops software solutions using JavaScript, Web Services, HTML, and CSS within automation environments.
  • Manages the health of CMDB and other data repositories in collaboration with the Infrastructure team to support assets and configuration.
  • Responsible for application support activities, including code migrations, replications, patching, and platform upgrades.
  • Utilizes data import templates for data loading and extraction to support reporting and analytics needs.
  • Responsible comprehensive process and knowledge documentation for ITIL and end user.
  • Coaches and mentors junior developers
  • Assists with data entry and validation from source documents into the automation platform database.
  • Extracts and transform data into formats suitable for reporting or further analysis.
  • Supports the development and configuration of custom applications within the ServiceNow and other automation platforms.
  • Troubleshoots and resolves technical issues in collaboration with other team members.
  • Ensures platform stability by adhering to best practices, standards, and governance policies.
  • Participates in training opportunities and self-learning to deepen knowledge of automation platforms, including ServiceNow.
  • Collaborates on team projects, contributing ideas to improve workflows and processes across the platform.
  • Displays professionalism, quality service and a positive “can do” attitude to internal members/departments of the Firm.
  • Assumes additional responsibilities as assigned.
 

Required Skills

  • ServiceNow Administrator (CSA) is desirable.
  • Additional certifications in ServiceNow modules (ITSM, CSM, ITOM, etc.) are preferred but training is available.
  • Strong understanding of ITIL/ITSM principles.
  • Proficient in JavaScript, HTML, CSS, and other relevant scripting languages.
  • Strong experience with REST/SOAP APIs and integration tools.
  • Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills including ability to communicate to all levels of end users, IS and Management staff.
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

Education:

  • Advanced Scripting/ Reporting and other classes area highly recommended
  • Bachelor’s degree

Required Experience:

  • 5+ years’ application design, development, and support experience in a large scale, complex environment
  • At least 3 years of experience with the ServiceNow tool, or a similar automation tool, strongly preferred.
  • Workflow and process improvement methodology and technology solution experience strongly preferred.
  • Law firm experience is preferred.

job.title: Staff Accountant

Position Overview
We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Staff Accountant to join our accounting team. The ideal candidate will have strong accounting fundamentals, solid experience with accounts payable, cost accounting, and accruals, and the ability to work independently with minimal supervision. This role goes beyond basic bookkeeping and supports essential financial operations within our growing organization.
Key Responsibilities

  • Support accruals and cost accounting processes, ensuring accurate recording of expenses, adjustments, and period-end allocations.
  • Prepare and post monthly journal entries, including accruals, prepaids, and inventory adjustments.
  • Assist with account reconciliations, identifying discrepancies and recommending corrective action.
  • Aid in month-end and year-end close processes to ensure timely and accurate financial reporting.
  • Work cross-functionally with operations, purchasing, and finance teams to provide accounting support and resolve issues.
  • Maintain accurate and orderly financial records in the company’s ERP system; recommend process improvements where applicable.
  • Support internal and external audits.
  • Assist with special projects and ad-hoc analysis as needed.

Required Qualifications

  • 3–4+ years of accounting experience, preferably in a corporate accounting environment.
  • Strong accounting knowledge beyond bookkeeping, with an understanding of GAAP principles.
  • Accounts Payable experience required.
  • Experience with cost accounting and accruals.
  • Comfort working with ERP systems (experience with custom or complex ERP systems is a plus).
  • Excellent analytical skills with strong attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities.
  • Effective communication skills—both written and verbal.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (preferred but not required if experience is strong).
  • Experience in retail, manufacturing, distribution, or consumer packaged goods (CPG) industries.
  • Experience supporting cross-department financial activities and improving accounting processes.

job.title: Sr Maintenance Technician
job.description:
Company: Client 
Location: Latham, NY 
Position: Sr Maintenance Technician 
Comp: $30-$42 hr 

Full job description

A little about the position:

Job Summary
To be a part of the team responsible for making constant improvements in all aspects of the facilities and maintenance department by identifying quality vendors and making constant improvements as required on existing machines. Supporting the Preventive Maintenance system to maximize the PM benefit while minimizing the down time on all equipment and processes. Will assist the Manager in the cross reference of replacement parts and using ones technical abilities to convert the hand parts and needed parts to provide safe functionality while maximizing up time at the facility.
Ensures that the plant and equipment used in the facilities and production are kept running safely with minimal down time while maximizing production and efficiencies. This includes total preventative maintenance, managing breakdowns of mechanical, hydraulic, pneumatic, and electrical on all equipment (including PLC’s, software programming) and planning and projecting down time to perform needed repairs while minimizing efficiency losses.
Some of What You Will Do:

  • Respond to machine breakdowns swiftly and identify the best practice in bringing the equipment up as quickly and safely as possible. Identify the root cause of issues and problem solve plant issues to keep the plant and production running.
  • Complete Preventative Maintenance tasks assigned to keep equipment up and running. This could include writing new PM’s, adding to existing PM’s, Increasing or decreasing frequencies using the senior techs skills as to the appropriate timelines.
  • Perform the movement, installation, repositioning of machines and equipment per plant engineer specs. Help the plant Maintenance Manager and plant engineer with equipment decision on a need base.
  • Work within 5S (Sort, Set, Shine, Standardize, Sustain) methodology to maintain a clean and organized workplace.
  • Undertake special projects as required.
  • Support training needs of the department and provide expertise to apprentices and trainees.
  • Train other maintenance staff as required
  • Operate effectively in a team environment and being willing to be heard and to listen at appropriate times.
  • Keep a positive mental attitude to induce a good teamwork environment.
  • Be reliable and accountable for your actions.
  • Always works safely following LOTO and all EH&S guidelines and rules.
  • Stays busy keeping projects and list work ready to go when the opportunity arises.
  • Utilize learned skillsets to impact any project, PM or task to constantly improve all aspect of required duties.

What You Bring to the Table: (Qualifications)

  • Holds a Technical or Vocational certificate or 5+ years’ experience in electrical/mechanical trades
  • Must have strong interpersonal, communication, organization and time management skills
  • Must have solid understanding of Preventative Maintenance Systems / CMMS
  • Always ensures a safe work environment exists for all employees, ensuring all EH&S and quality management procedures are followed at all times
  • Must be able to perform heavy lifting, pushing and pulling- approx. 50 plus pounds
  • Must be a self- motivated and self- driven independent worker
  • Able to work overtime, off-shifts and weekends as required
  • Basic computer skills / knowledge of MS programs such as Excel, Word, PP, Etc
  • Highly effective problem-solving skills.
  • Strong mechanical aptitude
  • Understand, train, and maintain the adherence to all LOTO procedures.
  • Flexibility and ability to multitask working on different projects and priorities throughout a day keeping a pleasant demeanor during stressful and demanding times.
  • Competency in basic hydraulic and pneumatic, theory and troubleshooting
  • Must have a solid understanding of electrical principles and fundamentals
  • Highly knowledgeable in single and three phase electrical theory
  • Must have knowledge of the following: components, devices and equipment used in industrial equipment applications and the related electronics including AC and DC motors, motor controls, electronic drives, PLC, instrumentation, and sensors etc.
  • Carry out the diagnostics of fault codes and the maintenance and repair of all types of facilities and production equipment in a safe, timely and professional manner returning equipment to operational service
  • Able to read/draw mechanical and electrical blueprints, ladder logic and schematics
  • Follow NFPA 79 Electrical Standard for Industrial Machinery and NFPA 70E Standard for Electrical Safety in the Workplace
  • Strong ability to troubleshoot train and repair equipment and system problems – both routine and complex to determine root cause
  • Diagnose problems, plan and complete work required, identify repair parts
  • Understand MRO forms and know how to procure parts needed

Why should you come work with Us?
Comprehensive Benefits for you and your family include:

  • Medical, dental, and vision insurance
  • HSA and FSA plans available
  • 401(K) with 50% company match up to 6%
  • Paid Time Off – 3 to 5 weeks per year with weekly accruals starting day one
  • Holidays – 11 Paid, eligible on day one
  • Life/ADD Insurance and Short/Long Term Disability insurance with buy up options

Professional Development: Training programs, Tuition Reimbursement, and growth opportunities.
Supportive Environment: A culture rooted in collaboration, integrity, and respect.
Some Important Information for you:
Position Title: Senior Maintenance Technician
Location: Latham, NY
Employment Type: On-site
Status: Full-time
Salary Range: $30-$42 dollars per hour
Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across US geographic markets. Starting pay for this role will vary based on multiple factors, including location, knowledge, skills, education, and experience. Pay ranges may be modified in the future.

Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status.
The client is a Drug-Free Workplace Employer. All offers of employment are subject to applicable pre-employment requirements.

job.title: COSTING MANUFACTURING ENGINEER
job.description:
The Cost Engineer will develop cost estimates during the quoting process for new products and maintain accurate standard costs for existing products. This role involves collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data related to materials, labor, tooling, and equipment to ensure accurate cost forecasting. The Cost Engineer will collaborate with cross-functional teams to optimize costs, improve efficiency, and support strategic decision-making for both new and legacy products.
Essential Responsibilities
· Utilize cost methodologies and tools to prepare and maintain reliable and accurate cost data.
· Identify and quantify potential cost uncertainties to ensure costing models capture the full range of risks.
· Establish cost estimates for production processes and tooling; review alternatives and recommend improvements.
· Track actual costs against estimates and report regularly to ensure alignment with forecasts.
· Investigate and identify cost reduction opportunities through detailed cost analysis.
· Contribute to cost monitoring and reporting systems; analyze cost trends and variances.
· Review monthly manufacturing variance reports to analyze deviations and adjust costs as needed.
· Coordinate with project management, engineering, procurement, and other stakeholders to ensure cost objectives are met.
· Analyze technical documents, blueprints, and vendor quotations to develop detailed cost estimates.
· Identify and mitigate potential project risks and their cost impact.
· Develop and implement cost optimization strategies and continuous improvement initiatives.
· Collaborate with sales, operations, and procurement teams to align financial and project objectives.
· Analyze and improve production efficiency through process evaluation and redesign.
· Design systems that integrate workers, materials, and machines to streamline operations.
· Lead and manage projects focused on process improvement and efficiency (Lean methodologies preferred).
· Use statistical tools and software to analyze production data, forecast trends, and support data-driven decisions.
· Support operations and customer initiatives through special projects as assigned.
Education & Experience
· Bachelor’s degree in manufacturing, Mechanical, Industrial Engineering, or related discipline (or equivalent experience).
· 5–7 years of related experience in cost engineering, estimating, or manufacturing operations.
· Knowledge of metal stamping, heat treating, plating, welding, mechanical assembly, and CNC machining preferred.
· Proficiency in ERP systems (Syteline or similar), Microsoft Excel, SolidWorks, Outlook, and MS Project.
· Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
· Excellent written and verbal communication abilities.
· Self-motivated, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities under tight deadlines.

job.title: Senior Quality Management Systems Engineer
job.description:
Overview
The Senior Quality Management Systems Engineer is responsible for maintaining, improving, and ensuring compliance of the company’s Quality Management System in accordance with ISO 9001, ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and other applicable regulatory requirements.  This role supports continuous improvement initiatives, internal and customer audits, CAPA processes, and documentation control to ensure product quality and regulatory compliance.
This position is responsible for developing and maintaining the company’s QMS documentation including SOPs, work instructions, and related forms and specifications The position is also responsible for ensuring individuals within the company are effectively trained to the QMS documentation applicable to their roles and responsibilities.
Principal Responsibilities
Quality System Maintenance & Compliance

  • Maintain and improve the company’s Quality Management System (QMS) to ensure compliance with ISO 9001, ISO 13485, FDA QSR, MDR, and other applicable global regulations.
  • Develop, revise, and implement quality system procedures, work instructions, and forms.
  • Support management review system through the monitoring of quality data, development and analysis of quality metrics, and development and presentation of actionable reports on the status of product quality and QMS compliance.
  • Initiate QMS improvements, as needed.

Audits, CAPAs, NCMRs, and Complaints

  • Prepare for and support internal, supplier, and external audits (e.g., FDA, Notified Body, Customers).
  • Manage and support CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action), Nonconforming Material Reports (NCMR), and Complaint processes.
  • Perform internal audits and follow up on findings to ensure timely and effective closure of corrective actions.
  • Facilitate root cause analysis, effectiveness verification, and trend analysis.

Training & Documentation

  • Support QMS training programs and ensure employee understanding of quality system requirements.
  • Oversee document control and record management systems.
  • Participate in the evaluation of change control activities to ensure proper documentation and regulatory impact assessments.

Design Assurance

  • Support product development projects, including assurance that design control requirements are met at all stages of a product’s development.
  • Support risk management processes in accordance with ISO 14971.

Supplier Quality

  • Perform supplier evaluations, including audits where appropriate and support supplier performance monitoring initiatives.

Training, Skills, Knowledge and/or Experience

  • 8+ years of quality engineering experience in the medical device industry. Prior defense/aerospace industry experience & manufacturing engineering experience a plus.
  • Fluent in ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, ISO 14971, and MDSAP requirements.
  • Experience using electronic QMS tools (e.g., QCBD, MasterControl, Arena, etc.).
  • Fluent in Microsoft Office 365 Suite (i.e. Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access, Teams, SharePoint, etc.)
  • Familiarity with ERP and PLM systems preferred

Competencies

  • Project management and leadership skills, with proven experience leading and mentoring individuals across different levels and functions to investigate the root causes of internal and external audit findings and to implement effective and compliance corrective actions in a timely manner.
  • Excellent verbal and written communications with ability to effectively communicate at multiple levels in the organization.
  • Ability to organize and prioritize multiple tasks and meet deadlines, with attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Ability to work within a team and as an individual contributor in a fast-paced, changing environment.
  • Ability to analyze quality data, develop associated metrics, identify trends, and develop actionable reports in support of Management Reviews
  • Strong organizational, interpersonal and presentation skills.

Supervisory Responsibility
None.
Educational Requirements 

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering or other technical field or an equivalent combination of education and work experience.
  • Advanced degree or certifications (e.g., ASQ CQE, CQA, ISO 13485 Lead Auditor) preferred.

Working Conditions

  • Office and controlled manufacturing environments, including manufacturing and engineering laboratory environments and ISO class 7 cleanrooms.  PPE required when in controlled environment rooms and cleanrooms.
  • May require occasional travel (<10%) related to audit activities.

job.title: Senior Data Vault Builder (REMOTE ROLE)
job.description:
Senior Data Vault Builder (Remote – EST Hours)
Compensation: $160,000 – $175,000
Location: Fully Remote (U.S. – Eastern Time Zone)
Category: Information Technology

About the Opportunity

We’re seeking an experienced Senior Data Vault Builder to design, implement, and maintain enterprise-scale Data Vault solutions supporting analytical and operational data needs. This is a hands-on technical position focused on architecting and optimizing Raw Vault, Business Vault, and Info Mart layers using Data Vault 2.0 methodologies.

This opportunity is ideal for a seasoned data professional with deep expertise in VaultSpeed (or WhereScape 3D), strong SQL skills, and a solid understanding of data modeling, lineage, and performance optimization—particularly within Snowflake environments.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design, build, and maintain Raw Vault components (hubs, links, satellites) using VaultSpeed or similar tooling.

  • Develop and optimize Business Vault and Info Mart layers to support analytics and reporting.

  • Implement efficient ETL/ELT pipelines using SQL, views, stored procedures, and VaultSpeed-generated code.

  • Translate mapping documents and business rules into scalable Data Vault models.

  • Apply CDC (Change Data Capture) and SCD Type 2 logic for historical tracking.

  • Monitor, troubleshoot, and tune data pipeline performance across all vault layers.

  • Profile and validate source data for design alignment and accuracy.

  • Create clear technical documentation and data flow diagrams from source through vault layers.

  • Ensure compliance with data governance and metadata standards.

  • Collaborate closely with data architects, business SMEs, and analytics teams to ensure business alignment.

Required Skills & Experience

  • 8+ years of professional experience in data engineering or data warehousing.

  • Proven expertise building and maintaining all layers of the Data Vault (Raw, Business, and Info Mart).

  • Proficiency with VaultSpeed or WhereScape 3D (and willingness to complete VaultSpeed training).

  • Advanced SQL development and performance tuning skills.

  • Experience designing and implementing Star Schema / Dimensional Models.

  • Strong understanding of data lineage, governance, and metadata management.

  • Hands-on experience in Snowflake environments.

  • Proficiency in erwin or similar data modeling tools.

  • Ability to provide occasional off-hours support as required.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Data Vault Certified Professional (DVCP) 2.1 certification.

  • Experience integrating with BI or self-service analytics tools.

  • Familiarity with Agile/Scrum development methodologies.

  • Strong communication skills and ability to document complex data flows for both technical and business audiences.

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field.

Compensation & Employment Details

  • Salary Range: $160,000 – $175,000 (based on experience, certifications, and technical proficiency).

  • Schedule: Full-time, remote (must be available to work EST business hours).

  • Work Authorization: U.S. work authorization required; sponsorship not available.

job.title: Accounting and System Analyst

RESPONSIBILITIES

· Provide operational and system support for budgets and financial reporting.
· Coordinate system upgrades and implement enhancements as familiarity with accounting processes grows.
· Identify opportunities to improve data integrity, reporting efficiency, and workflow automation.

REQUIREMENTS
· Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field.
· 3+ years of relevant experience in accounting, finance, or a similar role; real estate industry experience preferred.
· Requires knowledge of financial terms, concepts and accounting principles with ability to comprehend, analyze, interpret and extract data from various sources.
· Advanced excel skills, including PivotTables, Power Query, INDEX/MATCH, XLOOKUP, dynamic arrays, and advanced formulas (nested logic functions) for complex data analysis and automation.
· Familiarity with accounting & property management systems (RealPage) preferred.
· Excellent verbal and written communication skills

job.title: Electrical & Computer Technologies Scientific Advisor
job.description:
We are seeking qualified and experienced engineers with strong technical backgrounds in electrical engineering, computer science, software, or physics to join us as Scientific Advisors in our Electrical & Computer Technologies (ECT) group. This position can be based in our Boston, New York, or Washington, DC office or be fully remote. The expected salary range for this position is $80,000 – $120,000 annually.  Actual pay will be determined based on experience and other job-related factors permitted by law.

In this role, candidates will actively apply technical expertise to support the firm and its client’s complex Intellectual Property (“IP”) needs while collaboratively working with attorneys, legal practitioners, and other team members. This position will play a key role in important functions such as the preparation of patent applications and drafting of formal requests to the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (“USPTO”) to grant patents for inventions. 

Responsibilities include:

  • Reviewing technical disclosures – the detailed descriptions of new inventions that help determine whether the client should or can obtain a patent
  • Participating in client and inventor interviews to define a clear understanding of the complexities surrounding new inventions
  • Conducting comprehensive research in patent databases and non-patent literature to ensure the firm’s attorneys and legal practitioners have the necessary information to assess the legal viability and patentability of new inventions
  • Drafting patent applications, including preparing detailed specifications and claims that describe the new inventions and provide support during the application process with the USPTO
  • Assisting firm attorneys and legal practitioners in developing and drafting arguments in response to communications from the USPTO to secure patents

Qualifications:

  • Strong academic credentials including a degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Physics, or a related field of study
  • 2+ years of applicable industry experience in engineering or a related capacity
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, along with the ability to present findings in a professional and organized manner
  • Demonstrated ability to work independently and effectively prioritize while managing multiple deadlines and work requests
  • Sound judgment, maturity, and the ability to work collaboratively with all levels of legal practitioners within the firm’s organization
  • Eligibility to sit for the USPTO registration examination if not already registered.

job.title: EHS MANAGER
job.description:
ROLE: EHS MANAGER
COMP: $130-140K+ PLUS BONUS

Position Overview 
The EH&S Manager will lead the Company’s Environmental, Health, and Safety programs, providing strategic direction, technical expertise, and leadership to ensure compliance with all local, state, and federal regulations. This individual will drive continuous improvement initiatives, strengthen EH&S culture, and support a safe, sustainable, and compliant workplace. 
This position requires a highly motivated, results-driven professional with strong leadership, analytical, and communication skills. A deep understanding of OSHA, EPA, and other regulatory requirements is essential. 
Scope: T
 
Key Responsibilities 

  • Provide leadership across all levels of the organization to strengthen and sustain a positive EH&S culture.
  • Partner with site leadership to ensure compliance with federal, state, and local EH&S regulations.
  • Guide and coach employees on adherence to EH&S policies, procedures, and best practices.
  • Conduct Job Safety Assessments and implement effective controls to minimize hazards, including ergonomic improvements.
  • Lead EH&S audits and compliance reviews, ensuring adherence to policies and regulatory requirements.
  • Manage the worker’s compensation program, including claims management and return-to-work coordination.
  • Collaborate with facilities management and cross-functional teams to ensure EH&S compliance in all operations.
  • Provide timely data, performance trends, and corrective actions to site leadership and EH&S councils.
  • Develop and deliver EH&S training programs to ensure compliance and engagement.
  • Establish and drive action plans to achieve and exceed EH&S performance goals.
  • Lead environmental sustainability initiatives to reduce water and energy use, greenhouse gas emissions, and hazardous waste, while improving recycling and resource efficiency.
  • Oversee and manage hazardous materials and waste programs, including proper storage, handling, transportation, and disposal of specialized chemicals used in manufacturing processes.
  • Develop and maintain emergency response plans and lead response drills for fire, chemical spills, and other critical incidents.
  • Lead and manage the Investigation of all EH&S incidents, near-misses, and accidents to determine root causes and implement effective corrective and preventative actions.
  • Maintain and update all required environmental permits and regulatory reporting, including but not limited to, air emissions, hazardous waste generation, and wastewater discharge (e.g., Toxic Release Inventory (TRI), air permits, and Spill Prevention, Control, and Countermeasure (SPCC) plans).
  • Manage the company’s safety program for machine guarding, lockout/tagout, and confined space entry, which are critical in a manufacturing environment.
  • Establish a proactive safety culture through regular safety walks, behavior-based safety observations, and employee engagement programs.
  • Conduct routine inspections of manufacturing equipment, and facility infrastructure, to identify potential hazards and ensure proper operation.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for regulatory agencies (e.g., MassDEP, OSHA, EPA) during inspections and inquiries.

 
Qualifications & Requirements 
Education & Experience 

  • Bachelor’s degree in Environmental, Health, and Safety or related field; or equivalent combination of education and experience (5+ years).
  • Professional certifications (CSP, CIH, or equivalent) preferred.

Skills & Competencies 

  • Strong leadership and project management skills with the ability to influence across levels and functions.
  • Proven success implementing EH&S programs in manufacturing, warehouse, or R&D environments.
  • Solid knowledge of EH&S regulations, standards, and reporting methodologies.
  • Experience in risk assessment, program evaluation, and auditing.
  • Ability to thrive in a matrixed, fast-paced environment.
  • Experience with managing safety for hazardous materials and an understanding of related regulations (e.g., TURA, TSCA ).
  • Familiarity with fire codes and standards, such as NFPA (National Fire Protection Association), relevant to both products and facility operations. 
  • Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively train employees and communicate complex topics to diverse audiences. 
  • Strong problem-solving skills with a systematic approach to root cause analysis. 
  • Proficiency in MS Office, Outlook, and web-based applications.

#toponehire

job.title: Conflicts Attorny
job.description:
The Conflicts Attorney/or Senior assists with the identification, review, analysis, and reconciliation of all conflicts of interest related to incoming legal personnel Firm-wide. The role likewise advises on the identification and reconciliation of potential and actual conflicts of interest related to new client and matter opening across Firm offices and jurisdictions. The Conflicts Attorney is responsible for the creation and maintenance of Firm conflict waivers and reviews client Outside Counsel Guidelines for Firm compliance. This position reports to the Director of Conflict Services, the Associate General Counsel and General Counsel.

Duties and Responsibilities
 

  • Assist in the identification, resolution, and clearance of potential and actual conflicts of interest as relate to lateral legal personnel new hires and Firm new business.
  • Consult with the Director of Conflict Services and General Counsel’s Office as necessary to facilitate clearance and approval on all potential and actual conflicts of interest.
  • Follow-up with appropriate lawyer(s) and/or Firm personnel as required to perform any and all above functions, including, but not limited to, obtaining conflicts clearances, waivers, Outside Counsel Guidelines and engagement letters.
  • Generate and maintain conflicts waivers in furtherance of conflict resolution for use throughout all levels of the Firm.
  • Review client Outside Counsel Guidelines and assist with the Firm’s compliance therewith as necessary.
  • Assist in the implementation of Ethical Walls as necessary.
  • Assist in the training of new Conflicts Analysts and Conflicts Coordinators on the Model Rules of Professional Conduct.
  • Other duties as assigned.

Qualifications
 

  • JD from an accredited institution required
  • Good standing admission to a state bar required
  • At least two (2) years of demonstrated legal conflicts of interest researching, identification and resolution experience in the Conflicts Department of a major law firm or financial institution required.
  • Exceptional working knowledge of the Model Rules of Professional Conduct across Firm jurisdictions required.
  • Demonstrated ability to analyze and reconcile potential and actual conflicts of interest in a major law firm required.
  • Excellent critical thinking and problem-solving skills required.
  • Demonstrated ability to initiate, follow through and complete time sensitive projects with minimal supervision in a fast-paced global law firm environment required.
  • Demonstrated excellent oral and written business communication skills necessary to interact with Partners, Counsel, Associates and other Firm personnel throughout the Firm’s international offices required.
  • Attention to detail and strong analytical and organizational skills required.
  • Experience analyzing conflicts for complex transactional matters, lateral partners and other legal personnel candidates is strongly preferred.

#toponehire

job.title: CNC – MODELMAKER
job.description:
Model Maker (CNC)

? Location: New Haven, CT
? Compensation: $38.00 – $40.00 per hour (approximately $80,000 annually)
? Schedule: Full-time, Onsite

About the Role:
We are seeking a highly skilled Model Maker (CNC) to join an innovative engineering and product development team in New Haven, CT. This is an exciting opportunity to support the creation of cutting-edge product designs and custom test apparatus by applying your expertise in CNC machining, fabrication, and precision model making.

In this role, you’ll collaborate closely with engineers, designers, and lab technicians in a modern, hands-on model shop environment — transforming engineering concepts into reality.

Responsibilities:

  • Read and interpret detailed engineering drawings to accurately model and fabricate component parts and assemblies.

  • Operate and maintain CNC machinery, milling machines, lathes, surface grinders, and other shop equipment.

  • Create and modify CNC programs using Mastercam, SolidWorks, and G-code to ensure machining precision.

  • Design and build custom fixtures, jigs, and tooling to support complex machining operations.

  • Work with a variety of materials including metals, plastics, and wood to produce high-quality parts and prototypes.

  • Collaborate with engineers and designers to troubleshoot manufacturing challenges and improve product development efficiency.

  • Maintain a clean, organized, and safe work environment while adhering to all safety policies and procedures.

Qualifications:

  • 5+ years of experience in a model shop or precision machining environment.

  • Hands-on experience setting up and operating CNC mills and lathes.

  • Strong proficiency in CAM programming and G-code editing.

  • Experience with Mastercam and SolidWorks (version 2018 or newer) required.

  • Ability to read blueprints, interpret GD&T, and perform quality checks using precision measuring instruments.

  • Completion of a formal apprenticeship or technical trade school program preferred.

  • Excellent attention to detail, craftsmanship, and problem-solving skills.

What’s Offered:

  • Competitive hourly pay ($38–$40/HR)

  • Comprehensive benefits package including healthcare, 401(k) match, and paid time off

  • Tuition reimbursement and professional development opportunities

  • Stable, long-term career growth within a collaborative and supportive environment

job.title: Senior Manufacturing Engineer
job.description:
The Senior Manufacturing Engineer is a key contributor to the design and development of manufacturing processes,
tools, and procedures. Provides direct engineering support for New Product Introduction (NPI), development of assembly & test processes, tools, and inspection methods. Identifies and leads and continuous improvement activities and projects. Responsible for manufacturing process verification & validation and employee training effectiveness.
Ensures the smooth running of manufacturing processes with the highest possible quality and efficiency.
Principal Responsibilities 
? Designs, develops and documents manufacturing systems, processes and tools for new products, product & process
changes, to meet targets for quality, efficiency, and reliability.
? Creates and maintains manufacturing plans, procedures, process FMEAs, flowcharts, workflows, layouts, routings, time
standards, and associated tools/equipment, fixtures, and material handling systems.
? Provides direct manufacturing engineering support for product characterization, and product builds at prototype,
verification, validation, and commercial manufacturing stages, to meet ISO 13485 requirements & FDA regulations.
? Interfaces with design engineers during new product development or product enhancement projects to develop DMR
documentation and assure design for manufacturability, assembly & test.
? Identifies/leads continuous improvement projects using Lean methodologies to improve manufacturing/business
systems, processes, and tools to improve quality, eliminate waste, and reduce cost; fosters a Lean culture.
? Maintains manufacturing master data in ERP systems; initiates/approves engineering change requests/orders.
? Drives and supports the implementation of effective manufacturing process controls, statistical measurements, and
metrics to assure products & processes meet quality and business objectives.
? Performs capacity and utilization studies, time and motion studies, labor/cycle time analysis, and line balancing. 
? Represents manufacturing in all internal quality processes including MRB & non-conformance handling, complaint
reviews, and internal and external quality audits; drives resolution of quality issues using CAPA systems. 
? Functions as the lead technical liaison with suppliers and contract manufacturers; provides technical guidance to
suppliers regarding compliance with engineering drawings and other technical requirements/specifications.
? Partners with quality engineering and suppliers to ensure corrective action or problem resolution to meet quality, cost
and delivery targets, and support supplier performance management.
? Leads large scale projects related to facility moves, re-layouts, Lean training, and Lean transformation projects. 
? Perform miscellaneous duties and projects as assigned and required.
Training, Skills, Knowledge and/or Experience 
? 8+ years of related manufacturing/industrial engineering work experience required, including 3+ years of experience with
medical devices in FDA QSR & ISO13485 regulated environment.
? Experience with manufacturing of complex electro-mechanical products required; some quality engineering experience
and exposure to opto-mechanical/electrical product manufacturing preferred.
? Proven track record of creating FMEAs, performing process verification and validation, developing & validating test
methods, and transferring new products into manufacturing.
? Lean and/or Six Sigma Black Belt certification highly preferred, with demonstrated success in rolling out Lean methods
and tools to improve quality and efficiency in manufacturing environments.
? Microsoft Office 365 Suite & ERP/PLM proficiency required, with working knowledge with SolidWorks, Microsoft Visio,
Microsoft Access, Microsoft Project, and electronic documentation systems.
? Demonstrated experience in data collection and statistical analysis, proficiency with Minitab or other statistical software
highly preferred.
? Demonstrated understanding of mechanical dimensioning and tolerancing, interpretation of component specifications,
Quality tools/methodologies, problem solving and root cause analysis, 5S and poka-yoke.
Education Requirements 
Bachelor’s degree in manufacturing, industrial engineering, or a related technical field; master’s degree preferred. Master’s
degree preferred.
Competencies
? In-depth understanding of lean manufacturing and supply-chain management methods and tools.
? Strong attention to detail, with excellent analytical, problem-solving, and project management skills.
? Strong data collection & analysis, problem solving, presentation and communication skills.
? Adept at summarizing and presenting complex information to non-technical personnel.
? Ability to learn and utilize new knowledge quickly and effectively, work independently, without appreciable direction.
Supervisory Responsibility
None.
Working Conditions 
This job operates in manufacturing and engineering laboratory environments including ISO class 7 cleanrooms. PPE required
when in controlled environment rooms or cleanrooms.

Other Requirements 
Applicants must have US citizenship or US person status.
